<title level="2" right="Creature 14">[Fate's Prophet](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2557)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Demon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=42" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=70" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #191: The Destiny War](/Sources.aspx?ID=199) pg. 9

**Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344)

**Languages**
[Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Gnomish](/Languages.aspx?ID=5), [Varisian](/Languages.aspx?ID=44); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet, [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)

**Skills**
[Abyss Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +25, [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +25, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +30, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +28, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +28, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +25, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +25, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +25

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +5

**Con** +4

**Int** +5

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +8
</row>

**Items**
_[The Demon's Lantern](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2504)_, _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [hand crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=69)_ (10 bolts^ all poisoned with [shadow essence](/Equipment.aspx?ID=127)), bejeweled belt worth 3, 000 gp

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 35 

**Fort** +24 

**Ref** +25 

**Will** +27 

+1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 255

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 15, good 15

**Rejection Vulnerability** As succubi are beings of pure lust, creatures that reject their lust can metaphysically harm them. When a succubus fails a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) check to Embrace or [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=51), or when a creature succeeds at its save against a succubus's [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) spell or ability, the succubus takes 4d6 mental damage. For one hour after causing mental damage to a succubus in this way, a creature can deal 4d6 mental damage to the succubus with a successful [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) incorporating its rejection.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, fly 35 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 3d8+10 slashing plus 1d6 evil

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_hand crossbow_ +28 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 2d6 piercing plus [shadow essence](/Equipment.aspx?ID=127)

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 34
- **1st**
[Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) (at will)
- **2nd**
[Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will)
- **3rd**
[Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194) (at will)
- **4th**
[Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) (at will)
- **5th**
[Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69)
- **6th**
[Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=87)
- **7th**
[Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=369)
- **Constant (7th)**
[Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344)

**Divine Rituals** DC 34

- **1st**
[Abyssal Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=21)

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The succubus can take on the appearance of any Small or Medium [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91). This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage modifiers with their Strikes, but it might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning).

**Grab Attention** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) Fate's Prophet attempts to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) a creature using [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) instead of [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3). If the creature is willing, she grapples it automatically.

**Passionate Kiss** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Frequency** Once per round. **Effect** The succubus engages a creature they have grabbed in an embrace or other act of passion to drain its vital essence. The kiss makes the creature [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) or increases its drained condition by 1, to a maximum of 4. The creature takes 5d6 negative damage and the succubus regains Hit Points equal to the damage dealt. The target must succeed at a DC 34 Will save or be affected by a [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) to submit to more actions of passion rather than trying to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79).

</column>
name: Fate's Prophet

**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 42 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 [Demongate Colossus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2558) **Source** [Pathfinder #191: The Destiny War](/Sources.aspx?ID=199) pg. 12 **Perception** +24; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12) **Skills** [Abyss Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +27, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +26, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +25 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** +5 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +25 **Will** +22 **HP** 240 **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) 15, physical 10 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=372)) **Abyssal Link** The Demongate Colossus is built around a seething, churning mass of [Abyssal](/Planes.aspx?ID=13) energy that looks like a sphere of electrified red mist encompassing a single floating harrow card at its center—[_The Survivor_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2514). The Abyssal link is obscured until the Demongate Colossus is reduced to fewer than 120 Hit Points, at which point glimpses of it become visible through tears and holes in its leathery “flesh.” The potent magic drawn from this card, combined with the radiant warden's gatewarper aura, is what allowed the Prince of Wolves to invade [Harrow Court](/Planes.aspx?ID=23). Once the Abyssal link is revealed, a character can attempt to counter the Abyssal link with a [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) (7th level; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) DC 34), or can attempt to [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=68) the card with a DC 35 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) (master) check (on a failure, a thief attempting to Steal the card takes 5d10 chaotic and 5d10 evil damage—this damage is doubled on a critical failure). Either success causes a link collapse (see below).
**Gatewarper Aura** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) 60 feet. A creature that uses a [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156) ability within the aura's emanation or enters the emanation via a teleportation ability must succeed at a DC 31 Fortitude save or become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (drained 2 on a critical failure) as its body warps and distorts painfully, and have its destination changed to a point of the radiant warden's choosing within the emanation.
**Link Collapse** If the Demongate Colossus is destroyed or its belly portal is deactivated, demons that used the colossus's portal within a 40-foot emanation take 7d10 good damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) DC 30 Fortitude save). A creature that takes full or double damage is [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) for 1 minute. If the Demongate Colossus is still active when it suffers a link collapse, it becomes [stunned 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) and loses its gatewarper aura. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** hammer +30 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)), **Damage** 3d8+14 bludgeoning plus corrupted radiant blow **Melee** corrupted radiant beam +27 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d6 force plus 3d6 evil **Occult Innate Spells** DC 34 - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **6th** [Collective Transposition](/Spells.aspx?ID=43) - **7th** [Dimensional Lock](/Spells.aspx?ID=71), [Prismatic Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=233) **Corrupted Radiant Blast** ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The Demongate Colossus releases a 50-foot cone of crimson energy that deals 4d12 force damage and 4d12 evil damage (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The Demongate Colossus can't use Corrupted Radiant Blast again for 1d4 rounds. **Corrupted Radiant Blow** ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64)) When a non-evil creature is hit by the Demongate Colossus's hammer Strike, a red flash of Abyssal energy attempts to infuse the target with evil. The creature must attempt a DC 34 Will save; on a failure, the creature can't use any [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156) effects for 1 minute. On a critical failure, the creature is also [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13).
name: Demongate Colossus

markdown:
[The Shadow Prince](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2559) **Source** [Pathfinder #191: The Destiny War](/Sources.aspx?ID=199) pg. 13 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet, universal language **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +24, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +21, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +20 **Str** +4 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +7 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 100 feet, DC 28.
**Universal Language** Anything spoken by the Shadow Prince is perceived by the listener in its native language.
**Urban Legend** A brainchild is sustained only by the reputation that precedes them. Mindless creatures are immune to a brainchild and can't perceive them. The brainchild's size, features, and items, as well as the appearance of their attacks, match what the foes perceiving them expect. If foes expect to see different things, the brainchild chooses which to manifest. If any creature that can perceive the brainchild believes the brainchild has one of the abilities below, the brainchild has that ability. A creature can [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) or [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85) (against the brainchild's [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) DC) to attempt to disbelieve an individual ability. If at any point no creature perceiving the brainchild believes in the ability, the brainchild loses that ability immediately. If foes expect different particulars, such as one believing the brainchild is immune to fire and another believing they're immune to divinations, the brainchild chooses one to have.
  • [Tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) (imprecise) 100 feet
  • Immunity to one damage type, magic school, or condition
  • Weakness 10 to one damage type other than mental
  • Resistance 10 to physical damage, with an exception for either [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=272) or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=277)
  • [Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17) ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 100 feet, DC 28
  • 1d6 Extra Damage on Strikes, of a type one foe believes in
  • Additional Spells [_phantom pain_](/Spells.aspx?ID=220) and [_shadow blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=273) at 6th level

The Shadow Prince's illusory abilities granted by its urban legend include its immunity to emotion effects, its resistance to physical damage, its frightful presence, and the [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) caused by its melee Strikes. --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +22 **Will** +18 **HP** 200 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=43), [diseased](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=13), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effects, [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [_scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=268) **Weaknesses** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) 10 --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** illusory weapon +24 ([Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)), **Damage** 4d6+10 mental plus 1d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 30 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (×2) - **5th** [Phantasmal Killer](/Spells.aspx?ID=219) (×3)
name: The Shadow Prince

**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Crystal Sentinel](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2560) **Source** [Pathfinder #191: The Destiny War](/Sources.aspx?ID=199) pg. 23 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +23, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +23 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +22 **Will** +18 **HP** 235 **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), magic (see Golem Antimagic below), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 15 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=372) or bludgeoning) **[Golem Antimagic](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=57)** harmed by sonic (6d10, 2d8 from areas or [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)); healed by fire (area 2d10 HP); [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) by cold
**Vulnerable to _Shatter_** A crystal sentinel is affected by the [_shatter_](/Spells.aspx?ID=279) spell as though it were an unattended object.
**Spell Reflection** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11)) **Trigger** The crystal golem is targeted by a spell **Effect** The glass golem positions its magical, reflective surfaces to turn the spell back on the caster. It tries to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the spell by attempting an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) check for its counteract check. If it successfully counteracts the spell, the effect is turned back on the caster. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** bladed limb +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 3d6+14 slashing plus 1d6 bleed **Dazzling Brilliance** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The crystal sentinel creates waves of scintillating luminosity that cast [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 120-foot emanation (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for the next 120 feet). The light lasts until the start of the crystal sentinel's next turn, after which the crystal sentinel can't use Dazzling Brilliance for 1d4 rounds. A creature caught within the bright light or that enters the bright light must attempt a DC 33 Will save.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round, and then dazzled for an additional 4 rounds. While the creature remains dazzled, it's also [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) (this additional effect has the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) traits).
name: Crystal Sentinel

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Quoppopak Mummy](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2562) **Source** [Pathfinder #191: The Destiny War](/Sources.aspx?ID=199) pg. 34 **Perception** +24; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27 **Str** +8 **Dex** +2 **Con** +6 **Int** -2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +21 **Will** +24 **HP** 260 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 15 **Despair** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 30 feet. Living creatures are [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) while in a mummy's despair aura. They can't naturally recover from this fear while in the area but recover instantly once they leave the area. When a creature first enters the area, it must succeed at a DC 30 Will save (after taking the penalty from being frightened) or be [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 1 round. The creature is then temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** Tentacle only. --- **Speed** 10 feet, swim 35 feet, water glide 25 feet **Melee** beak +27 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+14 piercing plus oil rot **Melee** tentacle +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+14 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) and oil rot **Melee** ventral tube +27 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d6+14 slashing plus 1d6 persistent bleed and oil rot **Ignite Oil** **Trigger** The quoppopak mummy takes fire damage while it is above water; **Effect** The oil coating the mummy ignites. This fire does no additional damage to the mummy but does cause its melee Strikes to inflict an additional 1d6 fire damage. At the end of its turn, the mummy attempts a DC 10 flat check; on a success, the fire goes out. The fire also goes out immediately if the mummy submerges in water. **Oil Rot** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) This disease and any damage from it can't be healed until this curse is removed. As the disease progresses, the creature's flesh grows leathery, while a foul-smelling oily layer spreads from painful splits in the dried skin. A creature killed by oil rot melts into a puddle of foul-smelling oil and can be resurrected only by a 7th-level [_resurrect_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=20) ritual or similar magic; **Saving Throw** DC 33 Fortitude; **Stage 1** carrier with no ill effect (1 minute); **Stage 2** 10d6 negative damage and [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) (1 day) **Tentacle Stab** Frequency once per round; Effect The quoppopak makes two tentacle Strikes against the same creature. These Strikes deal piercing damage instead of bludgeoning, and the quoppopak can't Grab with them. Its multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after both attacks. **Water Glide** The quoppopak can stand and move on the surface of water or other liquids without falling through. It can go underwater if it wishes, but it must [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) to do so.
name: Quoppopak Mummy

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 44 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 [Markish Aghayarea](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2563) **Source** [Pathfinder #191: The Destiny War](/Sources.aspx?ID=199) pg. 37 **Perception** +29; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_detect alignment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=65), [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [wavesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=41) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=21) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +29, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +28, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +28, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +30, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +29, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +29 **Str** +7 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +6 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 **Items** _[The Beating](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2502)_, jewelry worth 2, 000 gp, _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=303) [thundering](/Equipment.aspx?ID=306) [trident](/Weapons.aspx?ID=45)_ --- **AC** 37 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +25 **Will** +27 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) **HP** 250 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), spell deflection, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 15 **Spell Deflection** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) A spellcaster who targets a devourer with a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) spell, [_banishment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=19), [_bind soul_](/Spells.aspx?ID=21), [_divine decree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=82), [_divine wrath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=86), [_possession_](/Spells.aspx?ID=225), [_spirit blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=301), or [_spirit song_](/Spells.aspx?ID=303) can attempt a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check to free a soul the devourer has trapped with Devour Soul. If this counteract attempt succeeds, the trapped soul is released (though the creature remains dead), and the devourer can't use any soul charges from that creature. Devourers are otherwise immune to these spells.
Vortex (aura, water) 40 feet. Water in the aura that is also in the same body of water as the marid is difficult terrain for [Swimming](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) creatures. Creatures with the [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) trait are immune. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 30 feet, swim 40 feet **Melee** _trident_ +30 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 3d8+15 piercing plus 1d6 electricity and 1d6 sonic **Melee** claw +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+15 slashing plus drain life **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 36, attack +29 - **6th** [Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=28), [Hydraulic Push](/Spells.aspx?ID=154) (at will), [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=160), [Solid Fog](/Spells.aspx?ID=290) - **7th** [Cone of Cold](/Spells.aspx?ID=47) (at will), [Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=51) (at will), [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) (at will; to [Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=8); Elemental Planes; or [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) only) - **8th** [Hallucinatory Terrain](/Spells.aspx?ID=145), [Hydraulic Torrent](/Spells.aspx?ID=155), [Illusory Creature](/Spells.aspx?ID=158) (×2) - **Constant (8th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (evil or good only) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 36, see Soul Spells below - **1st** [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) - **2nd** [Death Knell](/Spells.aspx?ID=63) - **3rd** [Bind Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=22), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=213) - **4th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) - **6th** [Feeblemind](/Spells.aspx?ID=112), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) - **7th** [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=369) - **8th** [Maze](/Spells.aspx?ID=187) **Rituals** - **2nd** [Create Undead](/Rituals.aspx?ID=10) **Devour Soul** ([Death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Markish touches a creature within reach, dealing 12d6 negative damage (DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). If a creature is slain by this ability, the creature's soul becomes trapped within Markish. While its soul is trapped, a creature can't be resurrected except by powerful magic such as a [_wish_](/Spells.aspx?ID=377) spell. Destroying Markish or successfully counteracting Devour Soul (see Spell Deflection) releases the soul. Markish can hold only one soul at a time. A soul has 5 soul charges per level of the originating creature (see Soul Spells below). The devourer can expend these charges to cast spells. If the soul is freed and the creature returns to life, the creature is [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) for every 5 soul charges expended. If reduced to 0 soul charges, the soul is consumed and can be restored to life only by _wish_ or similar magic. **Drain Life** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When Markish damages a living creature with his claw Strike, he gains 15 temporary Hit Points and the creature must succeed at a DC 36 Fortitude save or become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). Further damage Markish deals increases the condition value by 1 on a failed save, to a maximum of drained 4. **Grant Wish** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47)) **Frequency** 3 times per year; **Effect** Markish casts [_wish_](/Spells.aspx?ID=377) as an arcane spell to grant a wish to a mortal or [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creature (excluding himself), but he does his best to warp the effects of the wish to cause suffering or interprets the wish's wording in a way to torment the one who asks for it. **Rush of Shadows** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) Markish releases a jet of shadowy water in a 60-foot line, dealing 9d6 bludgeoning damage and 6d6 negative damage (DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). A creature who critically fails its save is also [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet (20 feet on a critical failure) and is [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) (enfeebled 2 on a critical failure). Markish can't use Rush of Shadows for 1d4 rounds. **Soul Spells** To cast occult innate spells, Markish must expend a number of soul charges equal to the spell's level. He can heighten any occult spell to a maximum of 8th level by expending more charges as he Casts the Spell. Markish starts this combat with a trapped soul that has 16 soul charges.
name: Markish Aghayarea

**4th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48) **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 33, attack +25 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Bullhorn](/Spells.aspx?ID=877), [Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) - **1st** [Bane](/Spells.aspx?ID=18), [Protection](/Spells.aspx?ID=238), [Sanctuary](/Spells.aspx?ID=266) - **2nd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59), [Death Knell](/Spells.aspx?ID=63), [See Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) - **3rd** [Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=26), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Vampiric Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=354) - **4th** [Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6), [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86), [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=211) - **5th** [Drop Dead](/Spells.aspx?ID=92), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Shadow Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=273) - **6th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Repulsion](/Spells.aspx?ID=254) - **7th** [Divine Vessel](/Spells.aspx?ID=85), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) (×4), [Regenerate](/Spells.aspx?ID=248) **Cleric Domain Spells** DC 33, 2 Focus Points - **7th** [Shared Nightmare](/Spells.aspx?ID=451), [Waking Nightmare](/Spells.aspx?ID=468) **Instinctual Tinker** The morlock tinkers with an adjacent [construct](/Traits.aspx?ID=35) or [mechanical](/Traits.aspx?ID=105) hazard. They attempt a [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) check against the construct's or hazard's Fortitude DC. The morlock can't succeed if the target's level is more than double the morlock's.
**Critical Success** The target gains 4d6 HP and a +1 circumstance bonus to attack rolls for 1 minute.
**Success** The target gains 2d6 HP.
**Critical Failure** The morlock injures itself, taking 2d6 damage (typically bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing, but potentially a different type at the GM's discretion). **Frenetic Casting** Xiuli Strides up to her speed. At any point during her movement, Xiuli can Cast a Spell that normally takes two actions or fewer to cast. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** Xiuli deals an additional 3d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures. **Swarming Stance** A morlock can share the same space as another morlock, but no more than two morlocks can occupy the same space. When morlocks share the same space, they gain a +1 circumstance bonus to attack rolls.

Scintillating Aura (arcane, aura, evocation, incapacitation,
**visual)** 30 feet. A swirling vortex of reflected color and light shimmers around the crystal dragon. Creatures in this aura's emanation are [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7). Each creature that ends its turn in the emanation must succeed at a DC 37 Will saving throw or be [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) (or stunned 3 on a critical failure). Once a creature succeeds at this save, it is temporarily immune to the stunning effect for 1 minute. The crystal dragon can turn this aura on or off using a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, and it can choose not to affect allies.
**Reflect Spell **Trigger** Vehanezhad is targeted by a ranged spell attack roll** **Effect** Vehanezhad adjusts a wing and gains a +4 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. If the attack misses, the spell reflects at the caster, who must roll a second ranged spell attack roll against their own AC to determine if the spell hits them instead.
**Twisting Tail **Trigger** A creature within reach of the dragon's tail uses a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) action or leaves a square during a move action it's using** **Effect** The dragon makes a tail Strike at the creature with a –2 penalty. If it hits, the dragon [disrupts](/Rules.aspx?ID=394) the creature's action. --- **Speed** 60 feet, burrow 40 feet, fly 140 feet **Melee** jaws +35 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+17 slashing plus 4d6 piercing plus crystal corruption **Melee** claw +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+17 slashing plus crystal corruption **Melee** tail +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d12+17 bludgeoning plus crystal corruption **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 43 - **Cantrips (8th)** [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132) - **1st** [Create Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=53) - **7th** [Project Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=237) - **8th** [Hallucinatory Terrain](/Spells.aspx?ID=145) (at will), [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=356) (at will) - **9th** [Illusory Creature](/Spells.aspx?ID=158) (at will), [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=160) (at will) **Breath Weapon** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) Vehanezhad breathes a flurry of piercing crystals that deals 19d6 piercing damage in a 60-foot cone (DC 40 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). A creature who takes damage from this breath weapon is exposed to crystal corruption. Vehanezhad can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds. **Crystal Corruption** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) Creatures afflicted by this curse slowly turn to solid crystal. This affliction's [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35), and [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) conditions can't be removed until the affliction itself is removed. Creatures with both the [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55) and [elemental](/Traits.aspx?ID=57) traits instead become carriers and suffer no ill effects aside from gaining the weakness to [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147). **Saving Throw** DC 40 Fortitude; **Stage 1** sickened 1 (1 hour); **Stage 2** weakness 5 to sonic and sickened 1 (1 day); **Stage 3** weakness 5 to sonic and slowed 1 (1 day); **Stage 4** weakness 10 to sonic and paralyzed (1 day); **Stage 5** weakness 10 to sonic, and the creature is permanently [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30). **Crystallize Flesh** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=50)) **Trigger** The crystal dragon damages a creature made of flesh with a jaws Strike; **Frequency** three times per day; **Effect** The dragon embeds transformative crystals in the creature's flesh. The creature must attempt a DC 40 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round as portions of its flesh turn crystalline.
**Failure** The target is slowed 1 and must attempt a Fortitude save at the end of each of its turns; this ongoing save has the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93) trait. On a failed save, the slowed condition value increases by 1 (or by 2 on a critical failure). A successful save reduces the slowed condition value by 1. A creature unable to act due to the slowed condition from Crystallize Flesh is [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30) permanently, transforming into a crystalline statue. The effect ends if the creature is petrified or the slowed condition is removed.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target is initially slowed 2. **Draconic Frenzy** Vehanezhad makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** Vehanezhad recharges her Breath Weapon whenever she critically hits with a Strike.
name: Vehanezhad

When a creature ends its turn in the aura, the constant clicking and ticking sounds coming from Ticktock worm into its thoughts and fill the listener with the sense that time is speeding up for everyone but itself. The creature must succeed at a DC 34 Will save or become [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) (or slowed 2 on a critical failure) until the end of its next turn.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 25 feet, climb 25 feet **Melee** tool fingers +32 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 3d4+14 piercing plus 1d6 evil and 2d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 37 - **3rd** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110) - **8th** [Chilling Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=36) **Shadow Scream** ([Aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) The sacristan opens their mouth to unloose a series of thunderous clicking sounds. This creates a 30-foot emanation of darkness and wailing sounds around the sacristan. Creatures with [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) can't see through this darkness. The sacristan can Sustain Shadow Scream for up to 1 minute. Non-[velstrac](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=111) creatures in the area when the ability is used, as well as those who enter or start their turn in the area, must attempt a DC 37 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is then temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) and deafened for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 20 mental damage, and is confused and deafened for 1 round.
name: Ticktock

Any PC who's legendary in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) and sees this can attempt a DC 36 check using one of those skills; on a success, they realize the crystal is manipulating Mogaran's actions in some way. If the crystal is destroyed or deactivated, Mogaran regains control of his actions (with repercussions as detailed on page 68). The crystal functions as a 19th-level magic item for the purposes of [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), but it can also be destroyed by specifically targeting it (AC 40; Hardness 20; HP 90). Alternately, a character can pry the crystal from Mogaran's brow with a successful DC 36 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check, which takes an Interact action, but only if Mogaran is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24).

markdown:
In her previous life, All-Seeing Hajeck was a small-time charlatan, but soon after she joined a traveling show called the Umbra Carnival she inherited a powerful magical artifact: the [_Deck of Harrowed Tales_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2497). Much like the _Deck of Destiny_, the _Deck of Harrowed Tales_ links to a demiplane—one known as the Harrowed Realm. Soon after she inherited the _Deck of Harrowed Tales_, Hajeck fell under the influence of a powerful dragon named Zassrion who had been trapped within the Harrowed Realm for untold ages. The dragon used Hajeck as a pawn to lure victims from the real world into the Harrowed Realm, where he attempted to use their bodies as a catalyst to empower his escape. When the dragon was ultimately defeated, Hajeck escaped his magical control. Ashamed and humiliated by her time spent under the dragon's control, Hajeck left the Umbra Carnival and relocated, in time, to the city of Absalom, where she hoped to bury her past and start a new life, only to become embroiled with an even more powerful harrow-themed artifact: the _Deck of Destiny_. And this time, she wasn't so lucky—this time, she paid with her life (see the start of the previous adventure, “The Choosing,” for more details). Campaign Use All-Seeing Hajeck is reincarnated at the start of this adventure by the _Deck of Destiny_. While she comes back as a more powerful entity—a lamia matriarch— she realizes her new incarnation is as much an insult as anything else, for she has become a living embodiment of the harrow card known as [_The Liar_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2481). She first appears in the Harrowed Realm and eventually learns she cannot leave this demiplane. Her interactions with the PCs can either redeem her for her life of lies or see her embrace deception and, potentially, become a conspirator against the PCs, as detailed in the adventure itself. As long as she remains their ally, however, All-Seeing Hajeck can draw upon the power of the Harrowed Realm to bolster their destinies by performing Harrowing Visions for them. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [All-Seeing Hajeck](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2573) **Source** [Pathfinder #191: The Destiny War](/Sources.aspx?ID=199) pg. 88 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Varisian](/Languages.aspx?ID=44) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +21, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +25, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +23, [Fortune-Telling Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +25, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +23, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +23, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +21, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +21 **Str** +4 **Dex** +5 **Con** +5 **Int** +5 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +7 **Items** [Harrow Deck](/Equipment.aspx?ID=824) --- **AC** 31 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +23 **Will** +22 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) **HP** 215 **Immunities** [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6) **Resistances** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) 10 **Harrowbound** Hajeck can only exist in the Harrow Court. If she leaves the plane, either willingly or by succumbing to an effect that forces her into a different plane, she instead immediately reappears somewhere in Harrowheart and becomes [drained 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 30 feet, swim 30 feet **Melee** claw +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 3d8+10 slashing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 32 - **1st** [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=356) (at will) - **2nd** [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=159) (at will), [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=160) (at will), [Mirror Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=197) - **3rd** [Glibness](/Spells.aspx?ID=135), [Sleep](/Spells.aspx?ID=288) - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) (×3), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) (×3) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) All-Seeing Hajeck can take on the appearance of a Varisian woman—her appearance from her previous life. This doesn't change her Speed or her attack and damage modifiers, but does prevent her from using Hajeck's Caress. **Hajeck's Caress** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Hajeck touches a creature, who must succeed at a DC 32 Will save or become [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (stupefied 2 on a critical failure). If the target fails additional saves against this ability, the condition value increases by 2 (to a maximum of stupefied 4). This condition value decreases by 1 every 24 hours. **Harrowing Vision** ([Divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [Fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Hajeck can use any harrow deck to perform a potent divination that taps into the surrounding magic provided by the Deck of Destiny's otherworldly creation. A Harrowing Vision takes an hour to perform, but most of that time consists of Hajeck and her chosen subject meditating and clearing their minds in a secluded area, culminating in an intense harrow reading that itself takes only a few minutes to complete. The target of a Harrowing Vision must remain adjacent to Hajeck for the entire hour, after which Hajeck shuffles her harrow deck and lays out seven cards, face down—one in the center (the future), two to either side (the present), and four more at the top, bottom, left, and right surrounding the central three (the past).
When the harrowing takes place, the target flips over the four cards from the past, followed by the two present cards, and then finally the central card. This method of harrowing symbolizes how the choices a person made in their past often present them with an either/or decision in the now, and that their decision on that subject will lock in a future result. When All-Seeing Hajeck performs this reading for a PC, try to interpret the four cards from the past as representing events from that character's history—be it their background or references to events that took place during the Stolen Fate Adventure Path. For the two cards representing the present, try to interpret them as symbols of questions, events, or mysteries the PC is currently facing.
Only the card representing the future has an actual game effect. When this central, final card is revealed, all of the PCs' potential futures suddenly flash in their mind in an overwhelming, chaotic vision. The PC must attempt a DC 32 Will save (note that as a PC grows more powerful and their Will save increases, they're more likely to achieve a better result from a Harrowing Vision, since they're that much closer to their ultimate destiny and are bolstered by the weight of their previous choices and decisions). If the suit of the card revealed for the PCs' future matches the PC's key ability from their class, they gain a +1 status bonus on this Will save. If the alignment of the card revealed matches the PC's alignment, they gain a +2 status bonus to this will save. If both match, these bonuses stack to a +3 status bonus, and the actual result of the save is improved by one degree of success. If neither the suit or alignment match the PC, they reduce the actual result of the save by one degree of success. Regardless of the result, that character cannot receive another Harrowing Vision until they gain a level, at which point any lingering effects from the Harrowing Vision end.
**Critical Success** The character experiences a vision of a specific future success, only to forget it an instant later. Once only as long as this effect persists when the character is affected by a critical failure for any roll they make or a critical success with any roll a foe makes, the character can change that roll into a Success or a Failure, as they see fit. In addition, the character gains the Success benefit detailed below.
**Success** The character experiences several overlapping potential successes in their future. As long as this effect persists (typically until the character gains a level), the maximum size of their hand for investing cards from the _Deck of Destiny_ increases by 1 to six cards.
**Failure** The vision is confusing and frightening, but has no lasting effect.
**Critical Failure** Not only is the vision confusing and frightening, but the character becomes obsessed with worst-case scenarios, becomes [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), and becomes cursed—they can no longer gain the benefit of their best possible futures. As long as this curse persists, the character treats all critical successes as regular successes. This is a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) effect.
name: All-Seeing Hajeck

<title level="1" pfs="">[The Prince Of Wolves, Abyssal Crime Lord](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2574)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The Prince of Wolves was once no more than a midlevel beastkin crime lord named Khaaroz, a conniving conspirator who'd spent nearly two decades building up a criminal network in the Abyssal city of Alushinyrra. His plans crumbled to ashes when the ruler of the city, [Nocticula](/Deities.aspx?ID=45), left the Midnight Isles to ascend from demon lord to goddess. Khaaroz fled Alushinyrra as it fell into chaos, hijacking a warship from the city harbor. After securing command over the crew by killing their previous captain, Khaaroz made his way to a relatively tiny island on the outskirts of the Midnight Isles, where he led his crew on a raid that resulted in the capture of the isle's previous ruler—a [vavakia](/Monsters.aspx?ID=976) tyrant named Mogaran.

 In the treasury of the fallen ruler's keep, Khaaroz discovered a collection of harrow cards, including ancient Varisian versions of several lost cards. He became fascinated and obsessed, particularly with a rare card called the Prince of Wolves—a card he felt symbolized him to perfection. Khaaroz learned more about the history of the cards, and he sent agents beyond the [Abyss](/Planes.aspx?ID=13) into Golarion to acquire them if possible. To many of his minions, Khaaroz was known only as the Prince of Wolves, a name he took from that intriguing harrow card he found among Mogaran's treasures.

 From his fixation on the harrow, Khaaroz became convinced that a great transformation awaited him in the future. At first, he suspected this transformation was an ascension from his mortal form into that of a full-fledged demon. After ensuring his new island home, now called “The Den,” was secure, Khaaroz left the Abyss to travel to Golarion in search of this great change.

 Not long after, he was contacted by a man named Drustan. Khaaroz thought Drustan something of a fool but was intrigued by his offer to join a group who sought to use the harrow to remake reality. Soon enough, as the Prince of Wolves, Khaaroz became one of the three leaders of the Unmatched. In the Varisian harrower Raven, Khaaroz found all the wisdom and intellect he sought, and while outwardly he appeared to work with the others, his true goal was always to seize the _Deck of Destiny_ for himself and to use it to unlock his true potential. When the Scattering spread the _Deck of Destiny_ throughout the world, Khaaroz only just managed to resist attacking the other Unmatched in a fit of frustration. Once again, he faced what he felt was an unfair disruption to his plans, similar to what had occurred years before with Nocticula's abandoning of Alushinyrra. Yet, he hid his anger and set off on his own to gather up as many of the cards as he could, reasoning that he'd be able to take those gathered by Raven and Drustan when the time came. 

<title level="2" right="">Campaign Role</title> The Prince of Wolves has already gathered some cards from the _Deck of Destiny_ when this adventure begins. While he loans three of these to his agents in the first part of the adventure in order to aid their invasion of the Harrow Court, he retains three others (in addition to, potentially, any that may have slipped through the PCs' fingers over the course of the Adventure Path). He is this adventure's primary antagonist and unlikely to survive the events of “The Destiny War.” If he does, he can continue to vex the PCs into the next adventure, potentially joining up with Raven to help her finish her plans.

While in hybrid shape, he gains a jaws Strike. While in animal shape, he gains the effects of a 5th-level casting of [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=10) to become a canine. He prefers fighting in hybrid shape.

**Hunt Prey** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) The Prince designates one or two targets he can see or hear as his prey. He gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) and [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) his prey. If he uses Hunt Prey against a third creature when he already has two designated, a prior creature of his choice loses the designation. His designation lasts until his next daily preparations.

**Pack Tactics**   If an enemy is within reach of the Prince and at least two of his allies, that enemy is flat footed against him.

**Skirmish Strike** <actions string="Single Action" />  The Prince either Steps and then Strikes, or Strikes and then Steps.

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   The Prince deals an extra 4d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.

**Tear Fate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Requirement** The Prince of Wolves' previous action was a successful melee Strike; **Effect** The Prince of Wolves twists his weapon in the wounds inflicted on the creature he just struck, then rips the weapon free. As he does so, he tears free fragments of that creature's fate, leaving them with ill fortune. The creature takes 12d6 mental damage (DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save); on a failure, the creature also becomes [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) (doomed 2 on a critical failure). The creature is thereafter immune to Tear Fate.

**Twin Parry** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirement** The Prince is wielding two melee weapons, each in a different hand; **Effect** The Prince gains a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of his next turn, as long as he continues to meet this ability's requirement.

**Twin Takedown** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Frequency** once per round; **Requirement** The Prince is wielding two melee weapons, each in a different hand; **Effect** The Prince makes two Strikes against his hunted prey, one with each of the required weapons. If both hit the same hunted prey, combine their damage for the purpose of its resistances and weaknesses. Apply his multiple attack penalty to each Strike normally

An elite representative of the Pactmasters of Katapesh, Usilket is a child of Pactmaster Krimiltuk. Usilket expressed a genderfluid identity long before their training as an agent of the Katapeshi [witchwyrd](/Monsters.aspx?ID=861) consortium began. They took their current name, with its feminine and masculine connotations, at the onset of their education. Their elastic identity proved to be an asset for developing expertise in empathy, impersonation, and understanding, especially in social circles outside Katapesh's witchwyrd culture. Instruction honed these talents, supplementing them with impressive unarmed skills. Usilket has become an ideal diplomat, speaker, spy, trader and, when needed, warrior. In Katapeshi society, Usilket holds great authority. Their filial position is the equivalent of a high-ranking aristocrat, but following tradition, Usilket sought opportunities to distinguish themself and improve their position. Usilket serves the government of Katapesh. However, they've also set up successful trade relationships involving exploration, diplomacy, and acquiring goods from unusual sources. Establishing relations with Uomotos and a foothold in Kho was, as fate has it, Usilket's idea. The Pactmasters agreed and gave Usilket dispensation to visit the Uomoto people and Kho to establish relations and assess threats and potential value. Soon thereafter, the Scattering occurred, and while a few cards ended up in the possession of the Pactmasters (including [_The Owl_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2512), which appeared amid Usilket's belongings), even more manifested in Kho. The Pactmasters were immediately interested in finding out more about the cards, but when their research drew the attention of [the Prince of Wolves](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2574), they decided to pause until this new threat could be assessed and dealt with. Campaign Role Usilket works as a facilitator. Although they might seem generous, all Usilket does is to fulfill the Pactmasters' goals of thwarting the Unmatched with as little risk to their consortium as possible. While Usilket isn't quite as powerful as the PCs, (particularly toward the end of this adventure), they can be used as an agent to facilitate a desperate rescue—particularly during the largest portion of this adventure, which takes place in the ruins of Kho. Take care not to fall back on this role too often, though—you don't want the PCs to overly rely on the witchwyrd and grow careless. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Usilket](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2575) **Source** [Pathfinder #191: The Destiny War](/Sources.aspx?ID=199) pg. 92 **Perception** +24; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Gnoll](/Languages.aspx?ID=17), [Kelish](/Languages.aspx?ID=38), [Mwangi](/Languages.aspx?ID=39), [Osiriani](/Languages.aspx?ID=40), [Vudrani](/Languages.aspx?ID=45); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +24, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +25, [Katapesh Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +26, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +24, [Mercantile Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +24, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +23, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +26 **Str** +3 **Dex** +5 **Con** +1 **Int** +3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [handwraps of mighty blows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=441)_, [_guardian aluum charm_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2498), _[greater hat of disguise](/Equipment.aspx?ID=442)_ --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +24 **Will** +24 **HP** 238 **Resistances** [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75) 10 **Absorb Force** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75)) **Frequency** once per round; **Trigger** A [_magic missile_](/Spells.aspx?ID=180) is fired at Usilket, and they are aware of it and have a free hand; **Effect** Usilket “catches” the missile, absorbing it and causing that hand to glow while it holds this energy. A hand that's holding energy can only be used for Force Bolt. The energy lasts for 6 rounds or until it is released.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)), **Damage** 3d10+9 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 33 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) - **1st** [Floating Disk](/Spells.aspx?ID=124) (at will), [Unseen Servant](/Spells.aspx?ID=352) (at will) - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Mirror Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=197) - **6th** [Resilient Sphere](/Spells.aspx?ID=255), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) - **7th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=256) (×2) - **Constant (7th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Monk Focus Spells** DC 32, attack +25, (3 Focus Points) - **7th** [Abundant Step](/Spells.aspx?ID=482), [Ki Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=484), [Ki Rush](/Spells.aspx?ID=485), [Ki Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=486), [Wholeness of Body](/Spells.aspx?ID=488), [Wind Jump](/Spells.aspx?ID=490) **Flurry of Blows** ([Flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=73)) Usilket makes two Strikes with their fist or with a monk weapon. If both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses. **Force Bolt** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75)) Usilket fires two magic missiles per action spent, dealing 1d4+1 force damage each. They can't spend more actions on this ability than they have free hands. If they use a hand that has Absorbed Force, that hand hurls three missiles instead of two, expending the held energy. **Meditative Therapy** ([Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** One of Usilket's most unusual abilities is a technique they learned during a long visit to Jalmeray. Usilket enters a trancelike state for 10 minutes, during which they can attempt to remove a disease or one of the following conditions: [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37). To remove the disease or condition, Usilket must [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) or condition with a [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) check. Usilket can attempt to counteract a disease or condition present in another creature, as long as that creature remains adjacent to the witchwyrd during the entire meditation. **Powerful Fists** Usilket's fist Strikes are treated as [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) and [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379) and don't take penalties when making lethal attacks.
name: Usilket

Aluum are metal and stone constructs created by the Pactmasters to aid in maintaining order in Katapesh. Each aluum can be controlled by a magical aluum charm, including the more powerful aluums that serve the favored agents of the Pactmasters as guardians. One such aluum attends Usilket at all times, unless he passes his [_guardian aluum charm_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2498) to a PC to allow them the protection the construct affords. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 33 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 [Guardian Aluum](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2576) **Source** [Pathfinder #191: The Destiny War](/Sources.aspx?ID=199) pg. 93 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +26 **Str** +7 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** -5 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -5 --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +24 **Will** +21 **HP** 210 **Immunities** bleed, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), magic (see below), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** 10 physical (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=372)) **Aluum Antimagic** Guardian aluums are immune to spells and magical abilities, with two exceptions. A [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118) spell or magical ability grants an aluum the quickened condition until the end of its next turn instead of its normal effects. A [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) spell or ability makes an aluum [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) until the end of its next turn instead of its normal effects.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +26 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d12+13 bludgeoning plus paralyzing force **Paralyzing Force** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) A creature damaged by the aluum's fist Strike must succeed at a DC 33 Fortitude save or become [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 1 round. On a critical failure, the creature is paralyzed for 1d4 minutes and falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). **Soul Shriek** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) The aluum emits a keening wail in a 15-foot cone that deals 12d6 sonic damage (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). A creature that fails its save is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36), or stunned 3 on a critical failure. The aluum can't use Soul Shriek again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Guardian Aluum

Obcisidaemons (known to some as obliteration daemons) care only for the brutality and violence that conflict brings. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Obcisidaemon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2577) **Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 80 **Perception** +35; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +33, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +39, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +34, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +36, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +32, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +36 **Str** +10 **Dex** +4 **Con** +8 **Int** +4 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +7 **Cloak of Souls** An obcisidaemon is shrouded at all times in a cloak of captured souls. It can hold a number of souls equal to the daemon's Charisma modifier. Destroying the daemon frees the souls, though this doesn't return the deceased creatures to life. A creature whose soul is trapped within this cloak can't be resurrected except by a 9th-level [_resurrect_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=20) ritual or similarly powerful magic. **Items** _+3 [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [halberd](/Weapons.aspx?ID=26)_ --- **AC** 43 **Fort** +35 **Ref** +29 **Will** +32 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) **HP** 425 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects **Weaknesses** good 20 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Scorched Earth** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) 60 feet. Any creature who dies within the aura and isn't drawn into the obcisidaemon's Cloak of Souls via Inherit Soul must attempt a 38 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature's body (but not its gear) is immediately reduced to a fine smear of ashes. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 50 feet **Melee** _halberd_ +36 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 4d10+18 slashing plus 1d6 evil **Melee** jaws +36 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d6+18 piercing plus 1d6 evil and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 38, +30 - **1st** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will; good only) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) - **6th** [Cloudkill](/Spells.aspx?ID=42) - **7th** [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=213), [Spell Turning](/Spells.aspx?ID=297) - **9th** [Disintegrate](/Spells.aspx?ID=76), [Meteor Swarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=191) - **10th** [Massacre](/Spells.aspx?ID=186) - **Constant (10th)** [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Consume Soul** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The obcisidaemon consumes a soul from its cloak to gain one of the following effects. A consumed soul in this way can't be resurrected except by [_wish_](/Spells.aspx?ID=377) or a similarly powerful effect.
  • _Empower Spell_ The obcisidaemon gains a +2 status bonus to its spell DCs and spell attack rolls until the end of its next turn.
  • _Empower Weapon_ The obcisidaemon's weapon gains the effects of a [greater flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295), [greater frost](/Equipment.aspx?ID=296), [greater shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=303), or [wounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=309) rune until the end of its next turn.
  • _Healing_ The daemon gains the benefit of a two-action, 8th-level [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) spell.
**Inherit Soul** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Trigger** The obcisidaemon slays a creature; **Effect** The obcisidaemon attempts to draw the creature's soul into its cloak of souls. The triggering creature must attempt a DC 38 Fortitude save. On a failure, its soul is consumed and added to the cloak of souls. If the obcisidaemon's cloak can't hold any more souls, the daemon can release one of the souls as a free action; otherwise, the soul isn't absorbed.
Soul Hoarding Obcisidaemons carry some souls for months or even years at a time, choosing never to consume them, even when doing so might grant an advantage in combat.
name: Obcisidaemon

Known also as terror daemons, the horrifying phasmadaemons make use of illusions and their own frightening appearance (that of a serpent with a bony tail, a horned crocodilian skull for a head, and several insectile pincers) to strike fear into the hearts of others. They represent deaths brought about by fright. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 38 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 [Phasmadaemon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2578) **Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 81 **Perception** +29; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +31, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +31, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +33, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +29 **Str** +8 **Dex** +8 **Con** +6 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +31 **Ref** +26 **Will** +31 **HP** 340 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) **Weaknesses** good 15 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 60 feet, DC 35
**Unending Terror** Escaping fear near a phasmadaemon is no simple task. Creatures don't automatically reduce their [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition while they are within the phasmadaemon's Frightful Presence aura. Instead, they must attempt a Will save at the end of their turn against the DC of the effect that caused the condition. On a success, the creature's frightened condition is reduced by 1. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 35 feet **Melee** jaws +33 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+16 piercing plus 1d6 evil and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** claw +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+16 slashing plus 1d6 evil **Divine Innate Spells** DC 38, +30 - **1st** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will; good only) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will), [Nightmare](/Spells.aspx?ID=208) (at will) - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Shadow Siphon](/Spells.aspx?ID=274) (×2) - **6th** [Shadow Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=273) (×2) - **7th** [Phantasmal Killer](/Spells.aspx?ID=219), [Shadow Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=273) - **8th** [Mask of Terror](/Spells.aspx?ID=185) - **9th** [Duplicate Foe](/Spells.aspx?ID=93), [Weird](/Spells.aspx?ID=375) - **Constant (9th)** [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 3d10+6 bludgeoning plus 1d6 evil, DC 35 **Consume Fear** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Requirements** The phasmadaemon has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20); **Effect** The phasmadaemon feeds on the creature's mortality and innate terror, dealing 6d8 mental damage. The creature must attempt a DC 38 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature takes no damage and manages to break free from the phasmadaemon's grab.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and increases their [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) conditioned by 1, to a maximum of frightened 4.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and increases their frightened condition by 2, to a maximum of frightened 4. If the creature is already frightened 4, it must attempt a DC 38 Fortitude saving throw. If it fails, it is reduced to 0 Hit Points and dies. This effect has the [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) and [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93) traits. **Inescapable Form** The phasmadaemon can [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=31) through tight spaces as if it were a Small creature. While Squeezing, it can move at its full Speed. The phasmadaemon can even Squeeze through spaces that typically fit only a Tiny creature, but does so at the standard speed for Squeezing. **[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=30)** claw
Fearful Machinations While daemons don't typically require food, phasmadaemons seem intent on feeding on the emotions, and especially the fears, of mortals. Some believe that the life force and emotions of creatures serves as the fuel for a phasmadaemon's magic, while others believe that phasmadaemons simply enjoy facing a mortal eye-to-eye while feeding, delighting in the fear in their prey's final moments.
name: Phasmadaemon

They deal an additional 2d6 points of damage with their melee Strikes against [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) targets. This damage is equal to the damage type of the Strike.

<title level="1" pfs="">[Nornhound](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2582)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

While [norns](/Monsters.aspx?ID=742) typically keep to themselves, only consulting their triumvirate or occasionally other fellow norns, they sometimes require servants to further their goals or protect their homes. Nornhounds are massive wolves spun from the old, tattered threads of fate, usually moments of time that norns severed to end a life. These wolves gain a portion of the same power over fate that norns do and serve their norn masters with unflinching loyalty.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 43

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 41

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Nornhound.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 18">[Nornhound](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2582)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="LN" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Gargantuan" />
<trait label="Fey" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=69" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 84

**Perception** +32; [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) ([precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411)) 120 feet, [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +30, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +31, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +31, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +33, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +30

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +9

**Dex** +4

**Con** +8

**Int** +4

**Wis** +6

**Cha** +5
</row>

**Sense Fate** ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76)) A nornhound automatically rolls a 20 when it rolls initiative.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 40 

**Fort** +34 

**Ref** +28 

**Will** +30 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 421

, regeneration 15 (deactivated by [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375))

</row>

**Immunities**
[negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)

**Weaknesses**
cold iron 15

**Beyond Fate** A nornhound can ignore any [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) or [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) effects that affect them. If they're forced to roll twice as part of such an effect, they can take whichever die result they prefer, even if the effect would normally force them to choose one of the results.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet, fly 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +35 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 3d12+17 piercing damage plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d8+17 bludgeoning plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=20)

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
temporal blast +33 ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 6d8 force

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 37
- **6th**
[Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=289)
- **8th**
[Inevitable Disaster](/Spells.aspx?ID=930), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=338) (self only; at will)
- **9th**
[Cast into Time](/Spells.aspx?ID=880)
- **Constant (10th)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344)

**Breath Weapon** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The nornhound unleashes a blast of temporal energy in a 30-foot cone that deals 15d8 mental damage (DC 40 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save). Creatures that fail their save become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round. The nornhound can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds.

**Follow the Thread**   A nornhound is permanently [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32). It can use its extra action only to Step or Stride.

**Pack Attack**   The nornhound's Strikes deal 1d6 extra damage to creatures within reach of at least two of its allies. [Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35) <actions string="Single Action" /> (attack); Huge, 3d12+10 bludgeoning, Rupture 36

**Time Bound** <actions string="Three Actions" />  The nornhound jumps into the air, releasing multiple blasts of temporal energy. The nornhound leaps to a point within 60 feet. It can make up to three temporal blast Strikes at any point during this movement, each against a different target. These attacks count toward the nornhound's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after all the attacks.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Nornhound Packs</title>

Long-lived or powerful [norns](/Monsters.aspx?ID=742) sometimes spin multiple nornhounds and keep entire packs as guardians. These packs make quick work of intruders and also gain additional powers when working together. Much like a norn triumvirate, a norn pack can their powers to affect the flow of time in a localized area or even stop time outright.

</aside>
name: Nornhound
