markdown:
While the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) and the [Dimension of Time](/Planes.aspx?ID=21) remain distinct realities, the two share a powerful connection. Suvardens are living embodiments of this connection. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Suvarden](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2583) **Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 85 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +16, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +17, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +15, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +15, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +15, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +13 **Str** +3 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +14 **Will** +17 **HP** 125 ([all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=1)) **Immunities** [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5), [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6), [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Reweave Fate** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Trigger** A non-suvarden creature within 30 feet of the suvarden attempts an attack roll, skill check, or saving throw; **Effect** The suvarden interferes with the triggering creature's action, pushing it toward a specific outcome. The suvarden decides on a favorable or an unfavorable outcome. With a favorable outcome, the creature rolls the triggering check a second time and takes the higher result; this is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect. With an unfavorable outcome, the creature rolls the triggering check a second time and takes the lower result; this is a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) effect. Regardless of the outcome, the triggering creature becomes immune to Reweave Fate for 1 day. --- **Speed** 40 feet, climb 20 feet, fly 20 feet **Melee** antler +16, **Damage** 2d12+6 piercing **Melee** hoof +16 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+6 bludgeoning **Primal Innate Spells** DC 25 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=142), [Know Direction](/Spells.aspx?ID=169) - **1st** [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) (×3) - **2nd** [Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=15) (×2) - **3rd** [Hypercognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=156) **Web of Fate** ([Divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The suvarden creates a web spun from the threads of fate in a 10-foot burst within 30 feet. The squares filled with the web are [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453). Each square can be cleared of the web by a single attack or effect that deals at least 5 slashing damage or 1 fire damage. A square has AC 5, and it automatically fails its saving throws. Each time a creature in the web begins to use a move action or enters the web during a move action, it must attempt a DC 25 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and doesn't need to attempt further saving throws against the web.
**Success** The creature is momentarily baffled by glimpses of possible futures and is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) for 1 round.
**Failure** Possibilities spun throughout the web overwhelm the creature. It becomes [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** As critical failure, but the duration is 1 minute and the creature is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) while it's confused.
A Matter of Time Suvardens have a connection to the [Dimension of Time](/Planes.aspx?ID=21), but exist more as natives of the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1). As a result, they do not possess the [Time](/Traits.aspx?ID=313) trait. Suvardens refuse to elaborate on their place within existence and time. A suvarden first tries to maintain the order of cause and effect with its guidance, but when that fails, more powerful or cruel creatures increasingly get involved. Most agree that meeting a suvarden is preferable to encountering a [hound of Tindalos](/Monsters.aspx?ID=702) or [siktempora](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=259), although there certainly exist powerful suvardens as well.
name: Suvarden

markdown:
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Sickened Doprillu](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2584) **Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 19 **Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_see invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +24, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +28, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +22 **Str** +8 **Dex** +6 **Con** +7 **Int** +1 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +26 **Will** +22 +2 status to all saves vs. [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) **HP** 260 , regeneration 20 (deactivated by [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27)) **Immunities** [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) (while wearing its mask), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) (while wearing its mask) **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 15 **Mask of Power** A doprillu's unique wooden mask is the source of its power. A doprillu deprived of its mask loses its regeneration and its immunity to enfeebled and slowed, and it immediately becomes [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13). The enfeebled value increases by 1 at the start of each of the doprillu's turns, to a maximum of enfeebled 4. If the mask is put back on, the doprillu immediately regains its abilities and loses the enfeebled condition. A creature can pull off the mask with a successful DC 34 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=34).
**Volcanic Veins** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) Fiery magma runs through the doprillu's veins. A creature that starts its turn [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the doprillu takes 7d6 fire damage.
**Deflect Arrow** **Trigger** The doprillu is the target of a physical ranged attack; **Requirements** The doprillu is aware of the attack, isn't [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against it, and has a hand free; **Effect** The doprillu gains a +4 circumstance bonus to its AC against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 40 feet, climb 40 feet **Melee** fist +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d8+16 plus 2d6 fire and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 26 - **Constant (6th)** [See Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) **Body Strike** **Requirements** The doprillu has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20); **Effect** The doprillu swings the grabbed creature as a weapon. This Strike has a +28 attack modifier and deals 3d8+16 bludgeoning damage. The Strike is [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) and has a [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) of 10 feet. On a hit, the grabbed creature takes half the damage dealt to the target. **Whirlwind Throw** **Requirements** The doprillu has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20); **Effect** The doprillu whirls the grabbed creature about, making a Body Strike against each creature in reach. After that Strike, the doprillu can hurl the grabbed creature up to 50 feet as a ranged Strike. This Strike has the same attack modifier and damage as Body Strike, but has the [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195) weapon trait.
name: Sickened Doprillu

On a failure, the attempt fails, the effect is lost, and the DC to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39) effects within the aura is increased by 1.
**Solar Flare** ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Trigger** The bul-gae takes damage; **Effect** The bul-gae releases a solar flare in a 30-foot emanation. After using this ability, the bul-gae's flames cool and they can't use Solar Flare again until after they have used Lunar Burst. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** jaws +32 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 3d8+17 piercing plus 2d6 cold or fire and [Improved Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=28) **Lunar Burst** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The bul-gae releases a blast of otherworldly frost that deals 6d8 cold damage to each creature in a 10-foot emanation (DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). On a critical failure, a creature also becomes [slowed 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35). After using this ability, the bul-gae's flames heat up and they can't use Lunar Burst again until after they have used Solar Flare. **Shadow Leap** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=143), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) **Requirements** The bul-gae is in [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) or [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=409); **Effect** The bul-gae dives into a shadow and reappears from another shadow within 40 feet. **Thermal Mastery** The bul-gae has total control over the powers of cold and fire. Each time they attack with their jaws, they can choose either cold or fire for the additional damage.

**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 50 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 48 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 45 [Ildamir](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2586) **Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 35 **Perception** +36; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Druidic](/Languages.aspx?ID=23), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +37, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +34, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +37, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +34, [Norn Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +36, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +32, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +32 **Str** +6 **Dex** +7 **Con** +6 **Int** +4 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +9 --- **AC** 45 **Fort** +35 **Ref** +33 **Will** +36 **HP** 375 ([all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=1)) **Immunities** [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5), [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6), [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Reweave Fate** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Trigger** A non-[suvarden](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2583) creature within 30 feet of the suvarden attempts an attack roll, skill check, or saving throw; **Effect** The suvarden interferes with the triggering creature's action, pushing it toward a specific outcome. The suvarden decides on a favorable or an unfavorable outcome. With a favorable outcome, the creature rolls the triggering check a second time and takes the higher result; this is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect. With an unfavorable outcome, the creature rolls the triggering check a second time and takes the lower result; this is a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) effect. Regardless of the outcome, the triggering creature becomes immune to Reweave Fate for 1 day. --- **Speed** 50 feet, climb 20 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** antler +36, **Damage** 4d12+16 piercing **Melee** hoof +36 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d10+16 bludgeoning **Primal Innate Spells** DC 42 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=142), [Know Direction](/Spells.aspx?ID=169) - **7th** [Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=15) (×2), [Mindlink](/Spells.aspx?ID=195) (at will), [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) (×3) - **8th** [Hypercognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=156), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=338) (self only) - **9th** [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) (self only) **Web of Fate** ([Divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The suvarden creates a web spun from the threads of fate in a 10-foot burst within 30 feet. The squares filled with the web are [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453). Each square can be cleared of the web by a single attack or effect that deals at least 5 slashing damage or 1 fire damage. A square has AC 5, and it automatically fails its saving throws. Each time a creature in the web begins to use a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) action or enters the web during a move action, it must attempt a DC 42 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and doesn't need to attempt further saving throws against the web.
**Success** The creature is momentarily baffled by glimpses of possible futures and is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) for 1 round.
**Failure** Possibilities spun throughout the web overwhelm the creature. It becomes [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round.
**Critical** Failure As critical failure, but the duration is 1 minute and the creature is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) while it's confused.
name: Ildamir

The curse ends if the cursed victim slays Trygve, or after 1 year passes.

</column>

<actions string="Single Action" />
_Icefang_ +39 ([Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)),

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +36 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 4d8+16 bludgeoning plus Grotesque Gift

**Grotesque Gift**  ([Olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246)) Bolti's attacks spatter his targets with rancid flesh and clotted blood. A creature damaged by one of Bolti's Strikes must succeed at a DC 39 Fortitude save or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (sickened 2 on a critical failure).

**Great Swipe** <actions string="Two Actions" />  Bolti makes a melee Strike and compares the attack roll result to the AC of up to three foes, each of whom must be within his melee reach and adjacent to each other. Roll damage only once and apply it to each creature hit. A Great Swipe counts as three attacks for Bolti's multiple attack penalty.

</column>

As [frightful presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17), plus a creature [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) by the aura becomes suspicious: it doesn't count any other creature as its ally and can't [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=75) or flank. On a critical failure, the creature also can't be a willing target for harmless or helpful magic.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Maintain Fate** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Trigger** The Keeper would be subjected to a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) effect, become [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35), or become [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36); **Effect** The Keeper manipulates reality to maintain control of her own destiny and twists the fate of those that dared tamper with her own. The Keeper disrupts the triggering effect and becomes [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) until the end of her next turn. She can use the extra action to [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94), Step, Stride, or Strike. If the effect originated from a creature, such as an enemy casting [_slow_](/Spells.aspx?ID=289) on the Keeper, that creature is targeted with an [_ill omen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=696) spell. The creature must attempt a DC 44 Will save to determine the effects of the spell. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 120 feet **Melee** _greatsword_ +40 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 4d12+16 slashing plus bloodbird **Melee** jaws +37 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 4d6+16 piercing plus bloodbird **Melee** talon +37 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 4d6+16 slashing plus bloodbird **Occult Innate Spells** DC 41 - **7th** [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=211) (at will), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) (at will) - **8th** [Crushing Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=57) (at will), [Modify Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=200) - **9th** [Ethereal Jaunt](/Spells.aspx?ID=105), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) - **Constant (9th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Bloodbird** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) A creature hit by a vilderavn's melee attack becomes cursed. It takes 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) that's difficult to stanch. The DC to stop the bleeding using [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=54) is 41, and healing the creature to full HP doesn't automatically end the bleeding. Removing the curse ends the bleeding. **Change Shape** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The vilderavn takes on the appearance of a Small or Medium [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91), [wolf](/Monsters.aspx?ID=415), [dire wolf](/Monsters.aspx?ID=416), or hybrid with both raven and wolf parts. The vilderavn can only use their jaws attack when in a form with a wolf's head, and their talon attack in a form with raven qualities. They can instead assume their raven knight form: a Medium humanoid in black [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13) carrying a [greatsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=24). They can use their jaws or talon Strikes only in a form that has that body part, and their greatsword only in knight form. **Souleater** ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) If the vilderavn kills a [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) target with a critical hit using their jaws Strike, they rip out and devour the target's heart and soul as part of the attack. While the target is dead, the vilderavn can Change Shape into the target's form, gaining a +4 status bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) the target. If magic would resurrect the creature, the caster must succeed at a DC 44 [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check to extract the target's soul from the vilderavn; otherwise, the spell fails.
name: The Keeper

**[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 44 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 [Svarýr Commander](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2590) **Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 43 **Perception** +33; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8); ravenspeaker, [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +34, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +38, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +34, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +34, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +33 **Str** +10 **Dex** +7 **Con** +7 **Int** +3 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +7 **Claimer of the Heroic Dead** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Svarýr commanders can detect the souls of those recently dead. A commander spends 10 minutes praying over the body of a creature who has been dead for no more than 12 hours; if that creature is worthy of becoming an einherji and of the appropriate alignment, the commander transforms that creature into a [Svarýr soldier](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2589).
**Ravenspeaker** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) Valkyries use ravens as servants and spies. They can speak with ravens, and they can have up to three raven servitors who follow their commands. Valkyries can constantly observe whatever their commanded ravens sense. **Items** _+2 [greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10)_, _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) [spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10)_ --- **AC** 44 **Fort** +34 **Ref** +32 **Will** +31 **HP** 330 **Resistances** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 20 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Recall the Fallen** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** An allied creature within 60 feet who isn't a [construct](/Traits.aspx?ID=35) or [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) is reduced to 0 Hit Points and their [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) value is 2 or less; **Effect** The commander restores 8d10 Hit Points to the target. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** _spear_ +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d6+17 piercing plus 2d12 electricity **Ranged** _spear_ +36 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 3d6+17 piercing plus 2d12 electricity **Divine Innate Spells** DC 38, attack +30 - **3rd** [Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=15), [Sanctified Ground](/Spells.aspx?ID=265), [Status](/Spells.aspx?ID=308), [Wanderer's Guide](/Spells.aspx?ID=368) - **6th** [Disrupting Weapons](/Spells.aspx?ID=80), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Heroism](/Spells.aspx?ID=149) - **7th** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) (self and mount only) - **8th** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) - **9th** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Weapon of Judgment](/Spells.aspx?ID=372) - **Constant (9th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Storm of Battle** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The commander hurls their spear into the air, creating a massive storm in a 100-foot burst. Spears of lightning rain down upon enemies in the area, dealing 5d12 electricity damage (DC 41 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).
name: Svarýr Commander

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 48 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 45 [Gegnir](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2591) **Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 44 **Perception** +35; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +38, [Fortune-Telling Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +35, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +37, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +35 **Str** +10 **Dex** +4 **Con** +10 **Int** +2 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 44 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=1) **Fort** +38 **Ref** +30 **Will** +33 **HP** 470 , regeneration 40 (deactivated by [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3) or [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) **Weaknesses** fire 20 **Head Regrowth** Gegnir has nine heads, and he can use regeneration to regrow a head that is severed from an effect like a [_vorpal_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=308) weapon. After regaining Hit Points from regeneration, the jotund troll attempts a DC 8 flat check. On a success, one missing head is fully restored; on a critical success, two missing heads are fully restored. If Gegnir loses his last remaining head, he dies immediately.
**Multiple Opportunities** A jotund troll gains an extra reaction per round for each of their heads beyond the first, which they can use only to make Attacks of Opportunity with their jaws or to Fast Swallow. They can't use more than 1 reaction for the same triggering action, even if a creature leaves several squares within their reach, and the jotund troll must use a different head for each Attack of Opportunity they make. Whenever one of Gegnir's heads is severed, he loses 1 of his extra reactions per round.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**[Catch Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=7)** --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 40 feet **Melee** jaws +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d12+18 piercing **Melee** claw +38 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+18 slashing **Melee** intestine +38 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d4+10 bludgeoning plus Intestinal Tether **Occult Innate Spells** DC 39 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=142), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246) - **2nd** [Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=15) (at will) - **8th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194), [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) (×3) - **9th** [Foresight](/Spells.aspx?ID=127), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) - **Constant (9th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Cacophonous Roar** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) Gegnir emits a cacophonous roar from all his heads with a mystical power that distorts the listener's mind. Each non-troll creature within 100 feet must attempt a DC 42 Will save. Gegnir can't use Cacophonous Roar for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Cacophonous Roar for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is confused for 1d4 rounds. **Fast Swallow** **Trigger** Gegnir Grabs a creature with his jaws; **Effect** Gegnir uses [Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35). **Gut Tug** Gegnir's intestines slither back into his body, pulling a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by his intestines toward himself. Gegnir rolls an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the creature's Fortitude DC. With a success, he pulls the creature into an adjacent space, and if he critically succeeds he can also make a jaws Strike against the creature after the pull. **Intestinal Tether** A creature hit by Gegnir's intestine becomes [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20). The creature isn't [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), but it can't move beyond the [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) of Gegnir's intestine. A creature can sever the intestine with a successful Strike against AC 41 that deals at least 20 slashing damage. This deals no damage to Gegnir but prevents him from using his intestine Strike until he regrows his intestine, which takes 1 minute. **Ravenous Jaws** Gegnir makes a number of jaws Strikes up to his number of heads, each against a different target. These attacks count toward the troll's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after he makes all of these attacks. **[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=30)** claw **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) Medium, 4d12+10 bludgeoning, Rupture 40
name: Gegnir

**Befuddling Lash**  ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) When the Speaker damages a creature with her tail, a wave of befuddlement clouds the target's thoughts. The creature must succeed at a DC 42 Will save or become [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (stupefied 2 on a critical failure) for 1 minute.

**Manipulate Luck** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The huldra touches another creature to manipulate the creature's luck. The creature must attempt a DC 42 Will save. On a failure, the huldra chooses good luck or bad luck. If the huldra chooses good luck, the affected creature can roll twice on one d20 roll within the next minute and use the higher result; this is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect. If the huldra chooses bad luck, the creature must roll twice and use the lower result on its next d20 roll; this is a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) effect.

**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 50 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 48 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 45 [The Betrayal](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2593) **Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 57 **Perception** +33; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +35, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +38, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +38, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +33 **Str** +7 **Dex** +7 **Con** +7 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +10 **Harrowkin Suit** Crowns
**Read the Cards** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Trigger** The harrowkin is about to roll initiative; **Effect** The harrowkin attempts to rewrite destiny. Roll 1d6 to determine a suit. The harrowkin gains the effects of the suit for 1 minute. If the suit matches the harrowkin's suit, the value is doubled.
  • **Hammers** The harrowkin gains a +2 status bonus to damage rolls.
  • **Keys** The harrowkin gains a +1 status bonus to AC.
  • **Shields** The harrowkin gains [fast healing 5](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15).
  • **Books** The harrowkin gains a +1 status bonus to skill checks.
  • **Stars** The harrowkin gains a +1 status bonus to saving throws.
  • **Crown** The harrowkin can reroll 1 check within the next minute and take the higher result. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect.
**Items** [_harrow deck_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=824) (53 cards; function as [darts](/Weapons.aspx?ID=68)) --- **AC** 45 **Fort** +33 **Ref** +35 **Will** +31 **HP** 360 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Defensive Suit** When the harrowkin attempts a saving throw connected to its suit—that is, Fortitude (Hammers or Shields), Reflex (Keys or Books), or Will (Stars or Crowns)—if they roll a success, they get a critical success instead.
**Twist Fate** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Trigger** A creature critically hits the Betrayal; **Effect** The Betrayal manipulates fortune in her favor. The triggering attack becomes a hit instead. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** claws +37 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 4d12+15 slashing **Ranged** _harrow card_ +37 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 4d6+15 piercing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 42, attack +34 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **1st** [Color Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=44), [Ill Omen](/Spells.aspx?ID=696), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244) - **2nd** [Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=15), [Hideous Laughter](/Spells.aspx?ID=150), [Mirror Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=197) - **3rd** [Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=26), [Hypnotic Pattern](/Spells.aspx?ID=157), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=213) - **4th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), [Phantasmal Killer](/Spells.aspx?ID=219), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) - **5th** [Shadow Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=273), [Shadow Siphon](/Spells.aspx?ID=274), [Synesthesia](/Spells.aspx?ID=328) - **6th** [Feeblemind](/Spells.aspx?ID=112), [Spirit Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=301), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) - **7th** [Prismatic Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=233), [Visions of Danger](/Spells.aspx?ID=358), [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=369) - **8th** [Discern Location](/Spells.aspx?ID=75), [Maze](/Spells.aspx?ID=187), [Uncontrollable Dance](/Spells.aspx?ID=347) - **9th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Foresight](/Spells.aspx?ID=127), [Wail of the Banshee](/Spells.aspx?ID=361) **Shuffle the Deck** ([Divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** The harrowkin has rolled initiative; **Effect** At the end of the current round, all creatures currently in initiative reroll their initiative, using the same modifiers as their original initiative roll, and use the new initiative values for all following rounds. **Shift Reality** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) If the Betrayal is in the Harrowing Room, she vanishes and then reappears at any location she chooses in Sonnorae's Library. If she's in the library, she vanishes and reappears in a space of her choice in the Harrowing Room.
name: The Betrayal

<title level="1" pfs="">[Raven Nicoletta, Thief Of Fate](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2594)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Born to a relatively minor noble family in Magnimar, Raven was never comfortable with the life she led. Social functions, overly proper etiquette, and everything else that life in high society brought with it seemed like an unnecessary set of restrictions and rituals for the sake of playing a part.

 As part of a coming-of-age ceremony, Raven was required to recount a history of her family lineage. Delving into her family's past led Raven to discover a buried part of her family history—a major divergence in the family tree after her great-great-grandmother's marriage. When Lonirra married Kamittar Nicoletta, she hid all vestiges of her former family, the Campostari clan. Raven was intrigued by the discovery and soon used the resources afforded to her to learn more about her family's forgotten past.

 Over the span of several months, Raven learned the great history of the Campostari harrowers, including how some of them traced their lineage back to the Imlios clan, a family of archivists, chroniclers, and harrowers dating back to the days before Earthfall. While the connection between Campostari and Imlios was already veil-thin by the Age of Darkness, Raven saw this as a sign. She was indeed fated for a life beyond that of nobility, and her destiny was kept hidden from her for years. With this information in hand, Raven ran away from home, intent on reconnecting with the Campostari side of her lineage.

 Raven's first stop was the city of Korvosa. She believed it big enough for her to start her search, but far enough away from Magnimar to avoid attempts by her family to take her back home. On her first day in the city, she met [Zellara Esmeranda](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2595), a local harrower making ends meet by performing readings and teaching new students how to use the harrow. Raven saw this encounter as another sign and immediately became Zellara's student. She spent the next few years living alongside Zellara, learning the secrets of the harrow and seeing Zellara as the mother she never had. During this time, she became aware of her magical abilities and learned to refine them with Zellara's help. After some time, Raven felt she had to move on and seek the Campostari clan, and with Zellara's blessing, the young woman set out from Korvosa.

 When Raven finally did locate her lost clan, she was elated. Unfortunately, the clan was not as eager to meet Raven. They saw Lonirra's abandonment of the clan as a betrayal, and Raven's connection to nobility immediately painted her in a harsh light. Raven's welcome was indifferent at best and, in some case, actively hostile. Heartbroken, Raven returned to Korvosa only to find that Zellara was gone. Unbeknownst to Raven, Zellara had died in the time that Raven was away, and the city's poor records had no information on her fate. Raven even consulted the harrow to try to locate Zellara, but she misinterpreted the lack of guidance as a sign that Zellara had intentionally abandoned her, choosing to hide her tracks even from harrow readings. This second betrayal, as Raven saw it, was enough to turn her bitter and resentful. She decided that if she was going to forge a destiny, she would need to do so on her own.

 Raven dedicated the following years to learning as much about the harrow as she could. Her search for knowledge turned up lost information pertaining to the Imlios clan, their work, and specifically, Sonnorae and the Harrowed Realm. Eventually, her search for knowledge led her to the Harrowed Three and their plan to create [the _Deck of Destiny_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2531). It was in their deck that she saw her ultimate goal: a way to fully claim her own fate once and for all.

 Raven was eventually able to contact [Lord Drustan](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2556) and [the Prince of Wolves](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2574). She formed an alliance with the two. When the time came for the Unmatched to steal the deck, Raven maneuvered Lord Drustan and the Prince of Wolves to lead the attack while she went in and stole the deck, hoping to escape with it before the other two arrived. Raven was unprepared for the norns to scatter the deck, however, so she retreated, hoping to develop a new plan and finally claim the deck for herself. 

<title level="2" right="">Campaign Role</title> Raven is the most dangerous member of the Unmatched, and is Stolen Fate's true villian. Unlike Lord Drustan and the Prince of Wolves, Raven doesn't simply want to tip the scales of fate in her favor with the Deck of Destiny's harrow reading. She plans to control destiny altogether.

[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31), [Skald](/Languages.aspx?ID=42), [Varisian](/Languages.aspx?ID=44)

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +31, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +41, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +37, [Fortune-Telling Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +33, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +35, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +33, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +31, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +31

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +5

**Con** +2

**Int** +3

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +9
</row>

**Items**
[_bracers of armor type III_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=416), [_circlet of persuasion_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=400), [_Deck of Destiny_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2531)

</column>

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 44 

**Fort** +30 

**Ref** +33 

**Will** +35 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 370

</row>

**Reclaimed Fate** Raven has eerie control over her immediate future. She can ignore any [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) or [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) effects that affect her. If she is forced to roll twice as part of a fortune and misfortune effect, she can take whichever die result she prefers, even if the effect would normally force her to choose one of the results. Finally, when rolling initiative, Raven can choose not to roll initiative and instead have a result of 16 + her initiative modifier.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +31 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)),
**Damage** 1d4+8 bludgeoning

**Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 42, attack +34
- **Cantrips (10th)**
[Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) (*), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334)
- **1st**
[Color Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=44), [Ill Omen](/Spells.aspx?ID=696), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244), [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) (4 slots)
- **2nd**
[Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=15) (*), [Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=28), [Hideous Laughter](/Spells.aspx?ID=150), [Mirror Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=197) (4 slots)
- **3rd**
[Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=26), [Hypnotic Pattern](/Spells.aspx?ID=157), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=213), [Wanderer's Guide](/Spells.aspx?ID=368) (*) (4 slots)
- **4th**
[Blink](/Spells.aspx?ID=27), [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), [Phantasmal Killer](/Spells.aspx?ID=219) (*), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) (4 slots)
- **5th**
[Cloak of Colors](/Spells.aspx?ID=41), [Shadow Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=273), [Shadow Siphon](/Spells.aspx?ID=274) (*), [Synesthesia](/Spells.aspx?ID=328) (4 slots)
- **6th**
[Feeblemind](/Spells.aspx?ID=112), [Spirit Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=301), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=338), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) (*) (4 slots)
- **7th**
[Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=125), [Prismatic Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=233), [Retrocognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=260) (*), [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=369) (4 slots)
- **8th**
[Discern Location](/Spells.aspx?ID=75), [Maze](/Spells.aspx?ID=187), [Uncontrollable Dance](/Spells.aspx?ID=347), [Unrelenting Observation](/Spells.aspx?ID=351) (*) (4 slots)
- **9th**
[Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Foresight](/Spells.aspx?ID=127), [Wail of the Banshee](/Spells.aspx?ID=361), [Weird](/Spells.aspx?ID=375) (*) (4 slots)
- **10th**
[Alter Reality](/Spells.aspx?ID=8), [Gate](/Spells.aspx?ID=130) (2 slots)

**Bloodline Magic**   When Raven casts a bloodline spell (marked with an asterisk), she gains [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) for 1 round, but can't use it to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62).

**Harrow Burst** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Raven unleashes a mass of magical harrow cards, dealing 15d8 slashing damage in a 30-foot emanation (DC 42 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). A creature that fails its save rolls 1d6 to determine a suit of cards: 1 = hammers (Strength), 2 = keys (Dexterity), 3 = shields (Constitution), 4 = books (Intelligence), 5 = stars (Wisdom), 6 = crowns (Charisma). The creature takes a –1 status penalty to all checks related to that ability score for 1 round (–2 status penalty on a critical failure). Raven can't use Harrow Burst again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

markdown:
The Esmeranda clan was a storied group of traveling merchants, fortune-tellers, and performers whose lineage could be traced as far back as ancient Thassilon (or so the family tales say). When the eldest daughter of the clan, Zellara, found love during a visit to the city of Korvosa and elected to settle down in that city, the rest of the clan considered her departure bittersweet. Losing Zellara was a blow for the clan, but they were not about to interfere with the desires of her heart. Zellara bid her family farewell and settled down with her husband, Aramir, in the city's cosmopolitan Midland district. The two found a home, had a son, and generally lived a happy life for a few years. When Zellara lost Aramir to sickness, the rest of her clan urged her to return to them, but she had grown to love Korvosa and its people. She chose to stay and make a living as a harrower for tourists and interested locals. She also took on several students during this time, teaching them the honored tradition of harrowing that was passed down among her clan. Unfortunately, Zellara's love of the harrow would ultimately lead to her death. During an outing in the city, pickpockets working for a small-time criminal named Gaedren Lamm stole Zellara's cherished harrow deck. Her son Eran took it upon himself to recover the deck, only to end up dying at the hands of Gaedren's thugs. With her son dead and the city guard unable to help, Zellara searched for Eran's killer, eventually tracking down Gaedren to his hideout only to be slain by him as well. Overcome with anguish and despair at the moment of her death, Zellara's spirit connected to her harrow deck, allowing her to live on as a [ghost](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=51) intent on stopping Gaedren and protecting the city of Korvosa. As a ghost, Zellara recruited a group of heroes to help her with her goal. From her harrow, Zellara could advise the heroes and frequently performed harrow readings to guide them during their adventures. With Zellara's help, these heroes not only brought Gaedren to justice, but eventually liberated all Korvosa from the tyranny of its new ruler, Queen Ileosa Arabasti. In the final days of this campaign to save Korvosa, Zellara's deck become empowered, transforming into a [_harrow deck of many things_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=610). This new deck gave the heroes a greater advantage moving toward their final battle. Feeling she had accomplished her goal and could trust the heroes to save the city, Zellara's spirit moved on to the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15). Once there, she awaited her soul's judgment, but realized that her connection to the power of the harrow deck of many things afforded her more liberty than other souls. She eventually discovered she could step “out of line” from her judgment, free to return when saw fit. Zellara saw this development as an opportunity to visit her loved ones one last time before moving on. Zellara returned to Golarion and watched the lives of her clan, the saviors of Korvosa, and her former students. She spent several years observing them from afar, occasionally providing subtle guidance to help them with their goals or problems. Among these was her final student, [Raven Nicoletta](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2594). Zellara checked in with Raven several times over the years, watching her grow into a brilliant harrower, surpassing Zellara's skill and even some of the greatest harrowers of the Esmeranda clan. In recent years, however, Zellara watched Raven become selfish, obsessed with power and legacy, and fall into the ways of evil. Zellara remained with Raven, hoping to return her to a better path, but found herself no longer able to influence her former student. Raven was lost to her. Zellara now believes that Raven is beyond saving and, while mourning this loss, hopes to find someone to put an end to the evil Raven seeks to enact on the world. Campaign Role Zellara is a guiding figure for the heroes. She's aware of Raven's entire history and can bring the heroes up to speed on Raven's abilities and motivations. Although Zellara's grown more powerful as a ghost than she ever was in life, Raven is still beyond her ability to stop all on her own—she needs the PCs to step up to this task. Zellara sees the same potential in these heroes as she saw in the heroes of Korvosa over a decade ago. She hopes that these heroes will be the ones to put an end to Raven's evil machinations, and perhaps even save her from herself. Having gained wisdom in her afterlife, Zellara can provide any information or advice the heroes might need on their journey to recover [the Deck of Destiny](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2531). **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 42 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 42 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 [Zellara Esmeranda](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2595) **Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 90 **Perception** +27; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), harrow sense **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Shoanti](/Languages.aspx?ID=41), [Varisian](/Languages.aspx?ID=44) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +26, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +28, [Fortune-Telling Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +26, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +28, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +26 **Str** -5 **Dex** +6 **Con** +2 **Int** +4 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +6 **Boundless Spirit** Unlike other ghosts, Zellara is not bound to a specific site or object. She can freely manifest wherever she likes.
**Harrow Sense** Zellara can detect the presence of harrow cards as an [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) sense out to 1 mile. She can detect harrow cards as a [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) sense out to 120 feet. --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +28 **Will** +25 **HP** 195 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), rejuvenation) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** all damage 15 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) **Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When Zellara is destroyed, she re-forms after 2d4 days at the site of her destruction, fully healed. If she is bound to a [harrow deck](/Equipment.aspx?ID=824) or harrow card, Zellara can manifest near the bound item instead. Once her ex-student [Raven](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2594) has been defeated, Zellara will return to the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15) to face her final judgment.
**Unbound Fate** Zellara's connection to the harrow and fortune-telling in life have given her full control of her fate in the afterlife. She can ignore any [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) or [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) effects that affect her. If she is forced to roll twice as part of a fortune and misfortune effect, she can take whichever die result she prefers, even if the effect would normally force her to choose one of the results. --- **Speed** fly 25 feet **Melee** ghostly hand +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d12+8 negative **Ranged** ghostly harrow card +26 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d8+8 negative **Occult Innate Spells** DC 34, attack +26 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=229), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **1st** [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45), [Mindlink](/Spells.aspx?ID=195) - **2nd** [Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=15), [Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=333) - **3rd** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Hypercognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=156) - **4th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), [Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=247) - **5th** [Crushing Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=57), [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=272) - **7th** [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=338) (at will; self only), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Flurry of Cards** Zellara unleashes a barrage of harrow cards that deals 7d6 negative damage in a 30-foot cone (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). **Harrow Binding** ([Divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Requirements** Zellara is within 120 feet of a harrow card or [harrow deck](/Equipment.aspx?ID=824); **Effect** Zellara intentionally binds herself to a harrow card or deck she can sense. While bound in this way, she grants her Stack the Deck ability to anyone carrying the bound card or deck. The trigger and effect for Stack the Deck changes to affect the creature carrying the bound item. She can still Stack the Deck for herself while bound. Zellara can unbind herself from the item as an action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait. **Stack the Deck** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Trigger** Zellara is about to attempt a saving throw; **Effect** Zellara looks to her immediate future and determines the most favorable outcome for her. She gains a +4 circumstance bonus to the triggering saving throw. If she rolls a critical failure on her saving throw, she can reroll the check, but does so without the bonus.
name: Zellara Esmeranda

<title level="1" pfs="">[Augdunar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2596)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Selectively bred over the course of centuries to be as strong, tough, and grouchy as the [dwarves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1) of Highhelm themselves, augdunars are a unique breed of mule favored by the merchants of the Sky Citadel for transporting goods and assisting with manual labor. Unlike mules found elsewhere on Golarion, augdunars are capable of producing offspring—though significantly fewer than ordinary equines. This has led to a steady, but controlled, stock of the creatures, allowing for the population to be self-sustaining. Augdunars are smaller than typical mules to accommodate dwarven height, measuring about four and a half feet tall and weighing between 400 and 600 pounds.

 Because of their ability to carry loads beyond that of the traditional mule, in addition to the ability to breed amongst themselves, augdunars are a frequent target of thieves or opportunistic merchants. No Highhelm citizen worth their stone willingly sells an augdunar to outsiders, so attempted thefts on the road are common. Unfortunately for these robbers, the mules are quite loyal to their owners and bray loudly whenever a stranger handles their leads. Combined with a heavy kick to the skull from the animal, any attempts to make off with an augdunar have low odds of succeeding.

 While rare, small groups of the augdunar have broken away from dwarven control over the years and created feral colonies in the Five Kings Mountains. These colonies are largely safe from ordinary predators, such as [wolves](/Monsters.aspx?ID=415), and graze on mountainside plants. Due to their low birthrate, feral augdunars don't need to have their population controlled, though caravanners do attempt to capture and redomesticate them in an effort to increase profit.

 Despite being natural beasts of burden, dwarves don't often use augdunars as mounts. The mules seem to have an instinctive dislike of riders, to which the deep bite marks and hoofprints left in those who try can attest. That's not to say that some haven't succeeded, however. Those who put forth the effort required to create a bond with an augdunar find that their persistence can pay off. Augdunar loyalty is unparalleled and even thieves know to not attempt to steal an augdunar that's carrying a rider.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

hoof +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 1d10+4 bludgeoning plus [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38)

**Back Kick** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The augdunar whirls around, delivering a powerful kick with both its hind legs. The augdunar makes a hoof Strike. If the Strike hits, the augdunar deals an extra 1d10 bludgeoning damage. This counts as two attacks when calculating the augdunar's multiple attack penalty.

**Determined Trudge** <actions string="Single Action" />  The augdunar Strides at a –5 foot circumstance penalty to its Speed, ignoring [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Augdunar Husbandry</title>

The augdunar breed of mule is uniquely situated to life in the Five Kings Mountains. They can subsist on relatively small patches of grass or hay, even during periods of heavy work, and require about as much daily water as a camel. The majority of Highhelm's augdunars are stabled in King's Crown to allow easier access to the surface and hitching to caravans. While the population of augdunars is now self-sustaining, the original methods used to create the breed were lost in one of the many attacks in Highhelm's history. Multiple [dwarf](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1) clans are actively pursuing the rediscovery of this knowledge, though with limited success. The competition between these clans has led to ongoing rivalries. The birth of an augdunar by a purebred horse or donkey has come to be considered a good omen among the citizens of Highhelm, signaling a year of increased prosperity
</aside>
name: Augdunar

Stout reptiles with scales in a checkered gold and white pattern, draft lizards sport powerful jaws and even stronger backs capable of hauling loads four times their own weight. Native to the underground tunnels of the Five Kings Mountains, the reptiles have been domesticated to serve as beasts of burden within the Depths and sprawling web of passages beyond. Zoologists theorize that they are a part of the same reptile family as [zetogekis](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1375), as the two share a similar build and other physical characteristics.
Draft lizards within the vicinity of Highhelm have adapted their diet to feed mainly on grindlegrubs. However, residual behavior suggests in lean times or before domestication they would devour stones for sustenance. The animals can still be observed habitually gnawing on boulders or large stones, cracking them with their powerful jaws and seeming to savor the taste as they roll the debris around in their mouths. Overworked draft lizards will stray from their handler's control in order to consume rocks, only to return back to their path with a new undercurrent of purpose.
Normally as docile as any other beast of burden, the draft lizards of Highhelm will defend themselves if attacked by predators from the Darklands. Curiously, the lizards have an intense hatred for [hryngars](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=38), attacking them without any prompting or hesitation. No dwarven records indicate teaching this behavior during the domestication of the animals, leaving its origins a mystery. Some caravanners even claim that the reptiles can sense the presence of hryngars, giving advanced warning of attacks by hissing menacingly into the darkness.
Though too large to be exceptionally dexterous, with careful training some draft lizards can be taught performative tricks. The Rolling Wheel is particularly known for a trio of reptiles that balance on their tails, catch hurled boulders in their jaws, and other stunts. The spectacle is well known in Highhelm, considered one of the big acts that the group performs. This has led to some younger dwarves attempting to teach their animals the same feats, mostly resulting in failure and light property damage in the Depths. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Draft Lizard](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2597) **Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 127 **Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +8, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +10 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** -4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +8 **Will** +11 **HP** 60 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** jaws +14, **Damage** 2d6+7 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) plus Lizard Poison **Melee** claw +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d4+7 slashing **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 2d8+7 piercing, DC 21 **Lizard Poison** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 21; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d4 poison damage and [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d6 poison damage and clumsy 1 (1 round); **Stage 3** 2d6 and clumsy 2 (1 round)
Draft Lizard Habitats The domestication of the draft lizard occurred at some point during the beginning of the Quest for Sky, when the [dwarves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1) who would later rediscover Highhelm encountered the creatures within the Darklands. After many failed attempts that resulted in crushed or lost limbs, the dwarves managed to bribe the reptiles into docility with a steady supply of grindlegrubs. In time, draft lizards have become central to dwarven caravans and movement within the subterranean tunnels of the Five Kings Mountains. The popularity of draft lizards as dwarven mounts comes as a surprise to visitors of the Sky Citadel, as the creatures are rarely seen above ground. When Highhelm was resettled by the dwarves, an attempt was made to bring the mounts to the surface, but the reptiles stubbornly refused to enter natural sunlight. While some progress has been made adapting them to the surface, it remains a rare sight to see a draft lizard outside of the stone warrens.
name: Draft Lizard

<title level="1" pfs="">[The Bloodstorm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2598)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A hundred thousand leeches, mosquitos, and parasites form a roiling mass of hunger and malice. It moves like a storm through the tunnels and caverns of the Emperor's Peak, constantly in search of its next meal. Though it has many bodies, it has just one, startlingly clever mind. The flames and smoke of the cities keeps it at bay, but in the darkness, it is at home. The [dwarves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1) of Highhelm have learned how to live with the threat, but those traveling deeper into the mountain must always remain vigilant.

 In the swamps of the Tanglebriar, food is difficult to come by. So when a grievously wounded demon, a [shemhazian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=102) known as Gratheyr, fled into its waters to escape from his enemies, every starving creature swarmed at the smell of blood. As the creatures devoured his flesh, they were corrupted by his [Abyssal](/Planes.aspx?ID=13) blood, and their hunger only grew. They feasted until nothing but his heart remained, still beating at the center of the swarm, binding it to a single will. With its meal finished, the bloated swarm left the swamp in search of its next prey. It moved aimlessly, hunting animals and travelers alike, until a great rumble beneath the earth attracted its attention. The Bloodstorm followed the vibrations deep underground, toward the scent of sweat and the sounds of voices.

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<row gap="medium">

---

**AC** 35

**Ref** +28 

**Will** +25 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 250

</row>

**Immunities**
precision, [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=36)

**Resistances**
physical 15

**Weaknesses**
area damage 15, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 15, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 15

**Bloodsense** The Bloodstorm can precisely sense [bleeding](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) creatures within 120 feet.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet, climb 40 feet, fly 15 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
**Requirements** The Bloodstorm is in Demon Shape; **Effect** pincer +27  ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d8+15 piercing plus 1d4 evil and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27)

**Biting Constrict** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The Bloodstorm is in Demon Shape and has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20); **Effect** The writing mass of the Bloodstorm's pincer bites the grabbed creature, dealing 3d8+8 piercing damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). On a failure, the creature also takes 1d8 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

**Biting Swarm** <actions string="Single Action" />  Each creature in the Bloodstorm's space takes 3d6 piercing and 3d6 slashing damage (DC 31 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). Creatures that fail their saving throw also take 1d8 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

**Feeding Frenzy** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** There are [bleeding](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) creatures in the Bloodstorm's space; **Effect** The bleeding creatures take 3d8 piercing and 3d8 slashing damage (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). The Bloodstorm gains temporary Hit Points equal to half of the damage dealt this way.

**Demon Shape** <actions string="Single Action" />  The swarm condenses into the solid shape of a [shemhazian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=102) demon. While in this form, it's a gargantuan creature, cannot share the space of another creature, and can use its pincer attack. It loses its resistance to physical damage as well as its weaknesses to area and splash damage. Any creatures sharing a space with the Bloodstorm when it transforms are pushed into the nearest empty space. The Bloodstorm can use this action again to revert to its swarm shape.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Sadistic Hunter</title>

While the Bloodstorm is mostly driven by animal instincts, it still has traces of the shemhazian's obsession with torment and pain. It's been known to leave victims wounded but alive, allowing them to narrowly escape. The swarm then follows the trail of blood, remaining hidden until the victim find's someone who can offer aid. Once the rescuer has treated the victim's injuries and they feel safe, it fills the tunnel with the sound of buzzing and floods in to strike. While [dwarves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1) from urban centers may consider these old wives' tales, those further from the city know well the dangers of an open wound.
</aside>
name: The Bloodstorm

Hand-carved by the Father of Creation himself and strengthened by the souls of history's most heroic dwarves, the Grand Defender serves as [Torag's](/Deities.aspx?ID=18) herald across the planes. While a true messenger of a deity, the Grand Defender manifests itself in a unique way to other heralds. The chassis of the Grand Defender exists within Forgeheart, Torag's subterranean realm in [Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=17). This frame is empty, however, and can only serve basic functions, much like a [golem](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=57). When faithful call upon the Grand Defender, Torag rouses the construct with spirits. As the might of the Grand Defender comes from Torag's will and the power of the souls within, the Grand Defender can take on any number of forms. Torag can send these spirits to alter any sufficiently sized statue of a dwarven ancestor. This process temporarily converts the carving into the Grand Defender itself, complete with its abilities and equipment. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 44 **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 44 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 [Grand Defender](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2599) **Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 129 **Perception** +29; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3), [Ignan](/Languages.aspx?ID=18), [Terran](/Languages.aspx?ID=22); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +30, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +28, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +28 **Str** +8 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +3 **Items** _defender's shield_, can't be damaged), [warhammer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=24) --- **AC** 40 **Fort** +29 **Ref** +23 **Will** +29 **HP** 280 (special) **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigue](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), magic (see below), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 15 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374)) **Weaknesses** evil 15 **Ablative Adaptation** **Frequency** once per minute; **Trigger** The Grand Defender's resistance to physical damage is overcome by the resistance's exception; **Effect** The Grand Defender sheds the outer layer of its body, altering its material composition and general appearance. The material that overcomes the Grand Defender's resistance changes to [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374), [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375), [mithral](/Equipment.aspx?ID=377), or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379) (Grand Defender's choice).
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**[Golem Antimagic](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=57)** harmed by acid (8d10, 2d10 from area or [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)); healed by fire (area 2d10 HP); slowed by electricity
**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32)**
**Vulnerable to Rust** Magical rusting effects, like a [rust monster's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=354) antennae, affect the Grand Defender normally. --- **Speed** 35 feet, burrow 35 ft. **Melee** warhammer +30 ([Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)), **Damage** 3d12+14 bludgeoning plus [Improved Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=28) **Ranged** force blast +28 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d12+8 force **Divine Innate Spells** DC 36 - **5th** [Flame Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=120), [Repelling Pulse](/Spells.aspx?ID=982) (×2) - **6th** [Blade Barrier](/Spells.aspx?ID=24) (as hammers dealing bludgeoning instead of slashing) **Hammer Storm** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The Grand Defender unleashes a volley of warhammers that deal 12d8 bludgeoning damage in a 50-foot cone (DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The Grand Defender can exclude any number of squares from the cone's area, preventing damage to creatures in those squares. Creatures that critically fail their save are [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet away from the Grand Defender and knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). The storm creates 24 Medium [warhammers](/Weapons.aspx?ID=47) that persist after the attack and can be used by creatures. The Grand Defender can't use Hammer Storm for 1d4 rounds.
Highhelm Statues As the pinnacle of [dwarven](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1) civilization for centuries, the Sky Citadel of Highhelm has endured many invasions. To cement their safety as [Torag's](/Deities.aspx?ID=18) chosen people, the dwarves have erected dozens of statues befitting the manifestation of the Grand Defender. The most famous of these is a colossal likeness of Taargick, the King of the Sky. Weathered and damaged beyond repair after a half dozen battles in which the Grand Defender protected the city, it now stands where the herald last left it, gazing over Stonebreach.
name: Grand Defender

The labyrinthine caverns and tunnels beneath Highhelm are home to many dangers, with the insidious graul being one of the more notable native species. These highly specialized ambush predators possess a natural camouflage that renders them completely imperceptible to all forms of [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) as they float silently through the darkness, allowing them to hunt creatures that rely on specialized senses to navigate their lightless domain. Surprisingly cunning, grauls are adept at using their magical talents for illusion and mimicry to separate potential prey from allies and light sources alike. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 21 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 [Graul](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2600) **Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 130 **Perception** +14; blindsight (precise) 120 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +10, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +10, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +12 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +3 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +8 **Will** +14 **HP** 60 **Darkvision Invisibility** The graul's rubbery flesh is saturated with a unique biochemical compound that renders it undetectable by any form of [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415). In [dim](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) or [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407), the creature can be perceived normally by any kind of vision.
**Odorless** The graul exudes no natural aroma, rendering it undetectable to creatures using the [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) ability. --- **Speed** fly 25 feet **Melee** jaws +14, **Damage** 2d8+5 piercing plus paralysis **Melee** tentacle +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d6+5 bludgeoning plus Tentacle Grab **Primal Innate Spells** DC 18 - **1st** [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=356) (at will) - **2nd** [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=160) **Paralysis** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93)) A living creature struck by a graul's jaws attack must succeed at a DC 20 Fortitude save or become [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28). It can attempt a new check at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each such check. A creature paralyzed by this ability is also affected by the graul's darkvision invisibility for as long as the effect persists. **Seize** **Requirements** The graul has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) with its tentacle; **Effect** The graul pulls the creature toward itself. The graul attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the creature's Fortitude DC. With a success, the graul [pulls](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) the creature 15 feet closer to it. **Sound Mimicry** The graul can perfectly imitate the sounds and speech of other creatures by attempting a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check against a listener's Will DC. The graul doesn't need to understand the language it's mimicking, but when speaking a language it doesn't know, the creature can only repeat specific words and phrases it has heard in the past 24 hours. The graul can't use this ability to duplicate voice-based abilities or spells. **Tentacle Grab** A creature hit by the graul's tentacle becomes [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the graul. The creature isn't [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), but it can't move beyond the reach of the graul's tentacle. A creature can sever the tentacle with a Strike against AC 18 that deals at least 10 slashing damage. This deals no damage to the graul but prevents it from using its tentacle Strike until it regrows the tentacle, which takes 1 round. The graul can move without ending the Grab as long as the creature remains within the tentacle's reach.
Photophobia Although grauls are not harmed or physically affected by light, the overwhelming sensation of vulnerability they experience when directly exposed drives them to avoid it wherever possible. Travelers through the tunnels surrounding Highhelm are advised to be on the lookout for blind and darkvision-possessing fauna that appear to be congregating and making homes near bioluminescent fungi clusters or other natural light sources they might normally ignore, as this behavior often indicates the presence of a graul's hunting ground.
name: Graul

markdown:
Pagulins are massive, multi-legged lizards that stalk the mountain passes of the Five Kings Mountains. These reptiles hunt [elk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2201) and [augdunars](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2596) by lying in wait behind rock formations or within the openings of caves. Pagulins produce a mildly acidic saliva that they spit at prey to temporarily blind them before moving in for the kill. A pagulin's tail features a deadly thagomizer, four large spikes that it uses to impale prey to prevent their escape. Five Kings citizens know that pagulins are much smarter than they look, with many reports of pagulins intentionally allowing prey to escape, only to follow a creature to its den and feast on its entire family. Though pagulins are deadly, most in the Five Kings Mountains are indifferent to them. Pagulins tend to rest for days at a time after a hunt, spending time to digest their massive kills. They're particularly territorial, however, and will gladly rouse from a post-meal stupor to defend their lairs. The lizards are also known to attack anything that interrupts a hunt or scares off prey. Many merchant caravans have dealt with an unexpected pagulin attack after riding past a herd of startled deer. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 25 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 [Pagulin](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2601) **Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 131 **Perception** +14; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +15, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +15 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** -4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +15 **Will** +12 **HP** 120 --- **Speed** 25 feet, climb 15 feet **Melee** jaws +18 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d10+9 piercing plus Grab **Melee** claw +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d6+9 slashing **Melee** tail +16 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+6 piercing plus Tail Impalement **Ranged** spit +16 ([range 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** Blinding Spittle **Blinding Spittle** A creature hit by a pagulin's spit must attempt a DC 22 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round. It can use an Interact action to wipe away the spittle and remove the dazzled condition.
**Failure** As success, except the creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round instead of dazzled.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature is blinded for 1 minute. The creature needs 2 total Interact actions to wipe away the spittle. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** **Damage** 1d10+6 piercing, DC 25 **Spike Rend** **Requirements** The pagulin has a creature impaled on its tail; **Effect** The pagulin violently pulls its tail out of the creature, dealing 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) to the creature. **Tail Impalement** A creature hit by a pagulin's tail becomes impaled on one of the tail's spikes. The creature becomes [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20). If the pagulin moves, it brings the grabbed creature along with it. The pagulin doesn't need to use additional actions to keep the creature grabbed; the creature remains grabbed as long as it's impaled. The grabbed creature can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) as normal. Large and larger creatures are immune to a pagulin's Tail Impalement.
Pagulin Lairs Pagulins tend to reside within the countless natural caves of the Five Kings Mountains. Identifying these caves is generally easy as the cavern floor and walls are pocked with small holes created from pagulin spittle. While pagulins are most common within the Five Kings Mountains and surrounding ranges, they also live in the more arid steppes and mountain ranges of northwestern Avistan. These pagulins are rarer than their Five Mountains cousins but have adapted to their differing environment. Rather than residing in caves, they create small burrows beneath shrubs or squeeze into crevices between large rocks. Reports of northern pagulins note how they're much more irritable, which some attribute to the hotter climes of these regions. Others attribute this to the cooler nights, as these pagulins seem most irritable early in the morning.
name: Pagulin
