<title level="1" pfs="">[Hyphae Tyrant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2602)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Leshy creations sometimes fall prey to herbivores and foragers. Whereas most take the loss in stride, fungus leshies sometimes grow bitter and explore the grimmer elements of fungi: decomposition and possession. Combining unethical experiments, mind-altering cordyceps spores, and occult magic fueled by their own victim narrative, a fungus leshy can transform into a hyphae tyrant, who in turn transforms corpses into sporeborn puppets to torment local fauna. These leshies' greatest tragedy is perhaps their own impermanence. As their agendas increasingly celebrate decay, a hyphae tyrant's spite and occult narrative slowly drain their life force, ultimately ending in a final burst of destructive energy.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fungus](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/HyphaeTyrant.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 4">[Hyphae Tyrant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2602)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Fungus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=77" />
<trait label="Leshy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=225" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #193: Mantle of Gold](/Sources.aspx?ID=206) pg. 86

**Perception** +11; darkvision, sporesense (imprecise) 60 feet

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Druidic](/Languages.aspx?ID=23), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11); speak with plants (fungi only)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +8, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +10, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +2

**Con** +4

**Int** +1

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +5
</row>

**Sporesense** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134))A hyphae tyrant intuitively senses and tracks all nearby fungi. The sense can distinguish between fungus creatures, features made of fungus, and creatures covered in spores.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 20 

**Fort** +12 

**Ref** +13 

**Will** +11 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 60

</row>

**Resistances**
negative 5

**Spore Burst** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) When a hyphae tyrant dies, a burst of primal energy explodes from its body in a 30-foot emanation, restoring 3d8 to each fungus creature and dealing 3d8 negative damage (DC 20 basic Fortitude save) to each non-fungus creature in the area. This area is filled with fungus, becoming [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d6+4 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
spore pod +12 ([range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 2d6+3 bludgeoning plus spores

**Occult Prepared Spells** DC 21, attack +13
- **Cantrips (2nd)**
[Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66)
- **1st**
[Lose the Path](/Spells.aspx?ID=940), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244)
- **2nd**
[Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=141)

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 20
- **2nd**
[Protector Tree](/Spells.aspx?ID=976) (creates fungus)
- **4th**
[Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=294)

**Primal Rituals** DC 20

- **2nd**
_create mycoguardian_

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The hyphae tyrant transforms into a Small fungus. This ability otherwise uses the effects of [_tree shape_](/Spells.aspx?ID=342).

**Spore Cloud** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) A hyphae tyrant unleashes a cloud of spores in a 20-foot emanation. Each non-fungi creature in this area must succeed at a DC 20 Fortitude save or take 2 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). A creature has its vision reduced as long as the persistent damage continues and can see only within 20 feet (or 10 feet on a critical failure).

**Spores**   A creature that takes damage from a hyphae tyrant's spore pod Strike must attempt a saving throw with the same DC and effect as its Spore Cloud ability.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Creating Mycoguardians</title>

To create a mycoguardian, such as a sporeborn, a ritualist can perform a ritual similar to [_create undead_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=10) called _create mycoguardian_, but providing valuable parasitic fungal spores instead of black onyx. This ritual uses Fungus Lore (trained) or Nature (expert) for both the Primary Check and Secondary Checks, and lacks the Evil trait.
</aside>
name: Hyphae Tyrant

markdown:
If a soulbound doll isn't large and strong enough for one's needs, there's the oversized soulbound mauler. An upgrade to the soulbound doll, this mannequin is powered by the entire soul of a sapient creature. Many a necromancer has attempted to use these constructs as a method to live forever, likely as a shortcut around the complicated and ultimately morbid path to lichdom. However, the soul within the mannequin has problems properly expressing itself and displays rudimentary control over the host, often resulting in childlike behavior anywhere from curious to sadistic.
Rather than embedding the soul focus gem in the construct's neck or chest, it's tethered by bands of ectoplasm within an exposed chamber on the mannequin's chest. Until the soulbound mauler is destroyed, the focus gem cannot be removed from the empty cavity in its chest. However, the gem can be displaced which causes the soulbound mauler significant anguish, not only damaging the connection between the gem and artificial body, but also impairing the construct's ability to function. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 21 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 [Soulbound Mauler](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2603) **Source** [Pathfinder #193: Mantle of Gold](/Sources.aspx?ID=206) pg. 87 **Perception** +10; darkvision **Languages** one spoken by its creator (typically Common) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +9 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +0 **Personality Cage** A soulbound mauler encapsulates an entire soul within its soul focus gem, with that soul's personality and past shining through along with a confusing mix of fragmented memories and instincts. This causes a soulbound mauler to match the donor soul's alignment and gain the corresponding alignment traits. When the mauler doesn't have a soul bound, it's inanimate.
 --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +11 **Will** +9 **HP** 72 **Immunities** bleed, death effects, disease, doomed, drained, fatigued, healing, mental, necromancy, nonlethal attacks, paralyzed, poison, sickened, unconscious --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d6+5 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 18, attack +10 - **1st** [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244) (at will) - **3rd** [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=141), one additional spell depending on the donor soul’s alignment (see sidebar) **Eager Rend** **Requirements** The soulbound mauler has a living creature grabbed. **Effect** The soulbound mauler holds the creature it has grabbed and attempts to tear it in two. That creature takes 5d6 bludgeoning damage (basic DC 18 Fortitude). On a critical failure, the target also becomes [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) 1. **Tethered Soul** The soul focus gem that powers a soulbound mauler is tethered with eight ectoplasmic leashes inside an open cavity in the creature's chest. It can be targeted directly with a [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41), [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35), or [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=68) action. Rather than the normal result of these actions, the soulbound mauler takes 4d6 mental damage and is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) 1 on a success; on a critical success, it takes 8d6 damage and is stunned 2.
Innate Spells Like a soulbound doll, a soulbound mauler gains an additional 3rd-level innate spell depending on its alignment, as listed below.
**Lawful Good:** [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148)
**Neutral Good:** [_jump_](/Spells.aspx?ID=167)
**Chaotic Good:** [_haste_](/Spells.aspx?ID=147)
**Lawful Neutral:** [_blindness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=26)
**Neutral:** [_invisibility sphere_](/Spells.aspx?ID=165)
**Chaotic Neutral:** [_shatter_](/Spells.aspx?ID=279)
**Lawful Evil:** [_slow_](/Spells.aspx?ID=289)
**Neutral Evil:** [_stinking cloud_](/Spells.aspx?ID=309)
**Chaotic Evil:** [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=146)
name: Soulbound Mauler

markdown:
While most sporeborn are created from the flesh of vertebrates or creatures with exoskeletons, fungal and plant creatures serve as a fine host for a sporeborn's parasitic spores. In these cases, the sporeborn mycelial network burrows through the corpse, replacing the original hyphae in fungal creatures and the xylem and pith in plants. This allows the sporeborn to puppet the creature, much as it does in mammals by replacing the tendons with its own rugged mycelium. Fungus and plant hosts are more likely to develop the potent poison sporeborn ability to enhance the hosts' natural abilities. **[Recall Knowledge - Fungus](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Sporeborn Myceloid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2604) **Source** [Pathfinder #193: Mantle of Gold](/Sources.aspx?ID=206) pg. 89 **Perception** +10; darkvision, scent (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +10 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** -5 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -5 --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +9 **Will** +10 **HP** 70 **Weaknesses** slashing 5 **Rancid Spore Pods** The sporeborn is covered in spore pods that rupture when it takes any piercing damage or a critical hit. In either case, adjacent creatures are hit with spores, causing them to become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) 1 unless they succeed at a DC 18 Fortitude save.
**Spore Pop** If a sporeborn myceloid is reduced to 0 HP by a critical hit, it pops, forcing it to immediately Emit Spores, even if it has already used the ability that day. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** fist +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d6+4 bludgeoning plus purple pox **Emit Spores** **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The sporeborn myceloid expels spores in a 10-foot burst centered on a corner of its own space. This cloud lasts until the start of the sporeborn myceloid's next turn. Each creature that is in the cloud or enters it is exposed to purple pox. **Purple Pox** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) Myceloids are immune;
**Saving Throw** DC 22 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 minute;
**Stage 1** 2d6 poison damage and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) 1 (1 day);
**Stage 2** 6d6 poison damage, stupefied 3, and the creature is compelled to seek out the nearest myceloid colony—this compulsion is a mental and emotion effect (1 day);
**Stage 3** The creature dies. Over 24 hours, its corpse becomes bloated and bursts, releasing a new, fully grown myceloid. **Spore Domination** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The sporeborn myceloid targets one creature affected by purple pox within 60 feet. That creature must attempt a DC 24 Will save. It is then temporarily immune to spore domination for 10 minutes **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** Until the end of its next turn, the target is helpful to sporeborn myceloids and can’t take hostile actions against them.
**Failure** As success, but for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As success, but until the purple pox is cured.
name: Sporeborn Myceloid

markdown:
The soul of a gnome killed by tragedy sometimes becomes so entangled in conflicting emotions that it becomes trapped and reanimates its own corpse. Known as unfeeling empaths, these undead wander in search of others' emotions. When they find thinking prey, they relentlessly goad that creature to elicit and consume their feelings. Though initially harmless, feeding drives an unfeeling empath into an expressive frenzy as they overload the victim's mind, resuming their senses only after their prey is dead. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Unfeeling Empath](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2605) **Source** [Pathfinder #193: Mantle of Gold](/Sources.aspx?ID=206) pg. 90 **Perception** +9; darkvision, empathic sense (precise) 60 feet **Languages** [Gnomish](/Languages.aspx?ID=5), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +9, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +8, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +5 **Str** +3 **Dex** +1 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +4 **Empathic Sense** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The unfeeling empath perceives all creatures affected by an emotion effect at the listed range. --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +5 **Will** +11 **HP** 36 (negative healing) **Immunities** death effects, disease, paralyze, poison, sleep **Resistances** negative 5 **Weaknesses** mental 5 **Emotional Extremes** If a creature rolls a success at a check to affect an unfeeling empath with an emotion effect, the creature gets a critical success instead. If a creature rolls a failure to affect an unfeeling empath with an emotion effect, the creature gets a critical failure instead. Likewise, when an unfeeling empath rolls a success at a Will save against an emotion effect, it gets a critical success instead. When a unfeeling empath rolls a failure at a Will save against an emotion effect, it gets a critical failure instead. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** fist +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 1d8+3 bludgeoning **Cathartic Howl** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) **Requirement** The unfeeling empath is under an emotion effect; **Effect** The unfeeling empath attempts to counteract the emotion effect, with a counteract modifier of +11. It then howls, projecting those emotions in a 15-foot cone (or a 30-foot cone if the unfeeling empath successfully counteracted the emotion effect). Creatures in the area take 2d8 mental damage (DC 18 basic Will save). A creature that critically fails this saving throw also takes 1d4 [persistent mental damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). **Drink Emotions** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60)) **Effect** The unfeeling empath regains 1d4 Hit Points and becomes emotionally excited for 3 rounds. While excited, the unfeeling empath takes a –1 penalty to AC, gains a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, and deals an additional 1d4 mental damage with its melee Strikes. **Goad** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The unfeeling empath floods a creature within 30 feet with countless memories and sensations. The target must attempt a DC 18 Will save. **Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** The target is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) 1 until the end of the unfeeling empath's next turn.
**Critical Failure** The target is stupefied for 1d4 rounds.
Misery Loves Company Unfeeling empaths appreciate any companions who can endure their outbursts. Lost souls and beings who prey on emotions sometimes adopt an unfeeling empath out of solidarity or because the undead gnomes are easy to torment. Thus, where these creatures appear, a sakhil, will-o'-wisp, or ghost is often nearby.
name: Unfeeling Empath

Strength and hunger drive most xulgath societies, reinforced by the tenets of their abyssal patron Zevgavizeb. A powerful leader can seize control of a xulgath community and direct their followers to fight as a unit, but formation fighting isn't something most xulgaths practice. This mob mentality often results in overzealous warriors tripping over or even biting each other. Suitably, these mobs have earned the name “ravenings,” a term also used when describing Zevgavizeb's carnivorous rampages and referring to the millennia of xulgath infighting and social decline. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Xulgath Ravening](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2606) **Source** [Pathfinder #193: Mantle of Gold](/Sources.aspx?ID=206) pg. 91 **Perception** +11; darkvision **Languages** [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +7 **Str** +5 **Dex** +1 **Con** +3 **Int** -1 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +12 **Will** +8 **HP** 72 (16 squares) **Weaknesses** area damage 5, splash damage 2 **Stench** ([aura](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=5), olfactory) 30 feet. A creature that enters the area must attempt a DC 21 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) 1, and on a critical failure, the creature also takes a –5 status penalty to its Speeds for 1 round. While within the aura, the creature takes a –2 circumstance penalty to saves to recover from the sickened condition. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune to all xulgaths stenches for 1 minute.
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44) --- **Speed** 25 feet; troop movement **Fang and Claw** **Frequency** once per round **Effect** The xulgath ravening lashes out at enemies within 5 feet (DC 21 basic Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions. 1d6 slashing damage 1d6+5 slashing damage 2d6+5 slashing damage **Infected Wounds** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) The xulgaths' Fang and Claw deals an additional 1d6 poison damage to any creature sickened by a xulgath's stench. **Sharpened Advance** The troop fires a ranged attack in the form of a 5-foot burst within 50 feet that deals 3d6 piercing damage (DC 18 basic Reflex save), then the troop either Steps or Strides up to 15 feet toward the area they attacked. **Troop Movement** Whenever a troop Strides, it first Forms Up as a free action to condense into a 20-foot-by–20- foot area (minus any missing squares), then moves up to its Speed. This works just like a Gargantuan creature moving; for instance, if any square of the troop enters difficult terrain, the extra movement cost applies to the whole troop. **[Form Up](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=43)**
The Power of Suggestion Xulgath ravening are most common in clutches controlled by spiritual leaders using some combination of cult behavior, group ritual, and even a little enchantment magic. By convincing participants that they're possessed by Zevgavizeb or a similar force, the leader then identifies a target and hopes for the best. Instilling secular doubt or incapacitating the leader can shatter a ravening's focus, causing its members to scatter or even turn on their leader.
name: Xulgath Ravening

Krohan's mistakes kicked off several important campaign developments, such as Zogototaru's rampage and the machinations of a hryngar villain the PCs haven't met yet. Krohan provides a strong hook for the PCs to recover and repair Taargick's clan dagger, all while providing greater context for the legends of High King Taargick and Clan Tolorr. During this adventure, Krohan's gradual discovery of his occult abilities also helps illustrate Rivethun lore, giving the players a window into this iconic dwarven tradition. Importantly, Krohan doesn't seek the spotlight. His own attempt at heroics inflicted real harm, and he would much rather resolve past misdeeds and support the PCs' adventuring careers.
Krohan's journey parallels many of the campaign's themes: acknowledging past wrongs and working to repair the damage. In helping him, the players can better internalize these objectives for when they depart Highhelm and have opportunities to start mending misdeeds of the past. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Krohan Veldollow](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2607) **Source** [Pathfinder #193: Mantle of Gold](/Sources.aspx?ID=206) pg. 93 **Perception** +9; darkvision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +9, [Dwarf Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +8, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +8, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +8, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +5 **Str** +2 **Dex** +0 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +1 **Items** [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Pick](/Weapons.aspx?ID=34), [Composite Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75) (20 arrows), [Buckler](/Shields.aspx?ID=1), [Adventurer's Pack](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1), [lesser healing potion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=186), [lesser antidote](/Equipment.aspx?ID=80) --- **AC** 18 (19 with shield raised) **Fort** +9 **Ref** +7 **Will** +10 **HP** 52 **Resistances** poison 2 **Occult Catharsis** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Krohan's magic converts his pain into spellpower. After he casts an innate occult spell, he attempts a DC 5 flat check, increasing the DC by twice the spell's level if it was not a cantrip. On a success, Krohan's Spirits Drawn to Suffering effect ends, he can reduce the value of one condition affecting him by 1, and he regains 2d6 Hit Points.
**Unwitting Rivethun** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Krohan has very limited access to Rivethun magic, able to cast spells only when experiencing pain or distress.
Spirits Drawn to Suffering ([occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes **Trigger** Krohan critically fails a saving throw, takes mental damage, takes damage from a critical hit, orgains a harmful condition with a numerical value (such as frightened or sickened). This effect does not trigger due to self-harm or similarly engineered circumstances. **Effect** Krohan gains the ability to cast his innate occult spells for 1 minute. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** pick +10 ([fatal d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=178)), **Damage** 1d6+2 piercing **Melee** composite shortbow +8 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d6+1 piercing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 19, attack +11 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Forbidding Ward](/Spells.aspx?ID=126) - **1st** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Magic Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=182), [Spirit Link](/Spells.aspx?ID=302) (3 slots) - **2nd** [Soothe](/Spells.aspx?ID=291), [Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=333) (2 slots) **Righthand Man** Krohan gains a +2 circumstance bonus to attack rolls and skill checks attempted to [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=75) others. He can attempt a [Dwarf Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) check in place of any other attack roll or skill check to Aid a [dwarf](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1).
name: Krohan Veldollow

This applies to any PC who didn't succeed at one or more skill checks to clean area D2.
**Site Bound** The ancestral echo can't leave the upper crypt. --- **AC** 16 **Fort** +7 **Ref** +6 **Will** +9 **HP** 15 (negative healing, rejuvenation) **Immunities** death effects, disease, paralyze, poison, precision, unconscious **Resistances** all damage 5 ([except <%TRAITS%75%%> force](/Traits.aspx?ID=124) <%END>, [ <%TREASURE%297%%> _ghost touch_](/Traits.aspx?ID=217) <%END>, [or <%TRAITS%128%%> positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) <%END>; double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) **Rejuvenation** ([necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Restoring the vandalized inscriptions and reciting their corrected stories ushers these spirits back to the afterlife. --- **Speed** fly 20 feet

Ferghaz **Languages** Empyrean, Common, Dwarven

**Ranged** chiding rebuke +8 (range increment 30 feet, magical), **Damage** 1d4+3 negative
**Oh, If Your Ancestors Could See You!** Ferghaz holds other dwarves to an impossible standard, and his attacks carry the weight of a hundred generations' judgment. His Strikes deal an additional 1d4 mental damage to dwarves.
Ulki **Languages** Draconic, Dwarven, Orcish
**Attack of Opportunity**
**Melee** ghostly greataxe +8 ([finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 1d12+3 fire

A creature that enters the area must attempt a DC 21 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) 1, and on a critical failure, the creature also takes a –5 status penalty to its Speeds for 1 round. While within the aura, the creature takes a –2 circumstance penalty to saves to recover from the sickened condition. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune to all xulgaths stenches for 1 minute.
 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** +1 clan dagger +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Dwarf](/Traits.aspx?ID=54), [Parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=190), [versatile B](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d4+3 piercing **Melee** jaws +10, **Damage** 1d6+3 piercing **Melee** claw +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 1d6+3 slashing **Ranged** shattering chant +12 ([range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)), **Damage** 2d6+4 sonic **Rituals** DC 17 - **2nd** [Consecrate](/Rituals.aspx?ID=8) **Far-Reaching Fear** The range at which Hhrulkaz can [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) Demoralize creatures increases to 60 feet, and she doesn't take a penalty if the creature doesn't understand her language. **Predator's Decree** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Requirements** Hhrulkaz is within 60 feet of a frightened creature or a creature she successfully hit with a shattering chant this turn; **Effect** Hhrulkaz condemns the creature to be eaten. The creature must make a DC 20 Will save. **Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** The target gains weakness 2 to all damage for 3 rounds. At the end of each of its turns, the target can attempt a new Will save to reduce the remaining duration by 1 round, or end it entirely on a critical success.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target gains weakness 4 to all damage. **Sacrificial Takedown** Hhrulkaz attempts an Athletics check to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) a creature. If she succeeds, she then makes a melee Strike against the target, granting her weapon the [fatal d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=178) trait until the end of her turn. Both attacks count toward her multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after she makes both attacks.

markdown:
The truth of life in the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) is that weather controls the world. The life-giving water that flows across Golarion, the maintenance of healthy forests, and the snows, thaws, and rains that help crops flourish all rely on the weather—weather that's shaped at the whims of the winds. Anemoi are powerful beings from the [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=4) tasked by ancient gods to guard the skies and shepherd the winds of every world across the planes. They guide the winds along their natural paths, and while neither malevolent nor malicious, an anemos has little regard for how a storm might affect any creature caught in its path. Anemoi can take several forms and shift between them as easily as they slip between the breezes. They wear humanoid guises to walk among mortals without drawing undue attention, and they transform into great, thunderous horses made of storm clouds and rain when they need to move quickly across the skies. Their true form, however, is wind coalesced into a humanoid shape, often with curling hair and feathery wings made of soft, drifting clouds. On Golarion, each of the four cardinal winds are created and directed by a quartet of cardinal anemoi. The cold north winds are guided by Boreal, bringer of winter. Austral shepherds hot winds from the south. Eural brings stormy winds from the east. Finally, the gentle winds of spring come at the call of Zephyr, from the west. These four cardinal anemoi, alongside their councils of lesser anemoi, shape and control the winds across all of Golarion. As far as anyone can tell, the cardinal anemoi don't answer to a greater authority. There are even more powerful maximal anemoi living in the Plane of Air, but they seem content to leave the Universe out of their plans. Though the cardinal anemoi try to keep a degree of separation from mortal affairs, lesser anemoi occasionally take a closer interest. Some monitor a single settlement or county, protecting, nourishing, or even vindictively destroying it. Each one has their own sets of interests. Many are vain or petty. Even more change attitudes just as quickly as the winds they guide. Pleasing an anemoi isn't a matter of following a set formula, but rather repeatedly changing course to give them what they desire for their mercurial current interests or perceived needs. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 43 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 41 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 [Anemos](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2614) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 78 **Perception** +33; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111); truespeech **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +38, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +31, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +33, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +31, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +38, [Planar Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +33, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +31, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +36, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +33 **Str** +4 **Dex** +9 **Con** +7 **Int** +6 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +9 **Truespeech** An anemos can speak with and understand any creature that has a language.
**Wind Orchestra** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16)) An anemos does not require [instruments](/Equipment.aspx?ID=37) to perform music, instead using their winds to create and carry the sounds of any instruments they wish to duplicate. They can mimic any number of instruments simultaneously, creating and directing their own personal orchestra. --- **AC** 43 **Fort** +29 **Ref** +33 **Will** +30 **HP** 310 , regeneration 15 (deactivated by earthbane) **Immunities** bleed, [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 20 **Blessed by the Winds** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206)) 80 feet. The winds grow turbulent for those who would dare to fly in the same space as an anemos, but they take care to never harm or inconvenience their shepherd. Air within the emanation is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) for [Flying](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) creatures that do not have the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5) trait. While the aura is active, the anemos cannot be affected by environmental air or weather affects unless they choose to be.
**Earthbane** An anemos's regeneration is suppressed for 1 round if the anemos is affected by an [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55) effect, or for as long as they are in contact with the ground and 1 round thereafter. If an anemos is submerged in at least 1 inch of mud, dirt, or stone, the anemos's aura deactivates, and the anemos becomes [stunned 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) and [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3).
**Redirect Weather** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) **Requirements** The anemos's aura is active; **Trigger** A creature within the anemos's aura uses an [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5) or [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) spell, or an air or electricity spell otherwise comes into effect within the anemos's aura; **Effect** The winds and weather of the spell obey the anemos's call. The anemos makes all the choices to determine the targets, destination, or other effects of the spell, as though they were the caster. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 200 feet; swiftness **Melee** thunderbolt +35 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d8+12 electricity plus 3d6 sonic **Ranged** air blast +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d10+12 bludgeoning **Ranged** thunderbolt +35 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 3d8+10 electricity plus 3d6 sonic **Divine Innate Spells** DC 40 - **1st** [Air Bubble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1438) (at will) - **4th** [Airlift](/Spells.aspx?ID=1303) (at will), [Vapor Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1738) (at will), [Voice on the Breeze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1325) (at will) - **7th** [Humanoid Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1560) (at will) - **9th** [Cleanse Air](/Spells.aspx?ID=1313) **Divine Rituals** DC 40 - **6th** [Sky Signs](/Rituals.aspx?ID=102) - **8th** [Control Weather](/Rituals.aspx?ID=9) **Collect Thunder** ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The anemos has a hand free; **Effect** The anemos runs a hand through the winds that swirl around them, the sparks from their fingertips coalescing into a thunderbolt. The anemos creates a thunderbolt in their open hand. If the anemos spends an action to Collect Thunder, a bolt instead strikes their open hand, creating a booming peal that deafens all creatures within 20 feet for 1 round unless they succeed at a DC 40 Fortitude save; this adds the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) trait to Collect Thunder. Any thunderbolts dissipate after 1 round. **Command the Breeze** When an anemos casts a ritual, they perform all aspects of the ritual themself, commanding their winds to complete all the ritual's components. They must fulfill any requirements for the ritual's additional casters and must attempt the checks normally performed by additional casters. In addition, anemoi can cast rituals faster than usual. If a ritual has a casting time measured in days, they can cast it in an equal number of hours. **Storm Strikes Twice** ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) The anemos throws a thunderbolt, then becomes a wind that carries them in an instant to the bolt's location to attack again. They make a ranged thunderbolt Strike against a creature within their first range increment, teleport to the creature's location as a gust of wind, then grasp the thrown thunderbolt and make a melee thunderbolt Strike against a creature within reach. Their multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until they've made both attacks. **Swiftness** The anemos's movement doesn't trigger reactions.
The Cardinal Anemoi Anemoi who are particularly ancient and powerful often have their own unique capabilities. In addition to their standard abilities, Golarion's cardinal anemoi possess the following additional innate spells. **Austral**, the south wind: **8th** [_flame vortex_](/Spells.aspx?ID=911); **5th** [_geyser_](/Spells.aspx?ID=918)
**Boreal**, the north wind: **8th** [_frigid flurry_](/Spells.aspx?ID=916); **5th** [_howling blizzard_](/Spells.aspx?ID=47)
**Eural**, the east wind: **8th** [_chain lightning_](/Spells.aspx?ID=33); **5th** [_pressure zone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1318)
**Zephyr**, the west wind: **8th** [_field of life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=114); **4th** [_petal storm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=967), [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294)
The Shape of the Air Anemoi on the [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=4) are typically genderless, while those who spend time in the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) sometimes experiment with or develop preferences for one of the genders of the mortals they watch or live close to.
name: Anemos

markdown:
Blustering gales are among the least of air elementals: spirits of the small breezes who sometimes arise in the wake of mightier creatures, like the downdraft of a swallow's wing or the sharp expulsion of a vigorous cough. Unwilling or unable to act effectively alone, they form into “gales,” collections of weak elementals that use their combined power to hurl insults and pummel those who defy them in a misguided attempt to rise above their humble origins. Together, they force other creatures to do their bidding, then move on when they grow bored. While most gales claim their abandoned minions were useless, in truth, blustering gales lack the commanding presence and confidence to retain vassals for long. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Blustering Gale](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2615) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 80 **Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +20, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +18 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +24 **Will** +18 **HP** 150 (16 squares) **Immunities** bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 10 **Bullying Bluster** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101)) 30 feet. The blustering gale constantly unleashes a tirade of insults and aggressive taunts. Each enemy who enters or starts their turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 30 Will save or become [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for as long as they remain within the aura (stupefied 2 on a critical failure). The troop's members are collectively able to mimic a few curse words or insults in every language, allowing their Bullying Bluster to affect any creature who understands a language. A creature who succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 1 hour. The blustering gale can activate or deactivate the aura with a free action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.
**[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 60 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Pummeling Winds** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The blustering gale bashes, batters, and pummels each adjacent enemy (DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d8+2 bludgeoning damage
2d8+12 bludgeoning damage
3d8+15 bludgeoning damage **Windstorm** ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527)) The blustering gale exhales as a unit, creating a powerful windstorm. This windstorm is a 10-foot burst within 100 feet that deals 3d8+10 bludgeoning damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Creatures that fail their saving throw are [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet in any direction (plus knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) on a critical failure). When the troop is reduced to 8 or fewer squares, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst and the distance pushed decreases to 5 feet.
Lackeys Blustering gales sometimes serve as heralds or enforcers for other creatures (including cloud dragons, jaathooms, and other powerful air elementals), bullying and intimidating others at the behest of a more influential overlord. While blustering gales often feel secure and confident in these roles, they rankle at subservience, and rarely feel content with such an arrangement for long.
name: Blustering Gale

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Comozant Wyrd](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2616)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Buzzing, crackling plasma makes up a comozant wyrd, electrically charged waves of greens, blues, and purples cascading in heatless flame. Given a mind and a semblance of a face, comozant wyrds need a surface from which they can crackle and writhe. Once secure, they seek out companionship from anyone nearby. In the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1), they can be found in many places: lashing themselves to sailing ships, dancing across the buildings, or anchoring to the stones of a mountain range. In the vast spans of the [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=4), however, comozant wyrds often come to feel extremely lonely. A lucky few become pets for jaathooms living in the plane's cities, but many wait on isolated floating islands for an airship they can attach to or, more tragically, strive vainly to reach a distant shore before sputtering out.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/ComozantWyrd.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 5">[Comozant Wyrd](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2616)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=57" />
<trait label="Incorporeal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=222" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 81

**Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111)

**Skills**
[Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +11, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +12

<row gap="medium">
**Str** -5

**Dex** +4

**Con** +0

**Int** -1

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Plasmatic Form** Unlike other incorporeal creatures, a comozant wyrd can't move into or through solid objects. If a comozant wyrd isn't adjacent to a solid object or surface of its size or larger at the end of its turn, it loses 10 HP. This HP loss cannot be mitigated or avoided in any way.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 21 

**Fort** +7 

**Ref** +15 

**Will** +12 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 60

</row>

**Immunities**
[disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision

**Resistances**
all damage 5 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75) or [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103))

**Illuminating Flames** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 30 feet. The comozant wyrd sheds [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in the emanation and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for another 30 feet. Heatless flames similar to the wyrd's own surround any creature in the emanation. A visible creature can't become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) while in the emanation, and an [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) creature becomes concealed rather than [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39). The wyrd can communicate empathically with any non-[mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) creature affected by illuminating flames, even if they don't share a language.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 15 feet, fly 25 feet

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
lightning lash +15 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [range 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 2d12 electricity

**Leap the Gap** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** The comozant wyrd is adjacent to a solid object or surface of its size or larger; **Effect** The wyrd [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) up to its Speed in a straight line, ending its movement adjacent to a different solid object or surface of its size or larger; this movement doesn't trigger reactions. The wyrd can move through other creatures during this movement, and all creatures it moves through take 2d12 electricity damage with a DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.

**Wyrd Wisdom** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** The comozant wyrd is communicating empathically with another creature through illuminating flames; **Effect** The comozant wyrd's odd means of communication brings strange insight. One creature the wyrd is empathically conversing with gains the benefits of an [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=15) spell, though only about this conversation topic, rather than any topic of the creature's choice.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Comozant Communication</title>

Creatures of emotion and instinct, comozant wyrds use simple images and concepts to convey deep and layered meanings. They're quite insightful, able to leap to solid conclusions as rapidly as they leap across solid surfaces. Most who “converse” with a comozant wyrd find the process enlightening, but have little desire make it a regular experience.
</aside>
name: Comozant Wyrd

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Despairing Pall](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2617)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Despairing palls are small, dark clouds that float aimlessly through the [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=4), casting literal and emotional shadows wherever they go. Pranksters and mischievous elementalists often summon them to rain on a rival's parade.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Despairing Pall](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2617)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=57" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 82

**Perception** +5; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +8, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +8

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +4

**Con** +1

**Int** +0

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +2
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 17 

**Fort** +4 

**Ref** +10 

**Will** +7 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 15

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145)

**Duskflow** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39)) **Trigger** The despairing pall is damaged by a melee Strike; **Effect** Darkness billows out from the despairing pall, covering its attacker in inky shadow. The despairing pall immediately Steps up to 15 feet in any direction. If the despairing pall took the triggering damage due to a reaction it provoked by moving, it can then finish the movement. For one round, the triggering attacker is cloaked in [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=409) and perceives light as one step lower ([bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) becomes [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408), for example), affecting its ability to sense creatures and objects accordingly.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** fly 50 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hot air +7 ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527)),
**Damage** pushed 5 feet

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
lightning bolt +7 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [range increment 50 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 1d6 electricity

**Downcast** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The despairing pall [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) up to its Speed, then rains gloom and despair in a 15-foot line straight down. Creatures in the area must succeed at a DC 16 Will save or take a –1 status penalty to attack rolls until the end of the despairing pall's next turn.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-41" />
name: Despairing Pall

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Often considered cousins of [phades](/Monsters.aspx?ID=179), veiled currents are loose veils of wind who catch prey within their folds.

**Dex** +6

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 100

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145)

**Naturally Invisible** The veiled current is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) at all times. When they take a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) of any kind, the veiled current is [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) instead of [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) as the vague outline of their humanoid form becomes faintly visible until the start of their next turn.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** fly 50 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
static fold +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d10+8 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
static scream +19 ([range increment 50 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 2d8+4 sonic

**Envelop** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** The veiled current begins their turn with a target of their size or smaller [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20); **Effect** The veiled current maintains the Grab and coalesces around the creature, stretching themself into a semisolid veil that smothers the creature within. This thereafter has the same effect as if the veiled current had [Engulfed](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=14) the creature (DC 26, 1d8+8 bludgeoning, [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) DC 26, Rupture 17). As engulfing a creature is a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300), the veiled current is [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) instead of [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) every round it has a creature engulfed.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-41" />
name: Veiled Current

markdown:
The shifting shapes of clouds in the sky have captivated the imaginations of mortal children for centuries. Pictures-in-clouds represent this pure possibility of air, transforming into anything the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) imagines them to be. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Picture-in-Cloud](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2619) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 83 **Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +26, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +24 **Str** +4 **Dex** +8 **Con** +5 **Int** +4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +26 **Will** +19 **HP** 175 **Immunities** bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **High Winds** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206)) 60 feet. Air within the emanation is difficult terrain for [Flying](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) creatures that don't have the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5) trait.
**Disperse** **Trigger** The picture-in-clouds takes damage from a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300); **Effect** The picture-inclouds disperses. Until the end of the current turn, they can't be attacked or targeted, they don't take up space, and any auras or emanations they have are suppressed. At the end of the turn, the picture-in-clouds re-forms in any sufficient space within 150 feet of where they dispersed; any auras or emanations they have are restored as long as the duration didn't run out while the elemental was dispersed. --- **Speed** fly 100 feet; swiftness **Melee** gust +26 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+10 bludgeoning plus [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=25) 15 feet **Ranged** lightning lash +26 ([range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d12 electricity **Cloudgaze** The picture-in-clouds shifts into their choice of an eagle shape, elephant shape, or sword shape. **Elephant Blast** **Requirements** The picture-in-clouds is in elephant shape; **Effect** The picture-in-clouds breathes out a 30-foot cone of air from their cloudy trunk. Creatures in the area must attempt a DC 30 Fortitude save to stand their ground. A creature [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) into a solid object stops moving and takes 4d10 bludgeoning damage.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is pushed 20 feet.
**Failure** The creature is pushed 40 feet.
**Critical Failure** The creature is pushed 40 feet and knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). **Feather Storm** ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) **Requirements** The picture-in-clouds is in eagle shape; **Effect** The picture-in-clouds [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) 125 feet, flapping their wings and creating a barrier along their path. This barrier has the effects of [_wall of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=367) (DC 30) and lasts until the end of the picture-in-clouds's next turn. **Slicing Wind** **Requirements** The picture-in-clouds is in sword shape; **Effect** The picture-in-clouds spins, forming a whirlwind that deals 5d8 slashing damage in a 15-foot emanation (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). **Swiftness** The picture-in-clouds's movement doesn't trigger reactions.
name: Picture-in-Cloud

Exalted jaathoom shuyookhs are ostentatious purveyors of dreams and nightmares. They can remain invisible like any jaathoom, but prefer to make themselves visible to better communicate and express their individuality through bright fabrics, expensive jewels, and flashy displays of excess. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Jaathoom Shuyookh](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2620) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 84 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111); (can't speak any language), cloud of visions **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +21, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +20, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +19, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +17, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +22, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +22, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +17 **Str** +5 **Dex** +7 **Con** +3 **Int** +5 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +6 **Cloud of Visions** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 120 feet. A jaathoom has telepathy 60 feet but can only show images rather than speaking. **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) jambiya_ (functions as a [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)) --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +21 **Will** +19 **HP** 150 **Turbulent Skies** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206)) 20 feet. Swirling winds make all squares in the emanation difficult terrain for Striding and [Flying](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) creatures. Creatures with the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5) trait are immune. The jaathoom can activate or deactivate this aura as a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.
**Naturally Invisible** The jaathoom is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) at all times. They can become visible or return to invisibility as a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 75 feet **Melee** _jambiya_ +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d4+11 slashing plus 1d12 electricity **Melee** fist +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d6+11 slashing plus 1d12 electricity **Ranged** crashing wind +23 ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [range increment 50 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d8+8 bludgeoning **Ranged** _jambiya_ +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d4+11 piercing plus 1d12 electricity **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 30 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) - **4th** [Ill Omen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1566), [Vapor Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1738) - **5th** [Illusory Creature](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567), [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=1569), [Nightmare](/Spells.aspx?ID=1615) (×2), [Pressure Zone](/Spells.aspx?ID=1318), [Sleep](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675) (×2) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (at will; to [Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=8); Elemental Planes; or [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) only) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The shuyookh transforms into a Small or Medium air elemental or aerial animal, such as an owl. This doesn't affect the shuyookh's statistics, but it could change the damage type of their Strikes. **Hurricane Blast** ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The jaathoom moves all creatures without the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5) trait in their turbulent skies aura 20 feet directly away, clockwise, or counterclockwise. A creature avoids being moved if it succeeds at a DC 29 Fortitude save. **Impressive Wish** ([Downtime](/Traits.aspx?ID=580)) **Frequency** three times per year; **Effect** The shuyookh conducts a _wish_ ritual for the benefit of a mortal, requiring no cost or secondary casters. The shuyookh's result is a success if they succeed at a DC 5 flat check or a failure if not. The shuyookh's visions of the subject's future inform how it interprets the wish. **Ominous Dreams** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Prediction](/Traits.aspx?ID=674)) The jaathoom sends a prophetic dream into the mind of a sleeping creature within 60 feet. An unwilling creature can avoid the vision if it succeeds at a DC 32 Will save. The jaathoom chooses the dream's subject but not its exact events. The target sees a brief vision of its future related to that subject, with the effect of [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=15). If the result is bad or mixed, the creature is [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) and doesn't start to recover from being frightened until it wakes.
Lady Dariya's Dazzling Emporium The reclusive jaathoom gem dealer and silversmith Lady Dariya crafts enchanted jewelry out of rare and unusual planar materials. She operates Lady Dariya's Dazzling Emporium in Shadow Absalom. She's also on rocky footing with Armun Kelisk and rumored to be in exile from the [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=4) now that [Ranginori](/Deities.aspx?ID=106) has returned.
name: Jaathoom Shuyookh

Some dangers lurking within the Endless Sky can doom prey before they even realize it; such are the ambush tactics of the 20-foot-tall elementals known as veldenars. Their inherent invisibility and pale, almost translucent forms make them among the most deadly creatures to call the [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=4) home. Veldenars resemble amphibians from the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1), albeit with myriad red eyes and an affinity for cold. Hunting along the edges of eternal storms, the event horizons of dead zones, and the banks of sky streams, veldenars prey on the lesser denizens of their home plane. They can often be found using their natural buoyancy to drift among the large chunks of magical ice that crossed onto the Plane of Air from its former border with the [Plane of Water](/Planes.aspx?ID=7). There, they extend their tongues to trap unwary flying insects or adventurers who might happen to float by. Their multiple eyes keep watch for their next meal even while devouring current prey. The intense cold of a veldenar's stomach rapidly incapacitates its victims, allowing the predator to focus on returning to invisibility and finding a secluded spot to digest. Victims who escape the veldenar's stomach often feel chilled to the bone for days. Veldenars aren't discerning about who or what they inhale. As a result, kinds of scrap can collect in their stomachs, especially inorganic material. Those hunting veldenars often wait until the elemental needs to regurgitate debris, as this is one of the few times a veldenar can be caught in a compromising position, unable to capture anything due to their clogged esophagus. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Veldenar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2621) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 85 **Perception** +24; [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=1), [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +20, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +23 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +5 **Int** -2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +21 **Will** +18 **HP** 190 **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) **Resistances** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 10 **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10 **Natural Invisibility** A veldenar is naturally [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) and only becomes visible when using a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300). Creatures it has engulfed remain visible within it, albeit slightly blurred by the veldenar's translucent skin.
**Viscous Breath** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206)) 15 feet. The veldenar raises the viscosity of the surrounding air just by breathing it. Creatures who enter or start their turn within the emanation treat the area as difficult terrain and take a –2 status penalty to any non-magical physical attacks they attempt. This aura is suppressed whenever the veldenar has a creature engulfed.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet **Melee** tongue +25 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+10 bludgeoning plus 1d10 cold **Vacuum** ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527)) **Requirements** The veldenar doesn't have a creature engulfed; **Effect** The veldenar forcefully unlocks then expands its jaws to inhale the air in a 15-foot cone before it, pulling Large or smaller creatures into its cavernous maw. The veldenar [Engulfs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=14) the creatures in the area (DC 27, 1d10+10 cold, [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) DC 27, Rupture 25).
Paired Predators Veldenars bond in partnership for life, which can be well over a century. Utilizing their ambush pack tactics, one veldenar will often drive potential prey into their partner's clutches. Visitors to the [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=4) should keep in mind that if they happen to spot one veldenar, another is likely close by.
name: Veldenar

Elementals of the [Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=5) can be fiercely territorial. Some more solitary elementals protect their lairs on their own, but occasionally others band together to defend larger regions that serve as shared turf. Notable among these alliances are avalanche legions, who patrol the perimeter of these shared territories, driving off intruders who dare to trespass upon their home ground. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Avalanche Legion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2622) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 102 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet **Languages** [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +23 **Str** +5 **Dex** +0 **Con** +4 **Int** -2 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +0 **Earthbound** When not touching solid ground, the avalanche legion is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35). --- **AC** 31 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +20 **Will** +21 **HP** 240 (16 squares) **Immunities** bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 10 **[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 30 feet, burrow 25 feet; earth glide, [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Earth Glide** The avalanche legion can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=93) through any earthen matter, including rock. When it does so, the legion moves at its full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of its passing. **Pummeling Boulders** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The avalanche legion unleashes an onslaught of blows against each enemy in a 5-foot emanation (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
2d8 bludgeoning damage
3d8+8 bludgeoning damage
4d8+11 bludgeoning damage **Spinning Stones** The avalanche legion spins in place, kicking up a barrage of stones. Each creature in a 10-foot burst within 30 feet of the troop takes 1d12+8 bludgeoning damage (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). When the troop is reduced to 8 or fewer squares, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Trample into the Earth** The avalanche legion speeds forward, running over creatures with their stone bodies and knocking them down. As [Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39); Gargantuan or smaller, 2d8 bludgeoning damage, DC 28. A creature that critically fails its save is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).
Familial Instincts Although elementals don't reproduce like typical creatures, [living landslide](/Monsters.aspx?ID=183) sometimes develop bonds with smaller or weaker earth elementals, such as [earth wisps](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1143), [living boulders](/Monsters.aspx?ID=650), and [sod hounds](/Monsters.aspx?ID=182). When several of these smaller elementals are gathered in one place, living landslides who cared for the creatures often continue to watch over their wards. Avalanche legions sometimes form from these gatherings, as multiple living landslides join forces to protect the smaller elementals.
name: Avalanche Legion

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Cullitox](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2623)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Cullitoxes dwell within deep caverns of the [Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=5), laden with crystals that glow under their own internal energy. A cullitox's hunger for gems will lead it to travel almost anywhere, even through planar tears. Occasionally, they even establish colonies on planes other than their own, should their newfound territory prove filled with crystals.

 A cullitox's scorpion-like body glitters with crystal. As light refracts between the facets, they concentrate and absorb it, energizing the cullitox and, some say, fueling the creature's magic.

 cullitoxes form small communities in locations where crystals are plentiful, working alongside one another to track down anything from a common quartz to precious rubies and sapphires. Scouting parties use their innate magic to communicate with each other via light and wordless sound, as well as to seek out more crystals. However, when precious stone resources grow scarce, cullitoxes become independent and territorial about their supply, taking crystals from wherever they can, even seizing them from other creatures.

 A cullitox's drive to gather crystals is akin to the drive to reproduce. As a cullitox gathers stones, it stores them within its body. Once the cullitox has enough stored material, the parent fuses its stores into a tiny replica of itself and extrudes the infant cullitox, sometimes referred to as a shardling, into the world. The process leaves the parent weakened for a short time, until it can gather enough crystal to repair its body and begin the process anew. Infant cullitoxes consume crystals in a similar way. They process the raw material over a few months to grow to adult size. Thereafter, a cullitox “eats” stone only to heal injuries.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

</column>

</row>

<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=57" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 103

**Perception** +9; crystal scent ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet, [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +9

<row gap="medium">

**Dex** +2

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Crystal Scent** A cullitox can sense crystals or gems within 60 feet as if using the [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) ability.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 18 

**Fort** +8 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +9 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 45

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, burrow 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
crystal stinger +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 1d6+5 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail spike +10 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 1d6+5 piercing and sink into stone

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 17
- **Cantrips (2nd)**
[Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528) (chiming; clinking; or rattling sounds only), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715)

**Rock Stride** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) The cullitox phases into adjacent rock that is large enough to accommodate it. Then, the cullitox senses similar or larger rocks within 60 feet and emerges from one. The cullitox can't use this ability again for 1 minute.

**Sink into Stone**   The spikes fired from a cullitox's tail phase into stone, pinning enemies in place. Enemies standing on or adjacent to a stone surface who are struck by a critical hit on a tail spike Strike are [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24). The DC to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) is 17.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Internal Gems</title>

When a cullitox dies, its body breaks into fragments of crystal. Some of these pieces are valuable, but potentially more lucrative are the gems the cullitox has stored inside its body to produce offspring. It takes semiprecious and precious stones worth 500 gp to produce an infant cullitox, though most slain cullitoxes have only a fraction of this amount stored. Once the stones become a new cullitox, they transform into the crystal of the infant's body, no longer the treasured material they once were.
</aside>
name: Cullitox

Gluttonous geodes form when natural forces crack open a geode that's steeped in elemental magic, causing a natural break that acts like a hinged jaw. Though seemingly unimportant and no larger than a common turtle, gluttonous geodes are bellicose and dogged in their pursuit of an enemy.

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Each long, thin leg of a crystal strider is a single enormous crystal, typically quartz or selenite. The strider uses this panoply of legs and a fluctuating physical structure to rapidly move about crystalline caverns, unimpeded by terrain.

The crystal strider can't Release Light again for 1d4 rounds.

markdown:
An outcrop of dense stone that spends ages eroding can gain sapience and slowly awaken with the wisdom of nature. Such elemental beings are slow to action and difficult to anger, spurred only by immediate threats. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Elder Outcrop](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2626) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 105 **Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) 120 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +29, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +25, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +26, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +26 **Str** +8 **Dex** -2 **Con** +8 **Int** +1 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +29 **Ref** +19 **Will** +26 **HP** 295 **Immunities** bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Dust Eternal** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55)) 30 feet. Dust swirls about the outcrop, rendering everything within its aura (including the outcrop) [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4). In addition, the dust at the outcrop's base creates [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) on the ground within the aura. The outcrop can activate or deactivate this aura using a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.
**Unstoppable** The elder outcrop's [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) condition can't exceed slowed 1, and it ignores penalties to its Speeds and the [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) condition. --- **Speed** 30 feet, burrow 30 feet; earth glide **Melee** column +27 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+14 bludgeoning plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=20) **Ranged** rock +27 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d6+14 bludgeoning plus call of earth **Call of Earth** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) A flying creature hit by the elder outcrop's rock ranged strike is affected by a 7th-rank [_earthbind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=94) spell (DC 33). **Earth Glide** The elder outcrop can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=93) through any earthen matter, including rock. When it does so, the elder outcrop moves at its full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of its passing. **Natural Formation** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) Until the next time it acts, the elder outcrop appears to be a natural terrain feature. It has an automatic result of 47 on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks and DCs to pass as a natural outcropping of rock. The outcrop's dust eternal aura is deactivated while Natural Formation is being used. **Stone Grip** ([Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) Great fingers of stone arise to grasp up to two Medium or smaller creatures on the ground in the outcrop's aura. Each target must succeed at a DC 33 Reflex save or be [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the stone hand (or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) on a critical failure; [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) DC 33). A creature grabbed or restrained by a stone hand at the end of its turn becomes [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) or increases its existing slowed condition by 1. When a creature is unable to act due to the slowed condition from this effect, the creature is permanently [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30). **[Throw Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=38)**
Wisdom of Stone Elder outcrops learn a lesson for every grain of rock eroded away, every squall and freeze that has shaped their craggy bodies. As wizened advisors and tutors, they have the respect and affection of other elementals, as well as from natural creatures. [Druids](/Classes.aspx?ID=6) of the [stone order](/DruidicOrders.aspx?ID=6) revere the wisdom of elder outcrops, always happy to receive their wide perspective, literal and philosophical.
name: Elder Outcrop
