<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Jabali Shuyookh](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2627)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A jabali genie of great power and high social rank is called a shuyookh. Strong in body and mind, jabali shuyookhs value physical skill, fine artistry, and displays of strength. Incredibly proud, they're easily offended and quick to anger but lash out only after weighing the consequences. Shuyookhs view their desires as greater than those around them, leading them to provide aid as quickly and efficiently as possible, fulfilling the terms of bargains expediently and to the letter.

 Jabali shuyookhs are industrious and have many interests, including athletic competitions, engineering, jewelry-making, masonry, metalworking, and sculpting. They enjoy games of chance and bargaining, whether in a gambling hall, arena, or marketplace. Some mix their interests by managing organizations or guilds involving such concerns. Many shuyookhs reap the wealth of the [Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=5) for sale elsewhere and speculate on the value of such commodities.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 35

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Genie_Jabali.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 12">[Jabali Shuyookh](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2627)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=57" />
<trait label="Genie" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=216" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 106

**Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +21, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +22, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +19, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +21

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +7

**Dex** +1

**Con** +5

**Int** +5

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Items**
_+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [falchion](/Weapons.aspx?ID=18)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 33 

**Fort** +24 

**Ref** +20 

**Will** +22 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 210

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, burrow 45 feet, climb 20 feet; earth glide

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_falchion_ +28 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)),
**Damage** 2d10+14 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d6+13 bludgeoning plus [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47) 15 feet and stone clutch

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
rock +27 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 2d8+13 bludgeoning

**Arcane Innate Spells** DC 32
- **Cantrips (6th)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485)
- **4th**
[Shape Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=1667) (at will)
- **5th**
[Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568), [Wall of Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=1751)
- **6th**
[Petrify](/Spells.aspx?ID=1628), [Mountain Resilience](/Spells.aspx?ID=1610) (at will; self only)
- **7th**
[Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (to [Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=8); Elemental Planes; or the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) only)
- **8th**
[Earthquake](/Spells.aspx?ID=1507)
- **Constant (5th)**
[Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The jabali transforms into a Small or Medium earth elemental or land animal, typically a camel or dog. This doesn't affect the shuyookh's statistics, but it could change the damage type of their Strikes.

**Earth Glide**   The jabali can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=93) through dirt and stone at their full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of their passing.

**Earthen Control**   When the shuyookh casts [_wall of stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=365), the edges can pass through creatures, which are shunted to the side of the wall opposite that edge.

**Economical Wish**  ([Downtime](/Traits.aspx?ID=580)) **Frequency** three times per year; **Effect** The shuyookh conducts a _wish_ ritual for the benefit of a mortal, requiring no cost or secondary casters. The shuyookh's result is a success if they succeed at a DC 5 flat check or a failure if not. The shuyookh fulfills the wish in the most straightforward, simple way possible.

**Stone Clutch**  ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585)) When the jabali [Pushes](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47) a creature into a stone barrier, the surface grips it with fingers of stone. The target must succeed at a DC 30 Reflex save or become [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the surface ([Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) DC 36).

**Stone Crush** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585)) The jabali deals 2d6+13 bludgeoning damage to all creatures within 100 feet that are [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by stone clutch, with a DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Industry Titans</title>

The mercantile empires of jabali shuyookhs extend across the Plane of Earth and onto other planes. Jabalis especially like to trade in regions of the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) and across the [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=6). Many customers value gemstones, metals, and other precious materials mined on the Plane of Earth. Thus, the resources of the Plane of Earth feed industry across the planes.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-50" />
name: Jabali Shuyookh

markdown:
Civilizations die, buried by time, but their memory may yet linger in shattered pottery, collapsed edifices, and destroyed artwork, compressed and clarified under the weight of rock and soil. Some excavated sites might still hold a sliver of consciousness in their base materials, which walks again as an avatar of an extinct people when awakened. These lithic loci represent the triumphs and folly of their society. Speaking to one is speaking to a witness of an archaic time. The lithic locus's persona expresses their culture in all dimensions, and thus the attitudes of lithic loci vary greatly. Their appearances show the aesthetics and technology of their time, suspended in a cloud of excavated earth. As plentiful as archaeological sites are in the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1), the [Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=5) holds countless more of far older provenance. Some of its inhabitants have even found lithic loci from peoples far older than themselves, seeing directly what would remain should they dwindle out of living memory. This has led some civilizations to even plan their decline by leaving a legacy in the living rock. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 37 **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 37 **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Lithic Locus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2628) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 107 **Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet **Languages** [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109); plus one ancient language **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +26, [Civilization Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +29, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +25 **Str** +6 **Dex** +0 **Con** +6 **Int** +7 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +2 **Antiques** A lithic locus contains at least one magic item. Any such antiques are durable, permanent items that were part of the locus's site and typify their culture. The locus receives the benefits of such an item as if wearing or holding it and can activate it. A lithic locus typically has an item of their level. --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +21 **Will** +27 **HP** 260 **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** ancient stone +28 ([Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d8+16 bludgeoning plus bury **Occult Innate Spells** DC 34 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482) - **3rd** [One with Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=1619) **Bury** Any creature hit by the locus's ancient stone Strike is partially buried in a tide of earth and rock, becoming immobilized (Escape DC 34). **Echo the Past** The locus calls forth a remnant of the past civilization to impart a lesson or control the present. Using this ability again ends any previous manifestation. The locus casts one of the following occult innate spells (DC 34) for the listed purpose.
[_**Dominate**_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87) The dominated creature takes on the role of a historical figure or someone in the social hierarchy (such as high priest) from the locus's civilization.
[_**Illusory Scene**_](/Spells.aspx?ID=161) The scene reenacts a historical event from the locus's civilization.
[_**Invoke Spirits**_](/Spells.aspx?ID=937) The spirits are drawn from the locus's memories of people from their civilization.
[_**Wall of Stone**_](/Spells.aspx?ID=365) The locus recreates the wall of a building from their civilization, complete with decorations.
Ancient Finds Lithic loci often contain important and valuable items. None of these elemental constructs are likely to agree to be looted, but some loci bestow their antiquities upon worthy successors, proud to see their culture's creations put to effective use in the modern day. Lithic loci can also direct earnest seekers to other lost treasures, helping to restore knowledge of an extinct society.
name: Lithic Locus

The vault keepers lost [Sairazul's](/Deities.aspx?ID=107) spark and have life spans of two to three millennia. They maintain and protect the xiomorns' many experiments. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Vault Keeper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2629) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 108 **Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 120 feet **Languages** [Orvian](/Languages.aspx?ID=120), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 300 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +26, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +26, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +26, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +26, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +24, [Planar Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +28, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +24 **Str** +4 **Dex** +8 **Con** +6 **Int** +8 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Craft Crystal Wand** ([exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=66)) **Frequency** twice per day; **Effect** The vault keeper spends 10 minutes creating a magic wand out of radioactive green crystal, containing any 5th-rank or lower [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55) spell of their choice. The wand can be used by other creatures, but it crumbles to sand at the end of the day and has no monetary value.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Brass Bastion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2631)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Brass bastions are formidable sentinels crafted from elemental fire and extraplanar brass. While they can be shaped into many forms, most depict muscular warriors with curving horns and stern or taunting expressions. Within each brass shell is a raging inferno—the source of its ceaseless heat. Though ifrits originally created brass bastions to act as servants and guards, the knowledge of how to craft them has spread throughout the [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=6). They can even be found on the [Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=5) thanks to interplanar trade. In Medina Mudii'a, brass bastions walk the streets, tend shops, and stand guard. Bespoke brass bastions are passed down as family heirlooms or gifted to business partners and allies. Scions of the wealthiest ifrit lineages never travel without a train of brass bastions clad in family livery. Destroying a rival ifrit's brass bastion is a quick way to erode their political status, though anyone caught performing such sabotage faces swift retribution.

 Most brass bastions prominently display the sigil of their owner or maker, though magical methods can also determine who crafted a specific construct. Brass bastions forged by favored artisans are a status symbol in many ifrit communities. Ifrits consider a brass bastion crafted by a non-ifrit to be inherently inferior, barely worth the cost of its materials. Brass bastions obey magical programming imprinted at the time of their creation or follow commands from their owners. Most can't speak, but a few have magical voices and can parrot simple phrases, recite poetry, or tell bedtime stories at the crafter's fancy.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 37

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32
</column>

</column>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Languages**

**Skills**

<row gap="medium">

**Dex** +0

**Int** -5

**Wis** +0

**Cha** -5
</row>

**Items**
_+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [falchion](/Weapons.aspx?ID=18)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 36 

**Fort** +27 

**Ref** +22 

**Will** +20 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 205

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), poison, [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)

**Resistances**
physical 15 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374)), spells 15 (except [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165))

**Molten Demise** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) When a brass bastion is destroyed, its body explodes in a flurry of elemental flame and superheated brass, dealing 4d6 piercing damage and 4d6 fire damage to creatures in a 20-foot emanation (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_falchion_ +28 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)),
**Damage** 2d10+12 slashing plus 1d10 fire

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d6+12 bludgeoning plus 1d10 fire

**Breathe Smoke** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) The brass bastion exhales superheated smoke and cinders in a 10-foot radius centered on a corner of its space. The smoke persists for 1 round. Any creature in the area (or that later enters the area) takes 15d6 fire damage (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save); on a critical failure, the creature catches fire, taking 2d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) as well. All creatures in the smoke become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4), and all creatures outside the smoke become concealed to creatures within it. The brass bastion can't Breathe Smoke again for 1d4 rounds.

**Heat Weapon**   Metal weapons wielded by a brass bastion superheat, dealing 1d10 additional fire damage (included in its statistics).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Brass Scraps</title>

On the [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=6), destroyed brass bastions are sold as scrap to enterprising ifrits, who reforge the remains into new brass bastions. Many ifrits pay more for the remains of a brass bastion crafted by their rivals yet react violently when offered brass bastion remains they originally created. Thus, identifying a brass bastion's original crafter is a valued skill. Few non-ifrits dare trade in brass bastion scraps, save in extralegal markets.
</aside>
name: Brass Bastion

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Brochmaw](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2632)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

With bodies of fired clay, brochmaws look like walking cylindrical ovens split across the middle by their titular maws, which rage with the fires of culinary perfection. Behind their maw burns a powerful internal oven, where the elemental creatures roast their prey.

 Brochmaws are driven solely by their voracious appetites, only leaving their lairs to acquire new meals. Early attempts to force brochmaws into service resulted in their would-be captors being reduced to nothing more than ash on a plate, accompanied by a written review of how they tasted. Now, brochmaws are employed all across the Planes, lured by the sweet promise of new, fresh meat.

 Though brochmaws have no use for money, they do trade gifts for services. The only thing they have to offer is cooked meat from their own mouths, which they deem extremely valuable. Surprisingly, all who have tried brochmaw meat claim it to be a savory roasted delight. Given that brochmaws offer nothing but cooked meat and desire nothing but raw meat, trading with them is a simple if slightly dangerous affair, often coming down to the quantity and rarity of the meat offered.

 Brochmaws hate to see meat go to waste. If one discovers a carcass of any sort, they call others of their kind to join them in a festive cookout, with dancing and feasting inevitably accompanied by compliments or complaints about the quality of the food.

bleed, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145)

**Weaknesses**
[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 15

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
skewer +27 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 3d10+8 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27)

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hot oil +25 ([range 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 3d6+7 fire damage plus 1d8 persistent fire damage plus 3 fire splash damage and marinade

**Marinade**   A creature taking [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) from the brochmaw's hot oil Strike is more readily cooked, taking a –2 circumstance penalty to Fortitude saves against Roast for as long as it is taking persistent fire damage.

**Roast** <actions string="Single Action" />  The brochmaw turns a skewer over their oven, cooking anything impaled on it. Creatures grabbed by the brochmaw's skewer take 3d6 fire damage (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). Creatures who have been Roasted take a –2 circumstance penalty to Fortitude saves against Serve for 1 minute.

**Serve** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** The brochmaw has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20); **Effect** The brochmaw eats their hard-earned meal off one of their skewers. The brochmaw bites down on one creature it has grabbed, dealing 12d6 piercing damage (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save); the brochmaw regains Hit Points equal to half the damage dealt. The creature is then freed from the skewer.

**Skewer Master**   The skewers of a brochmaw are more than long enough to hold multiple creatures. A brochmaw can have up to three creatures [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) on its skewer, and it can still make Strikes with its skewer even if it has creatures grabbed, though it can't target creatures it has grabbed.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Planar Delicacies</title>

Though brochmaws love to devour new things, they still have favorite meals they like to keep on hand. Brochmaws love amphibian meat, though the origin of this appeal is a mystery. They will eagerly consume [geniekin](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=145) of all kinds because the magical blood produces a psychedelic effect. Brochmaws think [celestials](/Languages.aspx?ID=16) are as dangerous as they are delicious, requiring a precise cooking process to avoid killing the consumer. Most brochmaws are far too impatient to prepare celestial meat properly, often to disastrous results.
</aside>
name: Brochmaw

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Lava Otter](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2633)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These fiery otters frolic and hunt in the lava streams of the [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=6). They're known to be highly territorial, instigating fights with rival otter families to compete for territory. Cautiously curious of strangers, entire packs have been known to descend upon anyone who attacks or event accidentally threatens a family member.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/ElementalFire_LavaOtter.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Lava Otter](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2633)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=57" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 129

**Perception** +7; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +7

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +4

**Con** +1

**Int** -4

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Below the Crust** A lava otter can [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63) at its full Speed in lava.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 15 

**Fort** +6 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +5 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 22

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, fire, paralyzed, poison, sleep

**Weaknesses**
cold 3

**Molten Form** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) Any creature that hits the lava otter with an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) Strike or otherwise touches it takes 1 fire damage. If a gallon or more of water touches the lava otter, or if it's affected by a [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) effect, its outer layer of lava hardens to a rocky shell, deactivating its molten form and imposing weakness 5 to bludgeoning damage. Molten form reactivates if the otter swims in lava for 1 minute.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, swim 40 feet (in lava only)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +9 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d4+1 piercing plus 1d4 fire

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d4+1 slashing

**Scattering Magma** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) **Requirements** The otter is in molten form, and its previous action was a successful jaws Strike; **Effect** The otter grips with its jaws and rapidly twists, flinging lava. The otter deals 1d4 fire damage to all creatures adjacent to it.

**Tight-Knit Family**   A lava otter can share the same space as another lava otter, but no more than two lava otters can occupy the same space.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Familiar Poaching</title>

Many greedy fire wizards wish to domesticate lava otters to act as their familiars, a sentiment loathed by most denizens of the [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=6). While the otters' burning pelts serve as a deterrence for would-be poachers, numerous naari (fire geniekin) still take it upon themselves to patrol lava otter habitats to protect them from poaching
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-43" />
name: Lava Otter

These enormous three-legged elementals resemble crows with flaming feathers, said to shine as bright as the sun. They nest in the smoldering Forest of Mulberry Coals, living in flocks of eight to a dozen, with ten being the most common. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Solar Crow](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2634) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 128 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), smoke vision **Languages** [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +22, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +21, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +19, [Sun Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +18, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +22 **Str** +2 **Dex** +6 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +5 **Smoke Vision** Smoke doesn't impair a solar crow's vision; they ignore the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from smoke. --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +22 **Will** +17 **HP** 170 **Immunities** bleed, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Glow** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100)) 25 feet. The solar crow casts [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 25-foot emanation (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for the next 25 feet).
**Glinting Wing** ([light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) **Trigger** A creature within 120 feet attempts to target the solar crow; **Effect** The solar crow defensively repositions a wing to shine light into the attacker's eyes. The target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for the duration of the triggering effect, granting the solar crow [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) against it. --- **Speed** 20 feet, fly 50 feet **Melee** beak +22 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 2d10+8 piercing plus 2d6 fire **Melee** talon +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 1d10+8 slashing plus 2d6 fire plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Blinding Heat** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The solar crow expands its wings and unleashes blazing hot, blinding light in a 120-foot emanation. Each creature in the area takes 9d6 fire damage with a DC 29 Reflex save. The crow can't use Blinding Heat again for 1d4 rounds, and its glow aura is deactivated during this time.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is blinded for 1 minute. **Burning Talons** A creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the solar crow takes 1d4 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). It can't recover from this damage while grabbed by the crow. **Snatch** A solar crow can [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) at half Speed while it has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) in its talons, carrying that creature along with it.
Story of Infamy Ancient tales from dragon-ruled Tian Xia tell of a flock of solar crows who wreaked havoc upon the Plane of Wood, driving an herb that could grant immortality to extinction before coming to terrorize Tian Xia itself. All but one of the crows were then shot down by a legendary archer.
name: Solar Crow

Living magmas can lie dormant for centuries below the earth before they awaken from their slumber in a fiery a volcanic eruption. This is especially dangerous when entire colonies of the molten elementals arise at the same time. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Living Magma](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2635) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 129 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +23, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +22 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +5 **Int** +4 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +20 **Will** +22 **HP** 250 **Immunities** bleed, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 15 **Molten Form** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) Any creature that hits the living magma with an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) Strike or otherwise touches it takes 10 fire damage. If a gallon or more of water touches the living magma, or if it's affected by a [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) effect, its outer layer of lava hardens to a rocky shell, deactivating its molten form and imposing weakness 15 to bludgeoning damage. Molten form reactivates if the living magma swims in lava for 1 minute.
**Volcanic Heat** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) 40 feet. Any creature that enters or starts its turn in the aura takes 15 fire damage (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). A creature that fails its save is also [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) until it's no longer in the aura.
**Trap Weapon** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) **Frequency** once per round; **Trigger** A creature hits the living magma with a melee weapon; **Effect** The living magma attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the triggering creature's Athletics DC. On a success, the living magma traps the weapon in its body and pulls it from the attacker's grasp. A creature can Interact to retrieve the weapon, but the attempt fails unless the creature succeeds at an Athletics check against the living magma's Fortitude DC (typically 36). If the living magma uses [Engulf](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=14), it also absorbs all trapped weapons, rendering them unreachable until it dies. --- **Speed** 40 feet, swim 60 feet (in lava only) **Melee** magma fist +27 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+11 bludgeoning plus 3d6 fire plus 2d4 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **[Engulf](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=14)** DC 33, 2d10 bludgeoning plus 4d6 fire, [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) DC 33, Rupture 25 **Launch Lava** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) The living magma hurls an exploding glob of lava up to 120 feet. Each creature in a 10-foot burst takes 7d6 fire damage (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). **Reignite** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) **Requirements** The living magma is not in molten form; **Effect** The living magma returns to molten form.
name: Living Magma

The leaders and luminaries of ifrits come from the wise but cruel shuyookhs. These spiteful manipulators lord their might over other genies and twist mortal greed into grief. Ifrit shuyookhs guide the Dominion of Flame with their vicious whims and unwavering belief in ifrit supremacy. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Ifrit Shuyookh](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2636) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 129 **Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +28, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +23, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +25, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +24, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +26, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +23 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +3 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +5 **Items** _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10)_, _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [scimitar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=38)_ --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +23 **Will** +27 **HP** 300 **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) **Weaknesses** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 15, [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) 15 **Heat of Blazing Wings** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) 5 feet, 4d6 fire damage (DC 31 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex)
**Exploit Regret** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101)) **Trigger** A creature fails an attack against the shuyookh; **Effect** The shuyookh asks the triggering creature if it wishes it had hit. If the creature indicates yes, the shuyookh takes damage as if the attack had hit and the expression of regret makes the shuyookh [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) for 1d4 rounds; they can use their extra action to [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94), Stride, or Strike. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 35 feet **Melee** _scimitar_ +31 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)), **Damage** 2d6+16 slashing plus 4d6 fire **Melee** fist +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d4+16 bludgeoning plus 4d6 fire **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 35, attack +27 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Illuminate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1359), [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565) - **4th** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (×2) - **5th** [Fire's Pathway](/Spells.aspx?ID=1354), [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530) (at will) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (at will; to [Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=8), Elemental Planes, or the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) only), [Volcanic Eruption](/Spells.aspx?ID=1746) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Burning Grasp** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) When the ifrit [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) or restrains a creature, that creature takes 4d6 fire damage, and takes 4d6 fire damage at the end of each of its turns until freed. **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The shuyookh transforms into a Small or Medium fire elemental or reptile, such as a snake. This doesn't affect the shuyookh's statistics, but it could change the damage type of their Strikes. **Combat Grab** **Requirements** The ifrit shuyookh has a hand free; **Effect** The shuyookh makes a melee Strike. If the Strike hits, the target is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) in the shuyookh's free hand. **Pedantic Wish** ([Downtime](/Traits.aspx?ID=580)) **Frequency** three times per year; **Effect** The shuyookh conducts a _wish_ ritual for the benefit of a mortal, requiring no cost or secondary casters. The shuyookh's result is a success if they succeed at a DC 5 flat check or a failure if not. The shuyookh subverts the wish-maker's true intentions, twisting the phrasing to maximize suffering
Hot Gossip Scion of the noble but disgraced Mishalq family, the ifrit information broker Qalkami Mishalq emerged from exile and returned to the Dominion of Flame following the death of Sultan Suleiman XXII. Qalkami used contacts in the Pathfinder Society to eliminate her rivals in the Cinderfury family and step into a power vacuum of her own creation, expanding her control and influence among the great nobility of Medina Mudii'a.
name: Ifrit Shuyookh

Typically members of the nobility, gatecrashers wear advanced power armor. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 19 **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Munsahir Gatecrasher](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2637) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 131 **Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +10, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +12, [Plane of Fire Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +10 **Str** +2 **Dex** +0 **Con** +4 **Int** +4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -1 **Items** gatecrasher armor --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +6 **Will** +11 **HP** 65 **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) **Weaknesses** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 5 **Heat of the Forge** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) 10 feet. An munsahir's skin radiates heat like a forge's fire. A creature that starts its turn in the area must succeed at a DC 19 Fortitude save or become [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) while it remains in the area. Creatures immune to [environmental heat effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) or with any fire resistance are immune.
**Self-Destruct** When the munsahir is reduced to 0 HP, their armor explodes at the start of their next turn, dealing 5d6 fire damage in a 10-foot radius (DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex). --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** hammer gauntlet +12 ([Free-Hand](/Traits.aspx?ID=613), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)), **Damage** 1d12+6 bludgeoning plus 1d6 fire **Ranged** flame jet +12 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d6 fire plus 1d6 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Blazing Rush** The gatecrasher Strides up to double their Speed in a straight line. They can pass through enemy spaces and make a hammer gauntlet Strike against each creature they move through. Blazing Rush can't be used again for 1d4 rounds. **Burning Touch** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The munsahir gatecrasher's Strikes deal an extra 1d6 fire damage (included above). When the gatecrasher successfully performs a [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) or [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) action, they also deal 1d6 fire damage to their target.
name: Munsahir Gatecrasher

Powerful deities and warlords strive for dominance over the [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=6) and the rest of the Inner Sphere in order to prove fire's supremacy. Armies of flaming soldiers go to war against each other on battlefields of smoke and smoldering char, all unable to bring their full power to bear. [Ymeri](/Deities.aspx?ID=108), the vicious and victorious Queen of the Inferno, commands legions of sootsoldiers: elementals capable of battling the armies of her foes and, even when destroyed, rising from the ashes of the Eternal Furnace, scorched and triumphant. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Sootsoldiers](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2639) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 133 **Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), smoke vision **Languages** [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +21, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +18, [Plane of Fire Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +18, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +18 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +0 **Smoke Vision** Sootsoldiers ignore the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from smoke. --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +21 **Will** +18 **HP** 165 (16 squares) **Immunities** bleed, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 10 **Ashen Smoke** When the sootsoldiers are reduced by an HP Threshold or are reduced to 0 HP, the destroyed soldiers crumble to a cloud of ash-laden smoke in 20-foot burst centered on the sootsoldiers. All creatures within the cloud are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4), and all creatures outside it are concealed to those inside. The smoke lasts for 1 minute or until dispersed by a strong wind.
**[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 40 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Incinerating Grasp** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The sootsoldiers reach to constrain each enemy in a 5-foot emanation in their fiery clutches (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The damage and additional effects depend on the number of actions.
2d8 fire damage
3d8+7 fire damage plus [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20)
4d8+7 fire damage plus grabbed **Seething Flash** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The sootsoldiers reignite and rush across the battlefield, Forming Up and Striding twice. At the end of this movement, a wave of flame and heat pours off the sootsoldiers, dealing 4d8 fire damage to other creatures in a 10-foot emanation, with a DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. A creature that critically fails its save is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).
The Radiant Host Sootsoldiers who serve the other Lord of Fire, the Lambent King [Atreia](/Deities.aspx?ID=101), are covered in glowing embers instead of black char. In place of ashen smoke's normal effects, any creature in the area must attempt a DC 29 Reflex save. On a failure, for 1 minute that creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) and its invisibility is negated.
name: Sootsoldiers

markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Asp Of Grief](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2640)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

On the [Plane of Metal](/Planes.aspx?ID=24), a soft whisper and gentle jingle often accompany the tempestuous winds, while what appear to be gold ribbons twist and writhe through the air. In actuality, these are asps of grief—intelligent, snakelike creatures drawn to strong emotions. The iron irises of their eyes allow them to see the ever-shifting magnetic fields of the Plane of Metal, which the asps can use to sail across the skies.

 Asps of grief prey on sorrow and heartache, and times of loss, mourning, and bereavement attract them. Wearing delicate iron bells to announce their arrivals at funerals, battlefields, and the traditional columbaria built for the plane's dead, asps of grief shake their heads, making their bells chime and alerting mourners to their presence. “Will you share your sorrow with me?” they ask, their gentle voices low and sibilant.

 If the bereaved agrees, the asps' tongues flick out, tasting the anguish of the living, sipping on sadness and fear, and providing a temporary reprieve from suffering. Though the asps are associated with death, most denizens of the Plane of Metal see them as good omens.

 Intensely curious creatures, asps of grief crave information about magic and the multiverse. They might attach themselves to adventuring parties or ask wanderers they meet for any obscure arcane knowledge they may have come across on their journeys. In exchange, the asps of grief offer to help these travelers work through any anguish or trauma they may be carrying— or at least ease their emotional pain for a time.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 27

**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 27

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/AspOfGrief.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 10">[Asp Of Grief](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2640)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=20" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=57" />
<trait label="Metal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=505" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 150

**Perception** +21; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), magnetic vision

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +21, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +21, [Psychology Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +19, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +19, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +21

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +7

**Con** +3

**Int** +7

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Magnetic Vision** An asp of grief can see magnetic fields, allowing it to detect large sources of magnetic metal (Bulk 1 or greater) as a [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) sense.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 31 

**Fort** +15 

**Ref** +21 

**Will** +21 

+1 status vs. [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 150

</row>

**Resistances**
physical 10 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374))

**Curtain of Calm** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 20 feet. The asp of grief consumes the emotions of the creatures around it. An enemy that begins its turn in the aura must attempt a DC 29 Will save. If it fails, the asp consumes its grief. The creature feels a lack of feelings and motivation, reducing its [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) value by 1 and becoming [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (or stupefied 2 on a critical failure) for 1d4 rounds. Regardless of the result of the saving throw, the creature is temporarily immune to curtain of calm for 1 minute. The asp becomes charged with grief (see iron grief) for 1 minute whenever a creature fails this saving throw.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, fly 50 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 2d12+10 piercing

**Arcane Innate Spells** DC 29
- **Cantrips (5th)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646)
- **3rd**
[Magnetic Acceleration](/Spells.aspx?ID=943) (at will; see iron grief)
- **5th**
[Magnetic Attraction](/Spells.aspx?ID=944), [Magnetic Repulsion](/Spells.aspx?ID=945)

**Iron Grief**   If the asp casts [_magnetic acceleration_](/Spells.aspx?ID=943) while charged with grief, it can either heighten the spell to 5th rank or target up to three creatures instead of one, rolling separately against each. After casting the spell, the asp is no longer charged with grief.

**Magnetic Traveler**   An asp can ride magnetic fields to travel long distances. In a location with strong magnetic fields (including the [Plane of Metal](/Planes.aspx?ID=24) and Golarion), its travel Speed is doubled if it's flying.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Calling the Asps</title>

In the [Plane of Metal](/Planes.aspx?ID=24), funeral rites often include placing the deceased upon tall towers surrounded by dishes of water. Chunks of sodium, a silvery-white alkali metal, dance across the surface of the water and catch fire, sending plumes of hydrogen gas into the air. As the asps of grief skate upon the magnetic fields, they can sense this announcement of death and sorrow and follow the trail back to the mourners.
</aside>
name: Asp Of Grief

markdown:
The [Plane of Metal](/Planes.aspx?ID=24) can seem unpredictable to the point of cruelty, and capritellixes embody this perfectly. With a head formed of discs that each depict a portion of their face, a capritellix reshapes their personality with a quick whir. Despite their different faces, each capritellix has a unified identity, with one memory and one name. Each mood that cycles through the pieces of their face is a massively amplified facet of their personality, rather than a completely different individual. The differences are subtle to most observers, but a capritellix can display numerous mood combinations—typically 64 for one with four faces per disc or 125 for one with five faces per disc. No matter how much they change their face, however, capritellixes can't suppress their haughty ego or shake the ceaseless despair they feel. The conflicting priorities of each mood within a capritellix make it difficult for any of these creatures to make long-term plans or form solid relationships. What one face builds up, another undermines, cursed all the while with full knowledge of the former intentions they will never fulfill and the well-laid plans they will abandon. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Capritellix](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2641) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 151 **Perception** +30; darkvision **Languages** [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112); plus one language for each of their mouths; [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +31, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +29, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +32, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +32, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +32, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +32, [Plane of Metal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +30, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +30 **Str** +6 **Dex** +8 **Con** +8 **Int** +7 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +9 --- **AC** 39 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=1) **Fort** +31 **Ref** +29 **Will** +30 **HP** 290 **Immunities** bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Resistances** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 10, physical 10 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374)) **Whirling Hands** **Trigger** A creature starts its turn in the capritellix's melee reach; **Effect** The capritellix makes a metal hand Strike against the creature. --- **Speed** fly 60 feet **Melee** metal hand +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+14 bludgeoning plus 1d12 electricity, plentiful metals, and [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47) 20 feet **Ranged** eye beam +33 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d12 electricity **Dual Beams** The capritellix makes two eye beam Strikes against different creatures. Their multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after both Strikes. **Plentiful Metals** Each of the capritellix's levitating hands is made of a different precious metal. Each time a capritellix makes a metal hand Strike, they choose whether the hand they use is [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374), [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375), [dawnsilver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=377), [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=378), [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379), or any other solid precious metal. **Shift Mood** The capritellix rotates the segments of their face, changing their personality and demeanor. Until they Shift their Mood again, the capritellix gains a +4 status bonus to a certain skill and to their Will DC against that skill, as well as a specific occult innate spell they can cast at will (9th rank, DC 38). **Angry** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7), [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87)
**Gregarious** [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12), [_uncontrollable dance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=347)
**Serene** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6), [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=315)
**Sly** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5), [_warp mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=369)
Recycled Remains When horribly damaged or weary, capritellixes travel to a communal necropolis, much like a fabled elephant graveyard. Thousands of metallic hands reach up from the landscape, clawing at the sky, and severed discs that used to make up capritellixes pile up in endless heaps. Other elementals sometimes salvage these disparate parts and assemble them into a new capritellix—a unique individual with a brand-new identity.
name: Capritellix

markdown:
A metal wisp is a roiling sphere of rust particles and sharp metal fragments, all furiously orbiting a liquid metal core that resembles the skull of a mortal creature—usually a humanoid, but animal and even more fantastical skulls are not uncommon. They don't form attachments as easily as wisps from other elemental planes, which tend to view metal wisps as too morose, although they retain a certain curiosity since their return from the planar severance. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 14 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 9 [Metal Wisp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2642) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 152 **Perception** +6; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), rust vision **Languages** [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +6, [Mining Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +4, [Plane of Metal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +4 **Str** +2 **Dex** +1 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Rust Vision** A metal wisp ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from rust clouds. --- **AC** 16 **Fort** +7 **Ref** +3 **Will** +6 **HP** 15 **Immunities** bleed, [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Resistances** electricity 2 **Resonance** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505)) 30 feet. All wisps vibrate at a frequency attuned to their element, resonating with and empowering all creatures and effects sharing that trait. Creatures in the area gain a +1 status bonus to attack and damage rolls made with metal weapons or effects with the [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505) trait; a creature with the [elemental](/Traits.aspx?ID=57) and metal traits gains this bonus to all attack and damage rolls.
**Accord Essence** **Trigger** An ally within 30 feet that benefited from the wisp's resonance in the last hour is targeted by an attack; **Effect** The wisp detonates itself in a small elemental explosion. Allies within 30 feet that have benefited from the wisp's resonance in the last hour gain temporary Hit Points equal to half the wisp's current Hit Points. These temporary Hit Points last 1 hour.
A wisp that uses this reaction is permanently destroyed, and it can be restored only by a _wish_ ritual or similarly powerful effect. If an ability would prevent the wisp's destruction (for instance, if the wisp is summoned and would merely be dismissed), Accord Essence has no effect.
**Rust Cloud** A metal wisp is constantly surrounded by a cloud of rust flakes that cause it to be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) from creatures more than 5 feet away from it. --- **Speed** fly 25 feet **Melee** tendril +6 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d4 piercing plus 1 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **In Concert** When a metal wisp rolls a critical failure on a check to [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=75), it gets a failure instead, and when it rolls a success, it gets a critical success instead.
name: Metal Wisp

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ferrous Butterfly](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2643)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Beautiful but deadly, the ferrous butterfly is a turkey-sized insectile creature bristling with dozens of gleaming, razor-edged blades. Considered harmless by most denizens of the [Plane of Metal](/Planes.aspx?ID=24), they nonetheless pose a significant threat to organic creatures, whose iron-rich blood they find irresistible.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Metal Scamp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2644)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The capricious and destructive metal scamps are self-appointed forces of entropy, taking great pleasure in indiscriminately wrecking everything around them, sometimes even to their own detriment. Metal scamps have a severe appearance, with pointed facial features and angular limbs, and their skin can be the color of any metal, often heavily oxidized as if by years of exposure to the elements.

<column gap="tiny">

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Mercurial](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2645)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Some of the most populous denizens of the [Plane of Metal](/Planes.aspx?ID=24), mercurials are weird creatures of liquid metal known for changing their features, chemical compositions, and temperaments with equal frequency. Excitable, curious, and friendly, mercurials are quick to offer assistance to extraplanar beings, but their volatile personalities can make them unreliable allies.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/ElementalMetal_Mercurial.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 2">[Mercurial](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2645)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=57" />
<trait label="Metal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=505" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 153

**Perception** +6; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +8, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +10, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +8, [Plane of Metal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +8

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +4

**Con** +3

**Int** +2

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Items**
[shuriken](/Weapons.aspx?ID=79) (5)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 17 

**Fort** +7 

**Ref** +11 

**Will** +8 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 30

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145)

**Resistances**
[electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 5

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d10+3 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shuriken +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 1d6+3 piercing

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The mercurial takes on the appearance of any Small or Medium humanoid. This transformation allows for significant detail and can reproduce the features of a specific individual, but the mercurial retains a shiny, liquid-metal appearance that renders the transformation unsuitable as a disguise unless they're impersonating another mercurial. It doesn't change the mercurial's Speed or the attack and damage bonuses of their Strikes, but it does allow them to transform their limbs into metal tools or melee weapons, potentially changing the damage type dealt by their Strikes.

**Metallurgic Adaptation** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The mercurial transmutes their liquid metal body into [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=272), copper, gold, iron, [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=277), or steel. Their unarmed melee Strikes are made of that material until they use Metallurgic Adaptation again.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Many Faces, Many Names</title>

As immortal beings with comparatively short attention spans, mercurials invariably become bored with their current identities sooner or later and seek a change by adopting a new face and persona. To avoid confusion among friends and acquaintances, a mercurial's name typically consists not only of their current moniker, but a list of the last few names used, presented in chronological order as far back as the mercurial can remember.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-343" />
name: Mercurial

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Kinzaruk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2646)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

In its natural state, the kinzaruk resembles a paper-thin, perfectly square sheet of lightweight silvery-white metal, approximately 15 feet across. It's almost never encountered this form, however, being far better known for its ability to intricately fold its body thousands of times in an instant to reconfigure itself into a varied assortment of deadly animal-like shapes.

<column gap="tiny">

([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 18

razor's edge +11 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d10+3 slashing

**Fold Form** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The kinzaruk swiftly unfolds and refolds its body into a new shape chosen from the options of [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=10). This grants it the movement Speeds and Strikes of the chosen form, but none of the other benefits. Its attack bonus is unchanged, and its damage bonus is +3. The kinzaruk can return to its natural form by taking this action again, and automatically returns if it falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38).

</column>

Looking much like an ooze, a quickiron plasm is a mass of the rust-red liquid skymetal known as [djezet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1410), prized in the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) for its magic-enhancing properties. Quickiron plasms feed on the residual energies left behind by large expenditures of magical power, which they can detect from miles away. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Quickiron Plasm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2647) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 154 **Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), magic scent **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +10 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +5 **Int** -4 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -2 **Magic Scent** The quickiron plasm can sense magical auras from up to 1 mile away as an [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) sense. --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +8 **Will** +10 **HP** 65 **Immunities** critical hits, bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Resistances** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 5 **Biomagical Feedback** The quickiron plasm's conductive properties are dangerous to those in physical contact with it. When a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by a quickiron plasm Casts a Spell, that creature takes 5 force damage per rank of the spell.
**Consume Magic** **Trigger** The quickiron plasm succeeds at a saving throw against a spell; **Effect** The quickiron plasm consumes energy from the spell, regaining 5 Hit Points per rank of the spell. --- **Speed** 15 feet **Melee** pseudopod +12 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d6+6 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 2d6 bludgeoning, DC 22
Djezet Extraction When a quickiron plasm dies, most of the [djezet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1410) making up its body becomes inert and useless. However, a skilled alchemist can sometimes extract trace amounts of the valuable skymetal from its remains. This is a 10-minute process that requires the [Alchemical Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=752) feat, a proficiency rank of expert or better in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4), and a successful DC 20 Crafting check. A successful attempt produces a single [_djezet dose_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1412) that remains potent for 1 hour before breaking down into a foul-smelling goo. The item has no value if sold due to its temporary nature.
name: Quickiron Plasm

These elementals resemble enormous beetles, with thick outer shells composed of multiple layers of heavily pockmarked, rusted plates protecting a jagged metallic skeleton. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Rust Scarab](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2648) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 155 **Perception** +9; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), rust vision **Languages** [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Plane of Metal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +11 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +5 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +2 **Heavy** As long as it is immobile, the elemental can't be forcibly moved or knocked prone. If it takes a move action, it loses this immunity until the start of its next turn.
**Rust Vision** A rust scarab ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from rust clouds. --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +9 **Will** +11 **HP** 65 **Immunities** bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Resistances** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 5, physical 5 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374)) **Crumbling Carapace** When a rust scarab is reduced to fewer than half its maximum Hit Points or is damaged by a critical hit, its outer shell breaks into a veil of rusty metal flakes. This causes it to lose its resistance to physical damage and its heavy ability, but it gains a rust cloud aura (as [metal wisp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2642)) and a 35-foot land Speed. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** claw +15, **Damage** 2d8+6 slashing plus tetanus **Tetanus** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) **Saving Throw** DC 19 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 week; **Stage 1** [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) (1 week); **Stage 2** clumsy 2 and can't speak (1 day); **Stage 3** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) with spasms (1 day); **Stage 4** death **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39)** Medium or smaller, claw, DC 23
Ancient Remnants No rust scarab specimens free of deterioration have been documented, leading to debate among scholars as to whether this state of corrosion is their natural condition, or whether the creatures are spectacularly ancient even by the standards of immortal elemental beings.
name: Rust Scarab

markdown:
This rotating sphere of dark, shiny metal floats about 5 feet off the ground, constantly surrounded by a whirling frenzy of smaller metallic objects caught up in its powerful magnetic field. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Living Lodestone](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2649) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 155 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +15 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +13 **Will** +16 **HP** 95 **Immunities** bleed, paralyzed, poison, sleep **Resistances** electricity 5 **Magnetic Field** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 60 feet. A living lodestone constantly emits a powerful magnetic field that is either positively or negatively aligned. Each creature within the aura that is wielding a metallic weapon, wearing metallic armor, or made partially or entirely out of metal is subject to an effect determined by the lodestone's current polarity.
  • **Negative** An affected creature is pushed 5 feet away from the lodestone at the start of each of its turns, and it treats each square in the aura as difficult terrain when moving closer to the lodestone. Unattended metal objects in the aura of 2 Bulk or less are pushed just outside the aura.
  • **Positive** An affected creature is pulled 5 feet toward the lodestone at the start of each of its turns, and it treats each square in the aura as difficult terrain when moving farther from the lodestone. Unattended metal objects in the aura of 2 Bulk or less are pulled adjacent to the lodestone.

**Electromagnetic Disruption** When living lodestone takes electricity damage, they automatically reverses polarity. --- **Speed** 20 feet; hover **Melee** jolt +15, **Damage** 2d6+8 electricity **Ranged** hurled metal object +13 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d10+7 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing (depending on object) **Hover** A living lodestone floats above the ground high enough to ignore all [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) and greater difficult terrain on the ground. **Magnetic Disarm** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The living lodestone attempts to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) a metal weapon from a creature within its magnetic field. On a critical success, the weapon is either pushed to just outside the aura if the polarity is negative or is pulled to the lodestone and sticks to it if the polarity is positive. An item stuck to the lodestone can be wrenched free with an Interact action. **Reverse Polarity** The living lodestone switches the polarity of its magnetic field from positive to negative, or vice versa. Each creature affected by the lodestone's aura falls prone unless it succeeds at a DC 21 Reflex save. The lodestone can't Reverse Polarity again for 1d4 rounds.
Lodestone Loot The cyclone of metallic objects constantly orbiting a living lodestone often contains items of value, ranging from simple coinage and bits of precious metal to long-lost weapons, jewelry, and sometimes even enchanted metal trinkets. The lodestone itself has no concept of the value of such things, and is just as likely to fling one of these valuable items at a foe as it is any other object.
name: Living Lodestone

A skymetal striker resembles a serpent—or, more precisely, the two-headed serpent known as an [amphisbaena](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1060)—made of a long string of dozens of spinning, sharp-toothed circular saw blades. These blades array themselves along the creature's body like beads on an invisible cord of magnetic force. Although the blades making up the creature are typically composed of many different metals and alloys, it is best known, feared, and named for the blades of [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=276) and [inubrix](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1415) that form the “heads” at either end of its serpentine body. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Skymetal Striker](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2650) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 156 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +17 **Str** +2 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +17 **Will** +13 **HP** 100 **Immunities** bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Resistances** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 5 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** inubrix fangs +17 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+6 slashing plus phase blade **Melee** orichalcum fangs +17 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+6 slashing plus temporal stutter **Phase Blade** A skymetal striker's inubrix fangs Strike ignores damage resistance from metal armor's armor specialization effects and the circumstance bonus to AC from metal shields, and it doesn't trigger the Shield Block reaction from a metal shield. **Temporal Stutter** A creature not already affected by temporal stutter that takes damage from a skymetal striker's orichalcum fangs Strike must attempt a DC 25 Fortitude save.
**Success** The target is [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) until the end of its next turn.
**Failure** The target is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) until the end of its next turn.
**Critical Failure** The target is slowed 2 until the end of its next turn. **Two-Headed Assault** The skymetal striker makes one inubrix fangs Strike and one orichalcum fangs Strike, each against a different creature. Its multiple attack penalty increases only after all the attacks are made.
Strange Metals [Inubrix](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1415) and [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=276) are types of skymetal—a collective term used in the Universe to refer to exceedingly rare metals, most possessing magical properties, found on distant planets and fallen stars. Skymetal can be found in abundance on the [Plane of Metal](/Planes.aspx?ID=24), however, and many metal elementals contain at least a bit of one type or another. Inubrix, colloquially called ghost iron, is prized for its ability to phase through other metals, while orichalcum's mystical properties can warp the very flow of time around it.
name: Skymetal Striker
