This bizarre entity is actually a massive collective consisting of tens of thousands of constituent parts, each a tiny metal sphere barely the size of a pebble. Controlled by a single elemental spirit, the swarm is capable of flowing like liquid through the smallest openings before shaping itself into a powerful combatant, a solid wall, or any other form circumstances might require. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Nanoshard Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2651) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 157 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +21, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +19, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +21 **Str** +6 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +21 **Will** +16 **HP** 120 **Immunities** bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145), [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=36) **Resistances** bludgeoning 5, electricity 10, piercing 10, slashing 10 **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 10 **Electromagnetic Dispersal** When a nanoshard swarm takes electricity damage, they automatically shift into swarm form. --- **Speed** none (barrier), 15 feet (battle), or 25 feet (swarm) **Melee** tendril +19 ([reach 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [versatile B](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d8+9 piercing **Barrier Form** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) **Requirements** The nanoshard swarm is in swarm form; **Effect** The nanoshard swarm forms a continuous 6-inch-thick solid wall, up to 60 feet long and 10 feet high, originating from any one square in its current space. The wall can follow any path, with each 5 feet being placed on the border between squares. It doesn't need to stand vertically, so it can form a bridge or set of stairs, for example. The wall must be formed in an unbroken open space where its edges don't pass through any creatures or objects.
A single 5-foot-by-5-foot section of the wall can be destroyed by dealing 18 points of damage to it, which also reduces the swarm's total Hit Points. A nanoshard swarm can't be knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) or [forcibly moved](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) while in barrier form, nor can it voluntarily move. A nanoshard swarm in barrier form can originate tendril attacks from any square of its wall. **Battle Form** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) **Requirements** The nanoshard swarm is in swarm form; **Effect** The nanoshard swarm coalesces into a Huge shape that looks like a humanoid or beast and can hold items. In battle form, its Speed is 15 feet, it's clumsy 1, and it has the following Strike.
**Melee** limb +19 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+9 bludgeoning **Swarm Form** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) The nanoshard swarm collapses into its natural form: a Huge sea of tiny metal spheres. It drops anything it's holding. While in swarm form, the nanoshard swarm's Speed is 25 feet, it can move through any area large enough for a single sphere to fit through without having to [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=31), and it gains Engulf.
[Engulf](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=14) DC 27, 2d8+6 bludgeoning, [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) DC 27, Rupture 18
Some Reassembly Required Nanoshard swarms are notoriously difficult to permanently dismantle. Should even a single constituent of a swarm manage to escape destruction, the minuscule elemental immediately seeks out the nearest source of raw metal and begins extracting material in order to create as many exact duplicates of itself as possible. Each subsequent duplicate then dutifully repeats the same behavior, doubling the number of constituents every few minutes, until the entire swarm has been fully reconstituted.
name: Nanoshard Swarm

A monstrous spider-like creature composed of the radioactive skymetal [abysium](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1405), this deadly predator contaminates anything it touches. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Abysium Horror](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2652) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 158 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +23, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +19 **Str** +7 **Dex** +3 **Con** +5 **Int** +3 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +19 **Will** +17 **HP** 215 **Immunities** bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Resistances** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 10 **Green Glow** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [radiation](/Traits.aspx?ID=421)) 20 feet. An abysium horror constantly emanates a powerful energy toxic to life. This radiation sheds dim light in the area. Any creature beginning its turn in the aura must attempt a DC 27 Fortitude save, becoming [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) on a failure or sickened 2 on a critical failure. Once out of the aura, an affected creature's sickened condition automatically decreases by 1 at the beginning of each of its turns.
**Heavy** As long as it is immobile, the elemental can't be [forcibly moved](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) or knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). If it takes a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) action, it loses this immunity until the start of its next turn. --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 20 feet **Melee** claw +23 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+10 slashing plus 2d4 poison **Ranged** radioactive shrapnel +23 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d8+7 piercing plus 2d4 poison **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39)** Large or smaller, claw, DC 27
name: Abysium Horror

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Oregorger](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2653)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Oregorgers are hulking, four-limbed brutes with bodies of raw primal metals accreted in layers. Driven entirely by an insatiable drive to consume metal, an oregorger stops at nothing to greedily devour as much metal as it can and expel it as caustic rust.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 28

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Oregorger.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 11">[Oregorger](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2653)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=57" />
<trait label="Metal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=505" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 158

**Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), rust vision

**Languages**
[Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +17, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +8

**Dex** +2

**Con** +7

**Int** -1

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Rust Vision** An oregorger ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from rust clouds.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 30 

**Fort** +24 

**Ref** +17 

**Will** +20 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 245

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145)

**Resistances**
[acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3) 10, [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 10

**Caustic Rust** ([acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206)) 5 feet. The oregorger continually leaks tiny fragments of partially digested rust into the air around it. Any creature that ends its turn in the aura takes 2d6 acid damage with a DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. A creature that critically fails is also [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, burrow 20 feet, fly 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
adamantine bite +23 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d12+12 piercing plus devour metal

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 2d6+12 slashing

**Devour Metal**   Any time the oregorger scores a critical hit with an adamantine bite attack, it deals the same amount of damage to any metal armor worn by the target, automatically bypassing any Hardness lower than 10. If a creature uses the [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) reaction with a metal shield against an oregorger's adamantine bite, the shield is automatically [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2), but no other item takes damage from that attack. Unattended metal items automatically take full damage from an oregorger's adamantine bite attack, ignoring their Hardness if it's lower than 10.

**Searing Spew** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524)) The oregorger belches forth a cloud of caustic, rusted debris from its maw, filling a cube adjacent to itself that's 10 feet on each side. Any creature in this area takes 6d6 acid damage and 6d6 slashing damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex). The ground under the cloud is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) for 1 hour, after which the shrapnel crumbles to dust. The oregorger can't use Searing Spew again for 1d4 rounds, but the ability recharges if the oregorger damages an item with devour metal.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-343" />
name: Oregorger

markdown:
Melomachs are massive, vaguely humanoid elementals composed of metal debris. They take great pride in decorating and augmenting their metallic bodies with haphazardly placed spikes, blades, humanoid or bestial skulls, and other similar adornments, the more ostentatious the better. When two melomachs meet, the resulting contests of physical might and vocal prowess can last for days, often leaving the landscape ravaged for miles in every direction. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Melomach](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2654) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 159 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +30, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +21, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +28, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +26 **Str** +9 **Dex** +4 **Con** +9 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +7 **Heavy** As long as it is immobile, the elemental can't be [forcibly moved](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) or knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). If it takes a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) action, it loses this immunity until the start of its next turn. --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +23 **Will** +23 **HP** 292 **Immunities** bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147) **Resistances** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 15 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 3d12+18 bludgeoning **Ranged** lightning +21 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d10+16 electricity **Lightning Punch** The melomach makes a fist Strike, then makes a lightning Strike against a different creature within 120 feet of the fist Strike's target. Both Strikes count toward the melomach's multiple attack penalty, but it doesn't increase until after both attacks are made. **Rhythmic Stomp** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The melomach's footsteps raise a clanging sound through their body to create a brief musical phrase. The melomach Strides, then generates the effect of one of the following bard composition cantrips at 7th rank: [_allegro_](/Spells.aspx?ID=380), [_dirge of doom_](/Spells.aspx?ID=382) (adds the [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) trait), or [_triple time_](/Spells.aspx?ID=392). **Scream** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) The melomach unleashes a guttural, ear-piercing wail. All creatures in a 120-foot cone take 7d12 sonic damage with a DC 32 Fortitude save. The melomach can't Scream again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage, is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36), and is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage, is stunned 3, and is deafened permanently.
Music of Mass Destruction A melomach's appetite for carnage is matched only by its love of loud noises, particularly the boom of explosions and the shriek of tearing metal. In addition to the sound of their own voices, melomachs are partial to the rapid, discordant strumming and powerful howls that typify the musical stylings typical of the [Plane of Metal](/Planes.aspx?ID=24). Boisterous performances often draw the attention of melomachs.
name: Melomach

markdown:
Zuhras, the genies of the elemental [Plane of Metal](/Planes.aspx?ID=24), are bold and imposing, fond of being the center of attention and drawing a crowd with flashy performances, displays of skill, and tales of their personal victories. Despite their large personalities, their long-term relationships tend to be cool and somewhat distant; zuhras often make alliances but rarely form friendships, and the trust required for deeper relationships is rarer still. The few mortals who've gotten to know a zuhra often describe them as bombastic but emotionally aloof. Since the reconnection with other planes, zuhras have sought out and formed bonds with other genies. They find their jabali cousins to be capable smithing partners, if a bit staid and dull. Zuhras rarely pay non-genie elementals any more than a passing glance, finding even the intelligent ones uninteresting to communicate with or hard to relate to. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Zuhra](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2655) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 160 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +19, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +15, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +17, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +17, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +19, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +16 **Str** +3 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** +4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +5 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [spiked chain](/Weapons.aspx?ID=60)_ --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +17 **Will** +17 **HP** 125 **Resistances** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 10 **Conductive Redirection** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56)) **Trigger** The zuhra is hit by an attack, spell, or effect that deals electricity damage; **Effect** The zuhra conducts the electricity through their body, taking damage as normal, and redirecting a bolt at one target within 30 feet that they can see. The zuhra makes a ranged attack roll with a +20 modifier against the target's AC. On a hit or critical hit, the target takes electricity damage equal to the full damage of the triggering effect. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** _spiked chain_ +19 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)), **Damage** 2d8+9 slashing **Melee** hand blade +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d4+9 slashing plus 1d4 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 26 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (×2), [Magnetic Attraction](/Spells.aspx?ID=944) (at will), [Magnetic Repulsion](/Spells.aspx?ID=945) (at will) - **4th** [Mercurial Stride](/Spells.aspx?ID=1374), [Weapon Storm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1758) - **5th** [Clad in Metal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1366) (can choose uncommon metals) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (at will; to [Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=8), Elemental Planes, or the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) only) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Blinding Reflection** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640)) **Frequency** once per round; **Requirements** The zuhra is in dim or bright light; **Effect** The zuhra briefly reshapes part of their metallic body into a concave surface to reflect the surrounding light into the eyes of a creature within 30 feet. The target must attempt a DC 26 Reflex save. It's then temporarily immune for 1 hour.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until the start of the zuhra's next turn.
**Failure** The target is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) until the start of the zuhra's next turn. **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The zuhra transforms into a Small or Medium metal elemental or animal. This doesn't affect the zuhra's statistics, but it could change the damage type of their Strikes.
Distant Thunder Zuhras favor music over all other arts. Given their long isolation, however, their preferences are quite unlike those of other genies or most musicians of the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1). They value volume, intensity, and discordant combinations of sounds. Lead vocal parts incorporate droning and screams, often with a chorus providing a melodic counterpoint.
Skimming Along Zuhras achieve flight by using magnetism to lift their own bodies into the air. As this grows more difficult the higher they go, many zuhras' preferred method of movement isn't walking or flying, but gliding along the smooth surfaces of their home plane much the way mortals cross ice on skates, with only a thin layer of electromagnetism between them and the ground.
name: Zuhra

The most powerful zuhras draw followers and elemental metal with overwhelming force of personality and literal magnetism. When called upon to conduct _wish_ rituals, they use their long isolation from the other planes as an excuse to misinterpret requests outlandishly, claiming linguistic differences. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Zuhra Shuyookh](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2656) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 161 **Perception** +23; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +24, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +26, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +26, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +24, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +28, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +22 **Str** +5 **Dex** +6 **Con** +8 **Int** +5 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +7 **Items** _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [spiked chain](/Weapons.aspx?ID=60)_ --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +29 **Ref** +23 **Will** +21 **HP** 212 **Resistances** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 10 **Conductive Redirection** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56)) **Trigger** The zuhra is hit by an attack, spell, or effect that deals electricity damage; **Effect** The zuhra conducts the electricity through their body, taking damage as normal, and redirecting a bolt at one target within 30 feet that they can see. The zuhra makes a ranged attack roll with a +27 modifier against the target's AC. On a hit or critical hit, the target takes electricity damage equal to the full damage of the triggering effect.
**Magnetic Field** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505)) 10 feet. All squares in the aura are [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) for creatures wearing metal armor or made of metal. Strikes with metallic weapons made by or against creatures in this aura take a –2 status penalty to the attack roll. Zuhras ignore these effects. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 35 feet **Melee** _spiked chain_ +27 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)), **Damage** 2d8+11 slashing plus 1d12 electricity **Melee** hand blade +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d4+11 slashing plus 4d4 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 32, attack +24 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) - **5th** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (×2), [Magnetic Acceleration](/Spells.aspx?ID=943) (at will), [Magnetic Attraction](/Spells.aspx?ID=944) (at will) - **6th** [Mercurial Stride](/Spells.aspx?ID=1374), [Wall of Metal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1379) - **7th** [Clad in Metal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1366) (can choose uncommon and rare metals), [Enthrall](/Spells.aspx?ID=1516), [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (at will; to [Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=8), Elemental Planes, or the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) only), [Weapon Storm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1758) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The zuhra transforms into a Small or Medium metal elemental or animal. This doesn't affect the zuhra's statistics, but it could change the damage type of their Strikes. **Magnetic Reposition** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The shuyookh targets any number of creatures affected by their magnetic field, and [moves](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) each target 10 feet in a direction the zuhra chooses. Each target can resist being moved if it succeeds at a DC 32 Fortitude save. **Magnetic Storm** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Requirements** The shuyookh's magnetic field is active; **Effect** The shuyookh electromagnetically flings razor-sharp metal scraps. Each creature in a 30-foot emanation takes 8d6 slashing damage and 3d12 electricity damage, with a DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. The shuyookh's magnetic field is deactivated for 1d4 rounds. **Mercurial Wish** ([Downtime](/Traits.aspx?ID=580)) **Frequency** three times per year; **Effect** The shuyookh conducts a _wish_ ritual for the benefit of a mortal, requiring no cost or secondary casters. The shuyookh's result is a success if they succeed at a DC 5 flat check or a failure if not. The shuyookh attempts to fulfill the wish in a way that creates an unstable or impermanent benefit.
name: Zuhra Shuyookh

markdown:
Pelegoxes are beings of condensed magnetic energy that fashion bodies for themselves using their surroundings. Though a pelegox's true form is a spherical core, it pulls metal fragments of various shapes and sizes to form a polyhedral shell. Though pelegoxes might develop preferences for a particular form when at rest, they continue to look for new configurations to call their own, happiest when they can experiment with endless possibilities, even if it means dismantling other metallic entities. These piecemeal creatures move by a combination of magnetic levitation and propulsion, and when that doesn't work, by continuously piercing the ground and shifting their weight forward, looking almost like moving caltrops. Over time, the magnetic signature of a pelegox becomes visible, etched into its body in patterns of concentric lines. These unique designs make it possible to distinguish between individual pelegoxes even when their surface has eroded. Pelegoxes are quick to form relationships with others of their kind. They delight in combining their individual bodies into ever larger, more elaborate structures and patterns. As such, it is rare to find a pelegox on their own— they prefer to travel in a clustered form. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Pelegox Cube](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2657) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 162 **Perception** +22; metalsense ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet **Languages** [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 30 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +22, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +20, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +21 **Str** +4 **Dex** +7 **Con** +6 **Int** +5 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Metalsense** A pelegox cube can sense metal creatures and objects as an imprecise sense. --- **AC** 31 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +24 **Will** +18 **HP** 210 (16 squares) **Resistances** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 10 **Weaknesses** area damage 15, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 10 **[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** fly 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Entrancing Shapes** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The pelegox cube rapidly shifts, creating a display of seemingly impossible geometric patterns. Creatures in a 60-foot cone take 9d6 mental damage (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save). A creature that fails is also [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) with the pelegox cube and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 minute. **Scrambled Strike** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The pelegox cube rearranges to create jutting spikes, attacking each enemy in a 10-foot emanation (DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
2d6 piercing damage
3d6+10 piercing damage
5d6+10 piercing damage **Shard Volley** The pelegox cube magnetizes fragments of metal at range. This is a 10-foot burst within 30 feet that deals 2d6+10 piercing damage (DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). When the troop is reduced to 8 or fewer squares, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst.
Refined Tastes Less interested in arbitrary assignments of monetary value, pelegoxes prefer metals that display new forms or colors. They are as likely to trade a pure sphere of dawnsilver for a jagged piece of corroded copper as they are to keep a priceless djezet carving forever. However, even pelegoxes assign great value to [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=276), as the skymetal's time-bending restorative properties grant the freedom to shape new configurations without being beholden to an unsatisfactory result.
name: Pelegox Cube

markdown:
The geniekin called taloses descend primarily from [zuhra](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2655) genies, [metal elementals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=343), and other denizens of the [Plane of Metal](/Planes.aspx?ID=24). Many taloses mold their natural talent for metalwork into careers as tinkerers, miners, or smiths, although just as many leverage the luster of precious metals in a quest for fame and fortune. Taloses are among the most metropolitan of geniekin, often born in or drawn to cities of various sizes, especially those with advanced metalworking or technology trades. Metal often needs to be worked to find its form and function, which may explain why many taloses seem to enjoy living among people, if not necessarily socializing with them. They often find themselves stumbling into jobs somewhere along the production line of technology, whether it's mining the raw ore from the earth, working it into wires and gears, or constructing the devices themselves. Some taloses resent and reject the expectations that they fill such roles, but many embrace their fascination with tech, often going on to become expert inventors. Taloses are therefore far more likely than other geniekin to make use of clockwork technology in their communities. Despite having long inhabited Golarion, albeit in small numbers, taloses have only rarely been recognized as geniekin. The largest number of them, and also the largest number whose true nature is known, inhabit Tian Xia. The traditions of metal as an element have lived much longer there. Taloses often find themselves drawn to discussions and study of elemental metal the instant they hear of the concept. Historical records speak of renowned courtiers and diplomats with skin of flawless silver or bronze. In the Ivory Port of Goka, the smiths of the family-owned Dragon's Iron Forge craft their blades and firearms with magnetic techniques handed down from their zuhra sheikha ancestor. A talos gadgeteer is a clockwork tinkerer. When confronted, their preferred tactic is to lure opponents into traps using their lesser explosive mines before attempting to engage directly. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Talos Gadgeteer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2658) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 163 **Perception** +3 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +6, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +6, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +6, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +6 **Str** +1 **Dex** +3 **Con** +1 **Int** +3 **Wis** +0 **Cha** +1 **Gadgets** A talos gadgeteer carries the following temporary [gadgets](/Equipment.aspx?Category=15&Subcategory=71), which have no value if sold and last for 24 hours or until
the next time the gadgeteer makes their daily preparations: [lesser ablative armor plating](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1102) (1), [lesser blast boots](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1104) (1), [lesser explosive mine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1111) (3). **Items** [artisan's toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=59), formula book, [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [light hammer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=24) (3) --- **AC** 17 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +6 **Will** +5 **HP** 17 (plus 5 temporary HP (from ablative armor plating)) **Resistances** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 1 **Reflective Defense** ([light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100)) **Frequency** once per round; **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet targets the talos gadgeteer, and they can see the attacker; **Requirements** The talos is in [dim](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) or [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407); **Effect** The talos gadgeteer reflects light off their metallic skin and into the enemy's eyes; it must succeed at a DC 14 Reflex save or be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until the end of the talos's next turn. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d6+1 bludgeoning **Melee** light hammer +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 1d6+1 bludgeoning **Ranged** light hammer +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 1d6+1 bludgeoning **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 14 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Detect Metal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1368)
Traveling Tinkerers While there are no known permanent talos settlements in the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1), there is a small nomadic community known as the Tinkerers' Caravan. Originally founded in Vudra and inspired by similar clans of janns and sulis, the caravan now travels throughout most of the eastern world, offering all taloses who wish to travel with them a sense of community and belonging that they might not find anywhere else.
name: Talos Gadgeteer

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Dewdrop Jelly](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2659)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Dewdrop jellies are small water elementals resembling jellyfish made of gelatinous bubbles. They typically travel in small schools, floating through the air or swimming through the endless seas of their home plane. When threatened, the jellies discorporate into a thin, vaporous mist, reconstituting into their bubble shape only after the danger has passed. Their clever elusiveness makes them hard to find, leading some to claim that dewdrop jellies are mythical flights of fancy told to entertain children.

<column gap="tiny">

([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/ElementalWater_DewdropJelly.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Dewdrop Jelly](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2659)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Aquatic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=168" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=57" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 180

**Perception** +7; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113)

**Skills**

**Dissolve** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The dewdrop jelly takes damage from a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300); **Effect** The dewdrop jelly dissolves into a fine mist. Until the start of the jelly's next turn, it can't be attacked or targeted and doesn't take up space. At the end of the round, the jelly re-forms in any open space within 25 feet of where it Dissolved.

tentacle +8,

On a success, the dewdrop attaches to the target's face, covering its mouth in the suspended water of its gelatinous body. If the target cannot breathe water, it begins to [drown](/Rules.aspx?ID=468). The DC to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) is 16.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Coldmire Pond](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2660)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Coldmire ponds are often mistaken for shallow pools of water, but these sentient bodies of living water actually crawl along the ground or float through other liquids. Coldmires are dangerous ambush predators, drowning creatures that stray too close to or, even more hazardously, stand or swim inside their waters.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 8">[Coldmire Pond](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2660)</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Aquatic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=168" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=57" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 180

**Languages**

[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +18

</row>

**HP** 135

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145)

**Resistances**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10

**Shallow Waters** The coldmire pond can occupy the same space as other creatures. Creatures who move through the coldmire pond treat it as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453). Two creatures both occupying the coldmire pond's space are flanking it, regardless of their actual positions within the coldmire pond.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, swim 50 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
clammy pseudopod +20,
**Damage** 2d8+9 bludgeoning and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** <actions string="Single Action" />  1d6 bludgeoning plus 1d6 cold, DC 25

**Drag Below** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** The coldmire pond occupies the same space as a target it has [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20); **Effect** The coldmire pond pulls the target below the surface. The target must succeed at a DC 26 Reflex save or fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) and begin [drowning](/Rules.aspx?ID=468).

**Flash Flood** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) Water surges out of the coldmire pond in a 20-foot emanation and crashes into nearby creatures, knocking them off their feet. Creatures in the area, as well as any creatures currently sharing the coldmire pond's space, take 4d8 bludgeoning damage and are knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Creatures standing inside the coldmire pond treat their result as one step worse.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-45" />

Often mistaken for [air elementals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=41) due to their hazy, almost-imperceptible forms, a boiling spring is a humanoid water elemental made of scalding steam and bubbling water. When summoned to the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1), a boiling spring raises the ambient temperature around them, making the surrounding climate humid and muggy. They seem to enjoy the discomfort they cause, though some regard it as merely a fun prank while others truly revel in the misery and look for ways to cause more. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Boiling Spring](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2661) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 181 **Perception** +23; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), steam vision **Languages** [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +26, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +26 **Str** +7 **Dex** +7 **Con** +7 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 **Steam Vision** The boiling spring ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from mist and steam. --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +26 **Will** +20 **HP** 255 **Immunities** bleed, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 15 **Freeze and Shatter** If the boiling spring is destroyed with cold damage, their body freezes over and explodes, sending out a wave of frigid air and ice shards that deal 4d6 piercing damage plus 4d6 cold damage to creatures in a 20-foot emanation (DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).
**Sweltering Heat** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) 25 feet. The boiling spring radiates heat, raising the air temperature around them. A creature that starts its turn in the emanation must succeed at a DC 33 Fortitude save or become [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) while it remains in the area; creatures immune to [environmental heat effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) or with any fire resistance are immune.
**Evaporate** **Trigger** An effect would deal fire damage to the boiling spring, even if they would ignore the damage; **Effect** The boiling spring evaporates into the air. Until the beginning of the boiling spring's next turn, they can't be attacked or targeted. They still occupy their space, and their auras still function as normal. --- **Speed** fly 30 feet, swim 80 feet **Melee** blistering fist +27 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)), **Damage** 3d8+13 fire **Ranged** heat wave +27 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [range increment 50 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d12 fire **Scalding Breath** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The boiling spring breathes out a cloud of steam in a 30-foot cone that deals 14d6 fire damage to each creature in the area (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The boiling spring can't use Scalding Breath again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Boiling Spring

Faydhaan shuyookhs are powerful faydhaans venerated as leaders, diplomats, and negotiators. As representatives of faydhaan-kind, they observe strict rules of etiquette and hospitality and hold the literary and performing arts in high esteem. A faydhaan shuyookh takes delight in greeting guests, and being greeted in return, to see how many different greetings they can demonstrate. Guests who creatively engage in this customary greeting game will impress a faydhaan shuyookh and receive magnificent hospitality. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Faydhaan Shuyookh](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2662) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 182 **Perception** +23; (25 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85)) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111), [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet, [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +24, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +26, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +28, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +25, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +28, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +26, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +27 **Str** +7 **Dex** +7 **Con** +5 **Int** +4 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +8 **Items** _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) jambiya_ (functions as a [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)) --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +27 **Will** +25 **HP** 240 **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 15 **Aura of Hospitality** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 60 feet. The faydhaan shuyookh dissuades those around them from hostility. Willing creatures in the emanation gain a +2 status bonus to [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) and [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks. The shuyookh can suppress or resume the aura as a free action. If the shuyookh [acts hostile](/Rules.aspx?ID=300), the aura deactivates and can't be reactivated for 1 hour. When any other creature in the aura acts hostile, it must attempt a DC 35 Will save. On a success, it's unaffected and doesn't need to save again for 1 hour; on a failure, its current action is disrupted, as are any other hostile actions it attempts for 1 round.
**Turbulent Seas** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) 60 feet. Water in the aura that is also in the same body of water as the faydhaan is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) for [Swimming](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) creatures. Creatures with the [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) trait are immune. --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 60 feet **Melee** _jambiya_ +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d4+13 piercing **Melee** fist +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+13 bludgeoning **Ranged** _jambiya_ +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d4+13 piercing **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 34, attack +26 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) - **2nd** [Befitting Attire](/Spells.aspx?ID=867) (at will), [Create Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1476) (at will), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will), [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663), [Water Breathing](/Spells.aspx?ID=1755) - **4th** [Hydraulic Push](/Spells.aspx?ID=1561) (at will), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (×2), [Vapor Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1738) - **5th** [Howling Blizzard](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559) (at will), [Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1473) (at will), [Mirage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1604), [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) (at will) - **7th** [Hydraulic Torrent](/Spells.aspx?ID=1562), [Planar Palace](/Spells.aspx?ID=1634), [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (at will; to [Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=8), Elemental Planes, or the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) only), [Summon Elemental](/Spells.aspx?ID=1698) - **Constant (7th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The faydhaan transforms into a Small or Medium water elemental, aquatic animal, or humanoid. This doesn't affect the faydhaan's statistics, but it could change the damage type of their Strikes. **Diplomatic Wish** ([Downtime](/Traits.aspx?ID=580)) **Frequency** three times per year; **Effect** The shuyookh conducts a _wish_ ritual for the benefit of a mortal, requiring no cost or secondary casters. The shuyookh's result is a success if they succeed at a DC 5 flat check or a failure if not. The shuyookh interprets the wish to please the most people possible. **To the Hilt** The faydhaan shuyookh makes a melee jambiya Strike and buries the blade deep, dealing an extra 3d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) on a hit (6d6 on a critical hit).
Shazathared Shazathared is one of geniekind's greatest storytellers, and her noble lineage traces back to long-deposed faydhaan padishahs. Recently freed from a long imprisonment, “the Jewel of the Padishah” can be found enjoying a warm welcome among faydhaans. When the muse strikes, she performs exciting moral tales from the _Songs of Shazathared_ to honor or enlighten her hosts and delight her audience.
name: Faydhaan Shuyookh

markdown:
Murajaus, one of many nomadic creatures of the [Plane of Water](/Planes.aspx?ID=7), appear [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) from the waist up but resemble a large hermit crab from the waist down, including a pair of strong pincers. Murajau communities are often multilingual, and many individuals are willing to work as translators and guides to adventurers if properly compensated. Rather than currency, murajaus prefer to deal in trinkets such as talismans, particularly those that protect travelers. For murajaus, such items signify their sincerity and unwavering desire to protect others. They trade tales among each other of treasure troves too dangerous to easily explore. Rather than keep this information solely for themselves, they're happy to share it with trustworthy people they meet in exchange for a small share. Faydhaans tell condescending tales of murajaus, describing their origin as royal servants who failed to fulfill a royal decree. Murajaus' own creation story, meanwhile, claims that the royal decree in question requested they travel the lands to provide shelter and hospitality to visitors, a mission they have since carried on for generations. They take great pride in treating guests well, especially in providing suitable food, drink, and gifts. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Murajau](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2663) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 183 **Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +14, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +11, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +13, [Talisman Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +13 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +2 **Items** [_mesmerizing opal_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=225), [_potency crystal_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=231), [_shark tooth charm_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=233), [Spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10) (3) --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +9 **Will** +11 **HP** 60 **Talisman Faithful** A murajau can affix talismans to their shell as though it were heavy armor, and they can affix up to three talismans to it at a time instead of one.
**Hefty Defense** A murajau's weight and legs make them incredibly difficult to trip or be moved unwillingly. They can be tripped only by creatures of their size or larger and have a +2 circumstance bonus to all defenses against being shoved or tripped. However, a murajau can [Stand](/Actions.aspx?ID=86) only as a 2-action activity. --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 30 feet **Melee** leg +14 ([versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d6+9 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** spear +14, **Damage** 1d6+9 piercing **Ranged** spear +14 ([thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 1d6+9 piercing **Retract** The murajau fully retreats into their shell, gaining greater cover; the bonus to Stealth applies only if the murajau is undetected. While in their shell, the murajau can't see outside and takes a –2 circumstance penalty to [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) Perception checks. The only [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) actions the murajau can take are to Stand or Step. The murajau can come back out as a single action. **Swinging Spear** The murajau makes a spear Strike. On a hit, the murajau twists their spear to move the target to any space adjacent to the murajau and deals an additional 2d6 bludgeoning damage as the target smashes into the murajau's thick shell.
More than a Mere Shell To murajaus, one's shell signifies homestead, status, identity, and cultural inheritance. Community elders and leaders often wear shells passed down from a long line of successors, which carry modifications and marks that denote predecessors' achievements and failures. On the occasion that their community numbers exceed the number of shells available, a hunting season might be declared. Participants who return with suitable trophies are revered for their contribution and bravery.
name: Murajau

markdown:
Though the [Plane of Water](/Planes.aspx?ID=7) is mostly liquid, it does contain its share of solid material, whether in the form of free-floating aquatic plants, hunks of coral, or even detritus from lost underwater cities. Flotsam and jetsam collect across the plane, and olobigondes are just one of the many creatures that have evolved to live among and consume this detritus. These flat, round fish are covered in a moss-like skin that makes them exceptionally difficult to spot as they hug the sides of flotsam, feeding off the waste that drifts into their wide, open mouths. While olobigondes are primarily detritivores, they've been known to lie in wait within a forest of kelp or against a mossy stone to ambush smaller creatures, such as water scamps or lone passing merfolk. The fish launch themselves from their hiding spot to take large bites out of their surprised prey, then grab the victim as it tries to flee. Despite their size and ungainly shape, olobigondes can move quickly in water, though their bursts of speed are usually short-lived. In addition to maintaining a firm hold on prey, olobigondes' mouths are filled with a unique toxin that weakens and decomposes living flesh. The hungry fish easily gulp down the resulting slurry. Ancient olobigondes grow truly immense, and their outward appearance evolves. There seems to be no limit to their size. Their skin even separates into plates with deep channels where water can accumulate, giving them the appearance of an entire patch of detritus rather than one piece. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 16 **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 16 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 [Olobigonde](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2664) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 184 **Perception** +7; [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +9, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +7 **Str** +3 **Dex** +1 **Con** +4 **Int** -4 **Wis** +1 **Cha** -5 **Camouflage** An olobigonde can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) in aquatic environments even if it doesn't have cover. However, there must be plants, debris, a seabed, or other objects for it to camouflage itself, not just open water. --- **AC** 17 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +7 **Will** +5 **HP** 38 **Ambush** **Trigger** A target creature passes within 20 feet of the olobigonde's hiding place and has not detected the olobigonde; **Effect** The olobigonde lunges out of its hiding place, [Swims](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) directly toward the triggering creature, and makes a jaws Strike against it. The target creature is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to this attack.
**Reactive Grab** **Trigger** A creature within the olobigonde's reach leaves a square during a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) action it's using; **Requirements** The olobigonde doesn't have a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20); **Effect** The olobigonde attempts to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) the triggering creature with its jaws. On a success, the target also takes 3 piercing damage (doubled on a critical success). --- **Speed** 5 feet, swim 30 feet **Melee** jaws +11, **Damage** 1d8+3 piercing plus decomposing toxin **Decomposing Toxin** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) A living creature struck by an olobigonde's jaws Strike must succeed at a DC 15 Fortitude save or become [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) and take 1d6 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) (or enfeebled 2 with 2d6 persistent poison damage on a critical failure). The enfeebled condition ends when the persistent damage does. A creature currently affected by decomposing toxin doesn't need to save again.
Olobigonde Toxin Alchemists who travel the planes have discovered how readily an olobigonde's toxin can decompose flesh, and some have discovered ways to incorporate it into their creations. An olobigonde's corpse yields approximately 1 gp worth of raw materials when harvested with a successful DC 16 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) or [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) check (2 gp worth on a critical success). This material can be used only to craft [alchemical bombs](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=7) with the [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) trait.
name: Olobigonde

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ore Louse](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2665)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Ore lice are six-legged insectile creatures that roost either just beneath or above the water's surface. They're highly territorial toward both strangers and each other, able to detect an intruder through the ripples in the water. Due to their diet, ore lice have incredibly tough chitin that's infused with metal. Cannibalism between ore lice is common when they're unable to locate other prey.

 Though there are far more suitable environments for ore lice bordering the [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=4), the creatures are often found where the [Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=5) meets the [Plane of Water](/Planes.aspx?ID=7). This was a mystery that puzzled many—until the [Plane of Metal](/Planes.aspx?ID=24) returned between the Planes of Earth and Water. Planar researchers realized that ore lice must have hunted here before the planar schism. On the Plane of Metal, ore lice often appear in horror stories chasing down metal elementals and ferociously devouring them. Their connection to [Ferrumnestra](/Deities.aspx?ID=273) adds to their infamy.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 20

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />

**Damage** 2d8+4 piercing plus rust and metal snack

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
leg +14 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [10 feet reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d8+4 bludgeoning plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=46)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
oxidizing spit +14 ([range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 2d8 acid plus rust

**Consume Living Metal** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The ore louse is adjacent to a creature that died within the last hour and had the [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505) trait or was another ore louse; **Effect** An ore louse can replenish its health by eating the remains of its kin or a once-living metal creature. The ore louse feasts upon the corpse, regaining 3d6 Hit Points. The ore louse can regain Hit Points from a given corpse only once.

**Metal Snack**   An ore louse gains 5 temporary Hit Points each time its mandibles Strike either damages or breaks a metal item using its rust ability, or if it hits a creature that has the [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505) trait or is made of metal.

**Rust**   An ore louse's Strikes causes metal to rapidly rust and corrode. If it succeeds at a mandibles or oxidizing spit Strike, the ore louse deals 3d6 damage (or double damage on a critical hit) to a metal item the target is wearing or holding, ignoring its Hardness. If the ore louse hits an unattended metal item, the item takes this damage automatically. If a creature uses the [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) reaction with a metal shield against the attacks, the shield is automatically [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2), but no other item is rusted on that attack.

**Water Stride**   The ore louse can stand and move on the surface of water or other liquids without falling through. The ore louse can go underwater if it wishes, but it must [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) to do so.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Ore Louse Boots</title>

Due to their ability to walk on water, numerous attempts have been made to use ore louse legs to produce footwear. Techniques tested so far include hollowing out the chitin or harvesting and refining the fine leg spikes. None of these attempts have succeeded as of yet.
</aside>
name: Ore Louse

The briny depths of the Boundless Sea sometimes twist large groups of merfolk into swarms with vicious rows of teeth and sharp claws known as saltborn stalkers. Saltborn stalkers hunt in groups, using their drooping, tentacle-like appendages as bioluminescent lures, posing them as wisps or bubbles of planar energy to draw unsuspecting prey into clever ambushes. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Saltborn Stalkers](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2666) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 186 **Perception** +24; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +22, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +22, [Plane of Water Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +26, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +22 **Str** +6 **Dex** +7 **Con** +5 **Int** +3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +26 **Will** +20 **HP** 240 (16 squares) **Immunities** bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 10 **Saline Crust** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) 20 feet; **Requirements** The saltborn stalkers are in a body of water; **Effect** Layers of the saltborn's salty skin flake off to foul the water around them. A creature that ends its turn in the aura takes 2d6 acid damage with a DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save; creatures with the [amphibious](/Traits.aspx?ID=207) or [aquatic](/Traits.aspx?ID=168) trait are immune.
**[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 10 feet, swim 60 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Lightlure** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Effect** The saltborn stalkers move their luminescent lures in an entrancing light show, drawing nearby creatures into their grasp. Each creature in a 100-foot emanation must attempt a DC 33 Will save; regardless of the result of its save, the creature is then temporarily immune to Lightlure for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) with the lures and must spend all its actions on its next turn to move closer to them as expediently as possible, avoiding obvious dangers along its path.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature is also [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1d4 rounds. **Salty Clutch** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The stalkers reach out to Grab their foes and drag them underwater. Each enemy in a 5-foot emanation must succeed at a DC 33 Reflex save or be [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the stalkers (or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) on a critical success). For the rest of the current turn, the saltborn stalkers can move toward water or in water without ending the grab, carrying any grabbed or restrained creatures along with them. **Scour the Bones** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The saltborn stalkers use their teeth and claws to vivisect each enemy in a 5-foot emanation (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
2d10 slashing damage
3d10+8 slashing damage
3d10+16 slashing damage
The Rite of Salt and Stone When a [merfolk](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=73) joins the ranks of the saltborn, they undergo a secret rite known only to other saltborn and the [brine dragons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=175) of Kelizandrika. The recruits are encased in graves of salt and ice and left at the floor of the Boundless Sea to claw themselves free. Those who overcome the trial are never truly rid of the salt from their tombs, which covers the body of every saltborn stalker.
name: Saltborn Stalkers

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Tantriog](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2667)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Wherever exquisite cuisine is consumed, amazing songs are performed, or epic waves crest, there wait tantriogs. With a crab's foreclaws, turtle's shell, and back flippers, tantriogs are among the more unique water elementals. They ply the Boundless Sea constantly seeking new experiences, whether the next big wave to ride, a tasty delicacy to sample, or a piece of art to collect. They crave new sensations and often seek them out at the expense of others.

 If one possesses rare art, lost lore, or fantastic surfing or cooking skills, a tantriog can be a wonderful companion. They're charming and friendly to those they find interesting, but their interest can be highly precarious, dropped the second someone bores them. Tantriogs may even betray acquaintances if it would give them another interesting story to tell. This makes befriending one a tricky proposition, as nothing can be taken for granted.

 It's a mystery how tantriogs came to wear their current forms. Some say that, in the earliest days of the elemental planes, tantriogs were assassins of ice and mist who committed a crime so great that they're being punished for it to this day. Others claim tantriogs assumed their shape after losing a bizarre cosmic bet. One of the more popular theories about the tantriog's current form is that in the past, they used to be able to shift shape as easily as water molds to its container. In their endless boredom, they were trying out a variety of shapes when a sudden curse (possibly cast by faydhaans) caused them to lose their ability while transforming from a turtle to a crab, leaving them stuck between the two. It's said that any who can teach a tantriog how to relearn their morphing ability could earn the elemental's undying friendship. Whatever the case, the surest way to anger a tantriog is to ask them about their appearance.

 Conversely, the surest way to befriend a tantriog is with compliments about their appearance or their skills. Don't be too fawning, though. Tantriogs don't like being tricked or made to look a fool.

Any creature hit by the tantriog's beak or spitball Strikes must succeed at a DC 24 Will save or become [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) (stunned 2 on a critical failure).

**Shell Defense** <actions string="Single Action" />  The tantriog retracts their limbs and head into their shell and ends their turn. This increases their AC to 26, but they can't act except to reemerge, which they can do as a single action.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Impressing a Tantriog</title>

A tantriog loves seeing a new sight or impressive exhibition of skill. Anyone who wants to stay in a tantriog's good graces needs to keep the new experiences flowing. For this reason, many tantriogs have formed unlikely friendships with gnomes, whose need to seek out new experiences provides common ground.
</aside>

Like other wisps, wood wisps roam their plane and attune to other wisps, wandering with them in symphonies. Because of their willingness to bond, wood wisps appear outside their home plane more than other wood elementals, though they're still relatively rare compared to other wisps. Wood wisps resemble coconuts trailed by thick nests of branches that cover and conceal even deeper webs of thorny vines. They are, unfortunately, both unaware of the pain these thorns can cause fleshy creatures and very physically affectionate in their eagerness to offer assistance. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 14 **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 14 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 9 [Wood Wisp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2668) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 204 **Perception** +6; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +7, [Plane of Wood Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +4, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +6 **Str** +3 **Dex** +1 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 16 **Fort** +5 **Ref** +2 **Will** +8 **HP** 20 **Immunities** bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** [axes](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1) 2, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 2 **Resonance** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125), [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508)) 30 feet. All wisps vibrate at a frequency attuned to their element, resonating with and empowering all creatures and effects sharing that trait. A creature in the area gains a +1 status bonus to attack and damage rolls for effects with the [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) or [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508) trait; a creature with the [elemental](/Traits.aspx?ID=57) trait and either plant trait or wood trait gains this bonus to all attack and damage rolls. For wood wisps, this bonus also applies to nonmagical wooden weapons, such as staves and clubs.
**Accord Essence** ([plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125)) **Trigger** An ally within 30 feet that benefited from the wisp's resonance in the last hour is targeted by an attack; **Effect** The wisp detonates itself in a small elemental explosion of leaves and pollen. This gives temporary Hit Points equal to half the wisp's current HP to all allies within 30 feet that have benefited from the wisp's resonance aura in the past hour. These temporary Hit Points last 1 hour.
A wisp that uses this reaction is permanently destroyed and can be restored only by a _wish_ ritual or similarly powerful effect. If an ability would prevent the wisp's destruction (for instance, if the wisp is summoned and would merely be dismissed), Accord Essence has no effect. --- **Speed** fly 20 feet, climb 40 feet **Melee** thorny vine +5 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d4 bludgeoning plus 1 piercing **In Concert** When a wood wisp rolls a critical failure on a check to [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=75), they get a failure instead, and when they roll a success, they get a critical success instead.
name: Wood Wisp

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Vegetable Lamb](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2669)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Vegetable lambs are gaining popularity as the first elemental to be summoned by spellcasters in training, as they're docile until provoked and easy to control. Casters must take care, however, to not let them germinate in the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1); they're a highly invasive species that can devour an entire forest within a decade.

 The healing and nourishing food that grows from vegetable lambs is appealing for adventurers and sedentary magical scholars alike. The taste is similar to the plants of the same kind that grow on Golarion, but not entirely. Many have noted that there's a slight similarity to meat—a bit of umami one wouldn't find in an ordinary flower.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 15

**[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/ElementalWood_WoodScamp.png" />
</column>

</row>

<traits>
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=57" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Languages**

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +1

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Nature's Bounty** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) Each day, a vegetable lamb grows 1d4 edible flowers (such as artichoke, broccoli, or dandelion) among the green cotton covering its body. These vegetables can be picked without hurting the lamb. A living creature can eat the vegetable with an Interact action to regain 1d6+4 Hit Points.

</column>

---

**Will** +8

headbutt +8,
**Damage** 1d6+2 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hoof +6,
**Damage** 1d6 bludgeoning

**Cottonseed Burst** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The vegetable lamb releases a cloud of cottony green pollen in a 10-foot burst centered on itself. All creatures caught in the burst that need to breathe, apart from the lamb, must succeed at a DC 15 Fortitude save or be [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) by coughing (slowed 2 on a critical failure).

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Wood Scamp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2670)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A wood scamp's veiny, bat-like wings mimic the lamina and venules of a leaf. This, along with their stout green torsos and limbs, enables them to easily blend into the branches of a living tree. They're unusually social for scamps and prefer to work in groups when possible. By their nature, they find their home plane rather boring, and many of them are abuzz with excitement as the plane reopens. They're eager to travel to new places where they can cause mischief.

The wood scamp can't Breathe Pollen again for 1d4 rounds.

**Thorn Puncture**   The wood scamp breaks off one of its thorn-like claws in the target's skin, dealing 1 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) until the thorn is removed, which requires an Interact action. This damage is cumulative with each thorn caught in a creature's skin.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Moss Sloth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2671)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These moss colonies coalesce over a century into the rough facsimile of a large sloth, right down to mimicking the unique structure of the animal's fur that attracts additional fungi and pollinating insects. Unless summoned or attacked, they rarely leave the trees upon which they first formed.

<column gap="tiny">

**Insect Swarm** The first time each day that the moss sloth takes damage from a bludgeoning melee attack, a patch of moss collapses inward, releasing insects that swarm the attacking creature and deal 2d4 piercing damage (DC 15 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Nursery Crawler](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2672)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The vibrant elemental energy of the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25) imbues even the most rotten of fallen trees, empowering their remains to travel in search of the best locations for the next generation to put down roots. This mono-eyed, rotten stump launches seeds that begin to sprout the moment they're buried, whether in the ground or in a living creature's skin.

<column gap="tiny">

<actions string="Single Action" />
root +9,

seed +8 ([range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 1d6+3 piercing plus germinate

**Germinate**  ([Plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668)) A creature hit by the nursery crawler's seed Strike must, on its turn, spend an Interact action to remove the seed; any seeds still implanted at the end of the creature's turn begin to sprout, dealing 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and rendering the seeded creature [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) and [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58). Removing a seed after it's sprouted deals 1d4 piercing damage; removing it before it begins to sprout does no damage. Removed seeds that land in viable soil sprout immediately and grow into new saplings after 1 hour.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Whipping Willow](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2673)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These tall, thin figures are too treelike to be [dryads](/Monsters.aspx?ID=312), yet too humanoid to be [arboreals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=8). Their blank faces and thin trunks bear only the slightest suggestion of humanoid features. Rather than hands, their long, branchlike arms eventually split into equally proportioned branchlike digits, which the willows use both to fight and swing through their native plane.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 19

**[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 4">[Whipping Willow](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2673)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=57" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 207

**Perception** +11

**Languages**
[Arboreal](/Languages.aspx?ID=26), [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +5

**Con** +2

**Int** +0

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +2
</row>

**Grounded** When saving against an effect attempting to knock them [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31), a whipping willow achieves one degree of success better than what they rolled. Additionally, the willow doesn't fall prone on a critical failure to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) an opponent.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 20 

**Fort** +10 

**Ref** +8 

**Will** +14 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 75

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145)

**Weaknesses**
[axes](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1) 5, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 5

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, climb 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
whip +11 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)),
**Damage** 2d8+5 bludgeoning plus strangling vines

**[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** <actions string="Single Action" />  2d4+5 bludgeoning, DC 21

**Strangling Vines**   When a whipping willow hits a creature with their whip Strike, they can choose to [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) and begin strangling the creature. The target is [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) and can't speak as long as it's strangled.

**Unseating Sweep** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The whipping willow attempts to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) all creatures within a 15-foot cone, making a single [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against all targets' Reflex DCs.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-345" />
name: Whipping Willow
