markdown:
A living grove is a singular connected entity, with dozens of genetically identical trunks united within the same root system. At rest, a living grove resembles nothing so much as a small arboretum of birch and aspen trees, though oak and redwood varieties that are significantly larger and tougher exist as well. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 20 **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Living Grove](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2674) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 207 **Perception** +15; [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) 30 feet **Languages** [Arboreal](/Languages.aspx?ID=26), [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108); (understands but can't speak) **Skills** [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +12 **Str** +4 **Dex** +0 **Con** +5 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +1 **Defensive Camouflage** The living grove can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) in natural environments even if it doesn't have cover. While Hiding, its root system is safely covered in dirt, granting the grove a +3 status bonus to AC. A critical hit cracks this protective layer of earth to disperse in the wind, ending the effect. --- **AC** 24 (21 without defensive camouflage) **Fort** +12 **Ref** +7 **Will** +15 **HP** 90 **Immunities** bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** [axes](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1) 5, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 7 --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** branch +15, **Damage** 2d8+4 bludgeoning **Ranged** nuts +11 ([range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d6+4 bludgeoning **[Engulf](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=14)** DC 22, 5d8 bludgeoning, [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) DC 20, Rupture 10. A creature Engulfed by the living grove must also attempt a basic Fortitude save as it's battered between the thin, tightly packed trunks.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
**Failure** The creature is stunned 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is stunned 4.
Slumbering Giants Long before mortal civilization, living groves made their way to the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1), buried their roots deep for safety, and fell dormant. A handful of these ancient elementals survive to this day, numbering among the oldest non-immortal entities yet living on the plane. Their long separation from the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25) has sent most into a deep hibernation, but an infusion of planar energy or powerful primal magic could potentially wake them up.
name: Living Grove

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Carved Beast](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2675)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

An untold number of stumps carved into roughly hewn animal shapes roam the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25). The statue portion of these carved beasts is incapable of articulated movement, though the living wood at their base is fully animate. When left to their own devices, carved beasts prefer to roughly mimic the behaviors of the animals they resemble, like children playing with inarticulate toys.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 22

**[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/ElementalWood_CarvedBeast.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 6">[Carved Beast](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2675)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=57" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 208

**Perception** +16

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +4

**Con** +3

**Int** +0

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +2
</row>

**Top-Heavy** While the carved beast is not Dug In (see below), its unwieldy design leaves it vulnerable to falling over. The DC of any attempt to knock the carved beast [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) is reduced by 5, and the beast takes a –5 status penalty to any check or save it attempts to resist being knocked prone. Additionally, whenever the beast fails to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) opponents with its roots Strike, it critically fails instead. If successfully [Shoved](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) by an opponent, the beast must succeed at a DC 20 Reflex save or fall prone.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 22 (24 while Dug In)

**Fort** +13 (+15 while Dug In)

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +17 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 92

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145)

**Weaknesses**
[axes](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1) 5, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
statue +15 ([Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)),
**Damage** 2d8+5 (3d8+5 while Dug In) bludgeoning plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=46)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
roots +17 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)),
**Damage** 2d8+5 bludgeoning

**Dig In** <actions string="Single Action" />  The carved beast digs its roots into the ground for better protection and purchase. While Dug In, the carved beast can't Stride nor use its roots Strike; however, this also negates the effects of top-heavy, grants the beast a +2 status bonus to its AC and Fortitude saves, and increases the damage of its statue Strike by 1d8. The carved beast can spend an action on its turn to end the effect; alternatively, the effect ends when the carved beast is [moved](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) by force, such as via a successful [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) attack.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Carved Treasures</title>

If an adventurer is careful while defeating a carved beast, the statue may be recovered intact from its defeated body and sold as a lesser art object. With a level-appropriate skill check, a character trained in Crafting can potentially increase the statue's quality to moderate or repurpose it into something else entirely, such as whittling a small bird shape into a decorative whistle.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-345" />
name: Carved Beast

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Pine Pangolin](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2676)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These creatures sleep while hanging upside down by their partially curled tails and rely on their thick wooden scales to protect them from danger. One such danger the scales negate is the falling damage from deliberately rolling up and dropping onto the head of an unsuspecting opponent, which is one of their favorite combat strategies. Beneath their scales, pine pangolin bodies secrete a thick, sticky tar that serves as a secondary line of defense.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 23

**[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 23

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 7">[Pine Pangolin](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2676)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=57" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 208

**Perception** +15

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +15, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +18, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +0

**Con** +5

**Int** +1

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +1
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 25 

**Fort** +15 

**Ref** +10 

**Will** +18 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 140

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145)

**Weaknesses**
[axes](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1) 5, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 2d12+3 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tongue +17,
**Damage** 2d8+3 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Roll Up** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) The pine pangolin falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31), closes up its scales, and rolls into a ball. While Rolled Up, the pangolin gains resistance 10 to physical damage and total immunity to [falling damage](/Rules.aspx?ID=402) from heights of 50 feet or less. The only action the pine pangolin can take is to [Stand](/Actions.aspx?ID=86), and the effects of Roll Up end once the pangolin Stands.

**Secrete Tar** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668)) The pine pangolin secretes a brown, sticky tar within a 5-foot emanation, making those squares [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) for 1 minute. Each creature that enters or starts its turn in a tarred square must succeed at a DC 25 Fortitude save or become [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79). On a critical failure, the creature falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31); prone creatures take a –2 circumstance penalty to their checks to Escape the tar. The pine pangolin is immune to the effects of its own tar.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">More Wooded Origins</title>

Pine pangolins grow in clusters of two to five individuals on giant trees, protected by a cozy layer of tar until their scales harden enough for their first drop. Snapdrakes are handcrafted for kizidhar nobility, often serving as loyal guards. Nobody knows where painted stags come from, as all who have tried to investigate have been eaten.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-345" />
name: Pine Pangolin

markdown:
Skilled kizidhar mages and gardeners handcraft these lovely creatures as much for their shuyookhs' aesthetic pleasure as for their protection. Creating a snapdrake requires first painstakingly constructing a frame of living wood in the shape of a drake before weaving thousands of colorful snapdragon flowers into intricate patterns to form its body and scales. Once complete, snapdrakes often serve kizidhars as a combination of guards, pets, and decorations for as long as they are well fed. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 24 **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Snapdrake](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2677) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 209 **Perception** +16 **Languages** [Arboreal](/Languages.aspx?ID=26), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108); (can't speak any languages) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +15, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +14 **Str** +4 **Dex** +6 **Con** +3 **Int** -2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +11 **Will** +19 **HP** 144 **Immunities** bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** [axes](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1) 10, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10 **Alluring Scent** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246), [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 30 feet. A creature that enters the emanation must attempt a DC 25 Will save. On a failure, the target is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) by the snapdrake and must use at least 1 action on its next turn to Stride closer to the snapdrake. On a success, the target is immune to the snapdrake's alluring scent for 1 hour.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** Tail scythe only --- **Speed** 20 feet, fly 50 feet; greater forest passage **Melee** fangs +14, **Damage** 2d12+4 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) and snapdrake pollen **Melee** tail scythe +16 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+6 slashing **Greater Forest Passage** The snapdrake ignores [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) and greater difficult terrain from plants and fungi. **Snapdrake Pollen** ([Plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 25 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 8 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison damage plus [dazzled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) (1 round); **Stage 2** 1d6 poison damage plus dazzled 1 and [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (2 rounds); **Stage 3** 2d6 poison damage plus [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) and sickened 1 (2 rounds) **Speed Surge** ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) **Frequency** 3 times per day; **Effect** The snapdrake moves up to twice its Speed. **Spray Pollen** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) The snapdrake breathes a blast of pollen in a 40-foot cone. Creatures caught in the blast must succeed at a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save or be exposed to snapdrake pollen. The snapdrake can't use Spray Pollen again for 1d6 rounds.

markdown:
At a distance, painted stags appear constructed in the general shape of male deer, their flanks and antlers decorated with unique, colorful patterns. But while they retain (and indeed, augment) the blueprints [Shumunue](/Deities.aspx?ID=275) imparted upon them, painted stags reject the confines of the Carved Lady's art of mimicry. Today, their cloven, hardwood hooves can crush bone and daub paint with equal skill, and their antlers snap together in a deadly vise. Their torsos rotate freely to navigate the latticed trees of their home plane—all the better to chase prey to their inevitable death. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 26 **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Painted Stag](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2678) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 210 **Perception** +21 **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +23, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +18 **Str** +7 **Dex** +3 **Con** +5 **Int** +2 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +12 **Will** +21 **HP** 175 , regeneration 10 (deactivated by [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) **Immunities** bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** [axes](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1) 10, fire 10 --- **Speed** 45 feet, climb 60 feet **Melee** antler +20, **Damage** 2d12+7 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** hooves +20, **Damage** 2d10+7 bludgeoning **Mauler** A painted stag gains a +5 circumstance bonus to damage rolls against creatures it has [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20). **Painted Dance** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The stag shakes the wooden plating along its body in a cacophonous clatter that sets its painted patterns dancing. All creatures within 60 feet of the painted stag who can see or hear it must attempt a DC 28 Will save; a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the stag takes a –4 circumstance penalty to its save. Regardless of the result of its save, each creature is temporarily immune for 1 hour.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) by the painted stag for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [stunned 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) and fascinated by the painted stag for as long as it's stunned.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except stunned 4.
Artistic Exchange Though few know it, painted stags paint their own markings—making their sporadic appearances in elven art and tattoos, particularly in those cultures descended from the lost nation of Mierani in Varisia, all the more appropriate. Many modern depictions, however, erroneously portray them as benevolent protectors of the forest. Only Darklands tattoo cultures consistently depict them as the relentless predators they truly are.
name: Painted Stag

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Elemental Thicket](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2679)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

In the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1), plant growth proceeds on a scale of days, weeks, years, and decades. Conversely, an elemental thicket grows constantly and in seconds. These writhing masses of living wood are the elemental embodiment of growth itself, swelling up when least expected to consume all in their path.

, regeneration 15 (deactivated by [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72))

This ability requires soil and has no effect on terrain without it, such as worked stone, solid rock, open water, etc.

Twins of rowan are among the most powerful elementals on the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25), specially crafted to patrol a wide range of wilderness and maintain the plane's carefully constructed order. Despite having two heads and torsos, each twins of rowan is a singular creature; like all wood elementals, their true consciousness is not in their heads but in the roots, and their true body is the trunk of the tree, not the extended limbs. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 31 **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Twins of Rowan](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2680) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 211 **Perception** +26; [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet **Languages** [Arboreal](/Languages.aspx?ID=26), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +24, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +26, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +21 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +8 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Shielded Eyes** A twins of rowan's protective mask
shields them from [blinding](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) and [dazzling](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) effects. --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +17 **Will** +27 **HP** 273 **Immunities** bleed, [blindness](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1), [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** [axes](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1) 15, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 15 **Beacon of the Rowan Guard** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508)) 40 feet. The lantern carried by the twins of rowan contains pure elemental life energy that resonates with and empowers all wood elementals. While within the emanation, a creature with the [elemental](/Traits.aspx?ID=57) trait and either the [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) trait or [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508) trait gains [fast healing 10](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) and a +2 circumstance bonus to all attack and damage rolls. The aura can be [counteracted](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) with a DC 30 check to [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78); doing so deactivates the aura for 1d6 rounds. --- **Speed** 35 feet, climb 25 feet **Melee** rapier +26 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+5 piercing **Melee** fist +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+5 bludgeoning **Ranged** stump +26 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d10+15 bludgeoning **Follow-Up Combo** The twins of rowan makes a rapier Strike, followed by two fist Strikes, all against the same target. These attacks all count toward the twins' multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after the twins make their attacks. **Lifespring Burst** ([Plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) Once per day, the twins of rowan can strike the ground with their sword, releasing a 30-foot burst of life energy centered on the twins that deals 14d6 vitality damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save); the twins of rowan is immune. The area affected by this burst then becomes [greater difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) as vibrant new plant life ruptures through it. **Throw Stump** As [Throw Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=38) but can also be used to throw stumps and logs.
Scions of Life Rowan trees have a long association with protection; Shumunue accordingly chose rowan as the base for the powerful guardian twins. Their weapons and protective masks are grown separately from younger saplings fed with mineral-rich magic that makes their wood as strong and sharp as steel.
name: Twins of Rowan

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Kizidhar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2681)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The genies of the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25), known as the kizidhars, are overbearing lords of their element. These master crafters of wood specialize in carpentry and all kinds of woodcarving, and they take a particular delight in reshaping the world in their own image—either through creating wooden statues of themselves or by manipulating and controlling the living things around them. Though they're able to carve themselves similarly, they usually regard their own forms as perfect.

 Shunning the company of other genies, they prefer to avoid those equal in power and instead surround themselves with circles of sycophants and flatterers. With the pathways to the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) open again for the first time in ages, kizidhars roam the multiverse to find mortals they can tempt into joining their palace retinues as servants, entertainers, and valuable sources of new information.

Kizidhars look like walking trees, averaging 10 to 12 feet tall with branches twisting to form their arms, faces, and other features. A kizidhar can reshape their body to create any wooden object.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 24

**[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Genie_Kizidhar.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 6">[Kizidhar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2681)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=57" />
<trait label="Genie" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=216" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 212

**Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108); [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294), [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +11, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +15, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +15, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +15, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +3

**Con** +5

**Int** +3

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Items**
[Staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12), [wooden shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=2) (Hardness 3, HP 12, BT 6)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 22 (24 with shield raised)

**Fort** +17 

**Ref** +13 

**Will** +12 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 110

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[axes](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1) 5, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 5

**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32)** <actions string="Reaction" /> 

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, climb 15 feet; parting the trees

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
staff +16 ([two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)),
**Damage** 2d4+10 bludgeoning

**Arcane Innate Spells** DC 22
- **Cantrips (3rd)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485)
- **2nd**
[Entangling Flora](/Spells.aspx?ID=1515) (×2), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (×2), [One with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618)
- **5th**
[Nature's Pathway](/Spells.aspx?ID=1613) (at will)
- **7th**
[Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (at will; to [Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=8), Elemental Planes, or the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) only)
- **Constant (6th)**
[Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681), [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The kizidhar transforms into a Small or Medium [wood elemental](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=345) or [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) creature. This doesn't affect the kizidhar's statistics, but it could change the damage type of their Strikes.

**Parting the Trees**   The genie ignores [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) from plants, and can share their space with larger plants, such as trees.

**Sculpt Wood** <actions string="Single Action" />  The kizidhar creates a wooden item out of their body that appears in their hand and remains attached. The item can be a one-handed simple weapon, shield, or object of light Bulk. A kizidhar can instead use this ability to transform an item it's already holding or to remove an object to free its hand. Any item created with Sculpt Wood crumbles to splinters if removed from the kizidhar's possession.

**Take Root** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668), [Wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508)) The genie thrusts their weapon into the ground. Plants sprout and grow out of the ground in a 20-foot burst centered on one corner of the kizidhar's square. All squares on the ground in that area are [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) until the kizidhar uses this ability again.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Patrons of Art</title>

Kizidhars are great patrons and beings to introduce into a campaign using [nature crafting](/Rules.aspx?ID=1956) rules. They are proud, vain, and happy to show off their superiority by creating wooden weapons and armor for those who perform a service for them. Kizidhars always seek to gain the upper hand in such deals, however, and PCs exchanging services for kizidhar goods should do so with the knowledge that a kizidhar rarely makes a deal if they don't believe they're getting the better side of the bargain.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-50" />
name: Kizidhar

markdown:
Kizidhar shuyookhs are the powerful rulers of the Glowing Infinity on the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25). They view other genies, including other kizidhars, as subservient to their own greatness, manipulating them as though they were outsiders whenever it helps achieve their goals. Kizidhar shuyookh diplomats are sent as emissaries from the Plane of Wood to the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1), representing the metropolis of Nodollin and Grand Sultana Eshal Muazzam Rayadii of the Glowing Infinity, Lady of All that Grows and Decays. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 33 **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 33 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 [Kizidhar Shuyookh](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2682) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 213 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108); [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294), [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +20, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +19, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +23, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +21, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +21, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +24, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +22 **Str** +5 **Dex** +5 **Con** +7 **Int** +4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +4 **Items** [Spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10), [duskwood shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=312) (Hardness 5, HP 20, BT 10) --- **AC** 30 (32 with shield raised) **Fort** +24 **Ref** +20 **Will** +20 **HP** 220 **Weaknesses** [axes](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1) 10, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10 **[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32)**
**Sprout Shield** **Trigger** An enemy hits the kizidhar shuyookh with a melee Strike; **Requirements** The kizidhar shuyookh doesn't have a shield raised; **Effect** The shuyookh can use Sculpt Wood to create a duskwood shield if they don't already have one. The shuyookh then [Raises their Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=98). The circumstance bonus from the shield applies to their AC against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 25 feet, climb 25 feet; parting the trees **Melee** spear +23, **Damage** 3d6+11 piercing **Ranged** spear +23 ([thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 3d6+11 piercing **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 30 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) - **2nd** [Entangling Flora](/Spells.aspx?ID=1515) (at will), [One with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618) - **3rd** [Wooden Double](/Spells.aspx?ID=1416) - **6th** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (×2), [Lignify](/Spells.aspx?ID=1404), [Nature's Pathway](/Spells.aspx?ID=1613) (at will) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (at will; to [Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=8), Elemental Planes, or the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) only) - **Constant (6th)** [Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681), [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The kizidhar transforms into a Small or Medium [wood elemental](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=345) or [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) creature. This doesn't affect the kizidhar's statistics, but it could change the damage type of their Strikes. **Manipulative Wish** ([Downtime](/Traits.aspx?ID=580)) **Frequency** three times per year; **Effect** The shuyookh conducts a _wish_ ritual for the benefit of a mortal, requiring no cost or secondary casters. The shuyookh's result is a success if they succeed at a DC 5 flat check or a failure if not. The shuyookh attempts to advance their own interests along with those of any beneficiaries from the wish. **Parting the Trees** The genie ignores [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) from plants, and can share their space with larger plants, such as trees. **Sculpt Wood** The kizidhar creates a wooden item out of their body that appears in their hand and remains attached. The item can be a one-handed simple weapon, shield, or object of light Bulk. A kizidhar can instead use this ability to transform an item it's already holding or to remove an object to free its hand. Any item created with Sculpt Wood crumbles to splinters if removed from the kizidhar's possession. **Seed of Ambition** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The kizidhar plants a desire for greatness in the mind of a creature within 50 feet. The target must attempt a DC 30 Will save. On a failure, until the end of its next turn, the target can't [Delay](/Actions.aspx?ID=77), [Ready](/Equipment.aspx?ID=741), or use reactions. Instead, it must use all its actions to Strike or cast the highest rank of spell available to it using spell slots or Focus Points, or take actions to facilitate doing so, such as drawing a weapon or moving toward an enemy. The target must focus its attacks either on the kizidhar or on the opponent it perceives to be the biggest threat. This effect ends if the kizidhar uses Seed of Ambition again. **Take Root** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668), [Wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508)) The genie thrusts their weapon into the ground. Plants sprout and grow out of the ground in a 20-foot burst centered on one corner of the kizidhar's square. All squares on the ground in that area are [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) until the kizidhar uses this ability again.
Wishcrafters Kizidhar shuyookhs consider themselves artists of wish-crafting, planting, and nurturing the seeds of an idea and guiding its growth into the wish they ultimately bring to life. Shuyookhs take great pride in improving a wish as they conduct the _wish_ ritual, often adding their own unique signature elements to wishes, akin to a maker's mark.
name: Kizidhar Shuyookh

markdown:
Great fruiting trees grow in massive orderly rows in a region of the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25) called Armory Grove. Over the course of decades, a regiment tree's branches grow heavy, laden with fruits uncannily shaped like people. They're cultivated by retired warriors whose tireless work ensures that these fruits grow into a form suitable for battle. Their efforts instill tactical knowledge in these fruit warriors so they're prepared to fight as soon as they fall from the tree—which they do simultaneously, forming one battle-ready unit. Each soldier is equal in skill and similar in form, with a wooden outer shell that splits into portions as the flesh of the fruit inside ripens. A slain warrior contains seeds within its body that can slowly germinate in rich soil with enough water—or blood. Every battlefield can become a new garden. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 24 **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Harvest Regiment](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2683) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 214 **Perception** +17; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +17 **Str** +6 **Dex** +1 **Con** +3 **Int** -1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -2 --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +14 **Will** +16 **HP** 135 (16 squares) **Weaknesses** area damage 8, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 8, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 8 **Juice Shower** When a harvest regiment is critically hit or critically fails a save against a damaging effect, sticky fruit juices splash out. This affects all creatures in a 5-foot emanation. A splashed creature takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speeds and everything is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) to it. A creature can Interact to clear off the juice.
**[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Raise Shells** The troop raises fragments of their shells shaped like shields to gain a +2 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of their next turn. **Seed Volley** The harvest regiment spits an orderly volley of hard seeds drawn from within their bodies. This volley is a 10-foot burst within 120 feet that deals 2d10 bludgeoning damage (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). When the harvest regiment is reduced to 8 or fewer squares, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Shell Smash** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The harvest regiment engages in a coordinated melee attack against each enemy in a 5-foot emanation, with a DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d8+3 bludgeoning damage
2d8+9 bludgeoning damage
2d8+12 bludgeoning damage
Out of Season The harvest regiment stat block represents the troop when it's harvested at exactly the right time. A “green,” or unripe, regiment might be pressed into service before it's ready, and an overripe version could fall off the tree partially rotten. Both of these use the [weak adjustments](/Rules.aspx?ID=791) and have 126 HP with thresholds of 84 and 42 HP. A green regiment loses juice shower and weakness to fire, and a rotten regiment's juice shower makes the creature [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) if it fails a DC 25 Fortitude save instead of the normal effect.
name: Harvest Regiment

markdown:
Lomori sprouts are passionate gardeners who closely tend new growth on the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25). Incredibly industrious, they lovingly guide young plants into elaborate constructions and patterns, forming tree tunnels, spiraling fields of flowers, complex hedge mazes, towering tapestries of interwoven bamboo, and other dizzying creations. Lomori sprouts meticulously collect the trimmings, seeds, and debris from the flora in their care. They plant the seeds to yield new growth and frenetically weave the rest into nests, spheres, and other simple objects. They're shy and skittish, often losing themselves in their labors, but they're always friendly to those who treat their gardens with respect.

Lomoris are [aeon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=2) spirits dispatched to the elemental planes long ago to pursue the act of creation. While most of the aeons dispatched to the other elemental planes performed their duties and rejoined their aeon brethren, those on the Plane of Wood lingered, becoming trapped as the plane receded. Over the ages, they adapted and evolved into lomoris. Today, lomoris have more in common with the other denizens of the Plane of Wood than they do with their aeon ancestors. Lomori sprouts, much like [conrasus](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=43), have a central black sphere, known as a lomori's core, that functions as their primary body and consciousness. Where conrasus forge a sturdy exoskeleton, lomoris cultivate a tall mound of prairie grass surrounding their core, which easily grows to be 2–3 times the core's height. The lomori shapes the mound as it grows, creating protective layers to guard their core and forming other useful appendages. Most lomoris have six intricately detailed, crab-like legs of wood that emerge from the lower half of their core, allowing them to rapidly scuttle across the ground or climb trees. **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Lomori Sprout](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2684) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 215 **Perception** +9; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108), [Rasu](/Languages.aspx?ID=82), [Utopian](/Languages.aspx?ID=36) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +7, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +10, [Gardening Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +11, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11 **Str** +0 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 17 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +11 **Will** +8 **HP** 50 **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 3, [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 3 **Scurry** **Trigger** A creature the lomori sprout can observe attacks the sprout; **Effect** After the attack resolves, the lomori sprout can Stride up to their speed. This movement doesn't trigger reactions from the triggering creature. --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 20 feet **Melee** grass lash +9 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [reach 5 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d6 slashing plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=20) **Take Root** **Requirements** The lomori sprout is on the ground; **Effect** The lomori sprout plants themself in the ground. Grasping roots erupt from the ground in a 5-foot burst within 60 feet of the lomori sprout, dealing 4d4 bludgeoning damage (DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save) to creatures in the area; on a failed save, a creature gains the [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) condition until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 20). The roots also make the area [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) for 1 minute, after which they decompose into fertile mulch; the area is no longer difficult terrain, and any creatures still immobilized by the roots automatically Escape. **Greater Forest Passage** The lomori sprout ignores [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) and greater difficult terrain from plants and fungi.
Get Off My Lawn! Lomori sprouts are industrious yet skittish, halting their work only to hide from intruders—if they can be bothered to stop at all. They're ceaseless in their efforts, often working together in large groups to tend vast swaths of land. Nothing rouses a lomori sprout to violence faster than damaging their garden or other creations.
name: Lomori Sprout

The nightwoods are parts of the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25) permanently bathed in darkness due to an absence of fluorescent plants. Here, the flora and fauna require darkness, not light, to thrive. [Troll](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=96)-like creatures known as nightwood guardians patrol these woods to ensure they remain in perpetual shadow. Considering themselves righteous gardens, they care little for the excuses of anyone else—intruders or residents. The guardians absolutely loathe the light, though it doesn't harm them as it does some varieties of creatures. Though wary of both light and any interloping lifeforms in their natural habitat, a nightwood guardian's fear turns easily to wrath, making the guardian a force of fury who seeks to drive out intruders. **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Nightwood Guardian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2685) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 216 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8), [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +23, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +21, [Nightwood Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +15 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +6 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +2 **[Light Blindness](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=22)** **Items** [Club](/Weapons.aspx?ID=2), elemental wooden shield (Hardness 8, Hit Points 64, BT 32) --- **AC** 26 (28 with shield raised) **Fort** +21 **Ref** +16 **Will** +17 **HP** 200 (flesh of wood) **Weaknesses** [axes](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1) 10, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10 **Fire-fueled Rage** When a nightwood guardian takes fire damage, they become enraged. The guardian gains 15 temporary Hit Points, deals 2 additional damage with melee Strikes, and has a –1 penalty to AC. They can't Raise their Shield or use actions with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait except for [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84). This effect ends when the nightwood guardian no longer observes any fire for 1 round or when they fall [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), whichever comes first.
**Flesh of Wood** ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508)) Wounds inflicted on a nightwood guardian turn their flesh to wood. When the guardian is reduced to 40 HP or fewer, their body becomes solid wood. They are [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36), have resistance 10 to all damage, and have [regeneration](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=26) 40 (deactivated by fire). This ends once the guardian has 100 HP or more, though they can choose to stay in this form indefinitely.
**Shield Eyes** When a nightwood guardian has their shield raised, they protect their eyes, losing the [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) and [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) conditions from light blindness and other [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100) effects.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)**
**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32)** --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** club +21 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+6 bludgeoning **Melee** jaws +21 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+6 piercing **Nightwood Roar** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=569), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** The guardian roars, snuffing out lights in a 30-foot cone. Ordinary flames and lights are extinguished, and the guardian attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) any magical light with a +21 counteract modifier and a counteract rank of 5.
Rare Gentleness Though most encounters with nightwood guardians end in violence, the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25) has many folktales about these giant creatures escorting youngsters lost in the nightwoods safely out of the darkness. Whether these stories are true or wishful thinking is debated, with some arguing that a nightwood guardian would only perform such a kindness on the orders of another creature
name: Nightwood Guardian

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ardande](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2686)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Ardandes are geniekin whose elemental birthright ties them to the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25). Their elemental connection can take many different forms, all expressions of the different types and permutations of elemental wood. They might embody the life and vibrancy of a seedling, full of potential and ready to grow. Or, they could be strong and flexible, like the branches and roots of ancient trees, interconnected and all growing together. They could also possess power over death and decay, like decomposing logs ready to fertilize and feed the next generation.

 Common on the Plane of Wood, uncommon in the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10), and only on the cusp of a resurgence into the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1), the ardandes often feel misplaced in the multiverse, born into the wrong place or the wrong time. Many spend their lives searching the planes for answers about themselves and their heritage.

 Ardandes are found in all major cities and civilizations on the Plane of Wood, though most live in Nodollin, the Glowing Infinity, a metropolis controlled by the powerful [kizidhars](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2681). Some ardandes say life in Nodollin is filled with infantilization and belittlement from the kizidhars, the wood genies who rule the land and much of the plane. The kizidhars largely view ardandes as genies like them, but a lesser form of genie that ranks below kizidhar commoners in the same way they are lesser to the shuyookhs. Though a few ardandes rebel against this social order, the weight of culture tells them hierarchies are natural to the element of wood, and they should accept their place even if they chafe.

 Many planar ardandes moved to Shodokar when the new city was founded, hoping the influx of portals leading out of the Plane of Wood would let them visit the rest of the multiverse and learn about their lost mortal heritage. They delight in meeting visitors and showing off the beauty and wonders of their home plane. Some have even moved to the Universe, and they make invaluable mentors for the new ardandes only just discovering their elemental potential across Golarion.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

</column>

</row>

**Damage** 1d4 slashing

**Ranged**

Natural [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) is destroyed, and creatures in the area with the [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) or [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508) trait take 1d6 void damage (DC 16 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">First World Ardande</title>

Some ardandes are born in the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10), the children of [dryads](/Monsters.aspx?ID=312) who take mortal lovers. These ardandes have little contact with other mortals and think of themselves as elemental fey rather than planar scions, a reasonable perspective when life in the First World means severance from the River of Souls. 
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Gennayn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2687)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Gennayns are minor genies who often serve as emissaries and messengers for greater elemental beings. While many find purpose in this humble mission, their true calling and interest is in the arts.

 They often appear as miniature, athletic humanoids with wisps of elemental energy continually dissipating from their bodies. These elemental energies often reflect their emotions and attuned element. Gennayns are also fond of ostentatious rings, amulets, and armbands.

 Gennayns eagerly assist powerful beings who reward and treat them well, but they're just as quick to abandon those they consider boring, stingy, or abusive. Beings who destroy artwork or mistreat artists will earn their eternal wrath.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 2">[Gennayn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2687)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Tiny" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=57" />
<trait label="Genie" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=216" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 226

**Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111), [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +8, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +6, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +8, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +6, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +8, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +10

<row gap="medium">
**Str** -2

**Dex** +4

**Con** +0

**Int** +2

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Inspiring Influence** A gennayn automatically succeeds with the [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=75) action supporting any [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) or [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) skill check. Their Aid action automatically counts as a critical success while aiding in the creation of new art or a new performance.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 17 

**Fort** +4 

**Ref** +10 

**Will** +8 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 30

</row>

**Resistances**
attuned element 5 (see below)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 15 feet, fly 15 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
elemental fist +10 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 2d6 damage (see attuned element)

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 18, attack +10
- **Cantrips (1st)**
elemental cantrip (see attuned element), [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), [Summon Instrument](/Spells.aspx?ID=1703)
- **1st**
[Cleanse Cuisine](/Spells.aspx?ID=1468), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554)
- **2nd**
[Translate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1723) (at will), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will; self only)

**Attuned Element**   Upon waking each day, a gennayn attunes to one planar element. They gain the corresponding trait, a movement Speed, a cantrip, resistance, and an elemental damage type for their energy fist attack based on their attuned element: **air** fly 30 feet, resist electricity 5, [_electric arc_](/Spells.aspx?ID=97), electricity damage; **earth** burrow 15 feet, resist bludgeoning 5, [_scatter scree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=990), bludgeoning damage; **fire** fly 30 feet, resist fire 5, [_ignition_](/Spells.aspx?ID=236), fire damage; **metal** burrow 15 feet, resist slashing 5, [_needle darts_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1375), slashing damage; **water** swim 20 feet, resist cold 5, [_frostbite_](/Spells.aspx?ID=245), bludgeoning damage; **wood** climb 15 feet, resist piercing 5, [_tangle vine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=330), piercing damage. The attunement lasts until the gennayn attunes to a different element.

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The gennayn transforms into a Tiny [elemental](/Traits.aspx?ID=57) or [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9). This doesn't affect the gennayn's statistics, but it could change the damage type of their Strikes.

**Little Wish** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([Fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Trigger** A creature the gennayn can see that's within 60 feet attempts a saving throw or skill check; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The gennayn reshapes reality in a small way to twist fate, allowing the creature to reroll the saving throw or skill check and take the better result.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Artistic Inspiration</title>

Talented individuals often claim to have been inspired by otherworldly forces. As gennayns are known to share inspiration and develop talent, many scholars and artists believe their aptitude unknowingly benefited from a gennayn's attention. Others speculate that being in a gennayn's good graces can stave off bouts of artistic ennui—or that angering a gennayn is a direct path to creative doldrums. Gennayns themselves don't claim to be muses, merely beings drawn to talent.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-50" />
name: Gennayn

markdown:
Regarded as the progenitors of geniekind, janns hold a position of esteem above the genies tied to specific planes. They possess an inborn ability to grant wishes, which the other branches of geniekind lack and greatly envy. Nomadic jann shuyookhs rarely hold court, preferring to gather only when the need arises to perform great deeds. Lesser janns respect and venerate these leaders for their wisdom and total dedication to preserving the honor and reputation of their people. A jann shuyookh reads portents and omens when dealing with duplicitous mortals. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Jann Shuyookh](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2688) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 227 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111), [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +17, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +19, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +18, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +16, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +17, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +19, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +18 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [scimitar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=38)_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [composite longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=74)_ --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +17 **Will** +18 **HP** 155 **Resistances** elemental resistance 10 **Commanding Presence** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 20 feet. A creature that enters the aura must succeed at a DC 26 Will save or be [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (frightened 3 on a critical failure), and is then temporarily immune for 1 minute. A [genie](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=50) (with the exception of another jann) takes a –4 circumstance penalty to their save.
**Elemental Resistance** The jann shuyookh's elemental resistance applies to cold, electricity, and fire damage, as well as all damage from elemental sources (including [environmental damage](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) from the elemental planes and damage from anything with the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165), or [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508) trait). --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 20 feet **Melee** _scimitar_ +20 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)), **Damage** 2d6+12 slashing and All Made One **Melee** fist +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661)), **Damage** 1d8+9 bludgeoning and All Made One **Ranged** _composite longbow_ +18 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=730)), **Damage** 2d8+9 piercing and all made one **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 28 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Know the Way](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582) - **4th** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (×2), [Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=1647) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (at will; to [Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=8), Elemental Planes, or the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) only) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **All Made One** The jann shuyookh calls upon all the elements that make up their being and chooses one. They gain an additional arcane spell they can cast at will and empower their Strikes with the element, dealing an extra 1d6 damage of the listed type. These benefits last until the shuyookh uses this ability again. **Air** [_lightning bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=172), 1d6 electricity; **earth** [_one with stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=188), 1d6 bludgeoning; **fire** [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=119), 1d6 fire; **metal** [_noxious metals_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1376), 1d6 electricity; **water** [_wall of water_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1028), 1d6 bludgeoning; **wood** [_wall of thorns_](/Spells.aspx?ID=366), 1d6 piercing. **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The genie transforms into a Small or Medium [elemental](/Traits.aspx?ID=57) or [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9). This doesn't affect the genie's statistics, but it could change the damage type of their Strikes **Wanderer's Wish** **Frequency** three times per year; **Effect** The jann instantly grants the benefits of a critical success with the _wish_ ritual to a mortal creature. This has no cost. That creature specifies what they wish for, but the interpretation is up to the jann. A jann typically attempts to grant wishes in a way that encourages growth and exploration. A summoned jann can't use this ability.
Evolving Essence With the return of the metal and wood elemental planes, janns found new elemental magic surging within their beings. This has caused rapid physical changes, and janns anticipate that it could eventually even change their moods and thoughts. Though most janns now feel the full breadth of elemental power, some still find themselves limited to just the elements of air, earth, fire, and water, or have diminished influence over metal and wood compared to the other four elements.
name: Jann Shuyookh

markdown:
Pulsating organs and tumors squirm loosely within a sac of skin, all forming the misshapen lump that is the body of a rakkatak. The predator's eyes scan the area, shimmering in the smoke like glowing dots. Wriggling insectoid legs—varying in number from one rakkatak to another—hang from its bulbous body. These limbs flail chaotically as a rakkatak fights, only calming when it's time to eat or rest. Most rakkataks fly alone, picking off meals in sudden swooping strikes, but they will occasionally form a flock. Despite having little faculty for communication with other creatures, rakkataks have a certain ravenous genius for hunting with kin. Typically, once one grabs prey, the others swoop in to latch on as well, ingesting their meal with leech-like mouths. Rakkataks possess insatiable appetites to help them withstand the long periods of famine between their infrequent meals as they travel the expanses of the [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=6). As a result, they easily overconsume if brought somewhere with more abundant fauna, like the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1). A single rakkatak can hold a surprising quantity of offal, even growing in size as its body expands. It becomes more unwieldy the larger it gets, which planar biologists believe to be a survival tactic to keep a rakkatak from indulging its hunger until it quite literally bursts. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Rakkatak](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2690) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 132 **Perception** +9; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +5 **Int** -3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -2 --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +11 **Will** +9 **HP** 90 **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) **Weaknesses** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 5 **Exploding Guts** When the rakkatak is critically hit or critically fails a Fortitude save, one of its organs bursts. Roll 1d4 to determine what effect this has.
**1.** Trapped noxious gas rushes out. The rakkatak is [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet away from the source of the triggering attack or effect.
**2.** Pus showers those nearby. Each creature in a 5-foot emanation is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).
**3.** The damage is severe. The rakkatak takes 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
**4.** Gelatinous rakkatak eggs explode forth and instantly hatch. Each creature in a 5-foot emanation takes 1d6 [persistent piercing damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) from the ravenous larvae. --- **Speed** 5 feet, fly 45 feet **Melee** mouth +15, **Damage** 2d8+7 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** leg +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 1d10+7 slashing **Predator's Stare** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The rakkatak turns its grotesque yet mesmerizing eyes upon one creature it can see within 30 feet. That creature must succeed at a DC 22 Will save or become [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) with the rakkatak. While fascinated, the creature must spend at least 1 action each round moving closer to the rakkatak as expediently as possible, and can't intentionally move away from it. The fascination ends after 1 minute or when the rakkatak uses Predator's Stare again, whichever comes first. **Suck Innards** **Requirements** A [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), or willing creature is within the rakkatak's reach; **Effect** The rakkatak deals 3d6 damage to the target (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). If the creature takes at least 12 damage, the rakkatak regains 10 HP.
Ashen Hunting Grounds Avoiding the blazing chaos and ifrit rule that typify much of the [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=6), rakkataks prefer ashen wastelands as their hunting grounds. They dig simple burrows just barely below the surface, called rakkatak hills. Within, they can doze and digest in peace or lay and tend their horrifying eggs.
name: Rakkatak

<title level="1" pfs="">[Slime Puppeteer (Ungothol)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2691)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Early in the alghollthu's attempt to control the sapient creatures of Golarion, they created the ungothol to feed them information. These slime-covered creatures would infiltrate humanoid settlements and send back information to their masters using powerful telepathy. Over centuries, the slime puppeteers proved inferior compared to the more versatile [faceless stalkers](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4).

Slime puppeteers disguise themselves as their humanoid meals. They envelop their target, killing it, then the puppeteer's skin and the corpse's skin both become gelatinous and merge. The puppeteer's form replaces the outer layer of the corpse's body, while long, wiry tendrils implant themselves in the corpses' nervous system. The result is a nearly-perfect copy of the dead creature, now controlled from within by the sinister alghollthu agent. The slime puppeteer can speak using the dead creature's voice, though without the original creature's language or memories.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 27

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Alghollthu_SlimePuppeteer.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 6">[Slime Puppeteer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2691)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="CN" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=1" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 82

**Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +16, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +10, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +16

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +4

**Con** +2

**Int** +3

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Telepathic Bond** ([telepathy](/Traits.aspx?ID=250)) A slime puppeteer maintains a strong telepathic bond with the alghollthu that created it. This bond has planetary reach, but the puppeteer is [blind](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) and [deaf](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) while accessing this bond.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 24 

**Fort** +14 

**Ref** +14 

**Will** +10 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 95

</row>

**Stinky Sweat** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246)) 10 feet. When a slime puppeteer takes fire damage, they form a layer of pungent slime on their skin that remains until washed away. While the layer is present, creatures adjacent to the slime puppeteer must attempt a DC 24 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34). A creature that succeeds at its save is immune to the puppeteer's sweat for 1 minute.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 10 feet, fly 30 feet, swim 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 1d6+7 piercing plus 2d6 poison

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
wing +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188)),
**Damage** 1d4+7 bludgeoning

**Envelop** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93)) The puppeteer wraps their body around an adjacent target (DC 24 Reflex negates). The target is [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) and the puppeteer is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24). The puppeteer can make only tail Strikes against the restrained creature and can only engulf a single Medium or Large creature. The puppeteer can end an Envelop as a free action.

**Mimic Form**   The puppeteer can take on the form of a corpse they have enveloped. This takes one hour, during which time the puppeteer can't move or attack and has an AC of 16. Once the target's form has been taken, the puppeteer cannot use its Envelop, fly speed, and swim speed abilities until Mimic Form ends. The puppeteer only has access to their wing Strike while using Mimic Form. The puppeteer can end Mimic Form as a three-action [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) activity.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Puppeteer Variations</title>

The slime puppeteer is only one attempt at creating a terrestrial spy; the alghollthu created countless more. It's challenging to tell how many of their kind survived until the modern day. Some slime puppeteers traded the sensitivity to hot environments for cold. Others were massively enlarged, targeting various giant communities. There are even rumors of elemental-infused slime puppeteers capable of infiltrating the elemental planes, but so far no solid evidence has been presented to recognized scholars.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-1" />
name: Slime Puppeteer

<title level="1" pfs="">[Ulat-Kini Initiate](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2692)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Several groups of ulat-kinis have rediscovered the ancient way of kirtanang, a martial art from that uses broad, flowing motions of the claws.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 21

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="LE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Amphibious" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=207" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 83

**Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Alghollthu](/Languages.aspx?ID=24), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +5

**Con** +2

**Int** +0

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 21 

**Fort** +11 

**Ref** +13 

**Will** +8 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 60

</row>

**Resistances**
[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 5

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, swim 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d8+5 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fangs +13,
**Damage** 2d4+5 piercing plus 1 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)

**Flurry of Claws** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The ulat-kini initiate makes two claw Strikes against a single target.

**Kirtanang**   The initiate's Strikes take no penalty from passing through water.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Ulat-Kini Culture</title>

The creatures who surface humanoids consider scum still hold grand empires beneath the waves. While some ulat-kinis still revere the alghollthu who created them and others have learned to hate their creators, there are ulat-kinis who have found a new way forward. They have developed their own culture outside of alghollthu influence, founding new underwater traditions and creating new cultural standards.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-1" />
name: Ulat-Kini Initiate

markdown:
When not being controlled and manipulated by alghollthus, ulat-kinis have found they can develop formidable psychic powers. Those who master this craft are dubbed “mindreavers” by their awestruck kin. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 25 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 [Ulat-Kini Mindreaver](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2693) **Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 83 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Alghollthu](/Languages.aspx?ID=24), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 30 feet **Skills** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +15, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +12 **Str** +1 **Dex** +3 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +6 **Items** [_pendant of the occult_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=450) --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +12 **Will** +16 **HP** 100 **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 10 --- **Speed** 20 feet, swim 40 feet **Melee** claw +14, **Damage** 1d6+3 slashing **Melee** fangs +12, **Damage** 2d4+3 piercing **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 22 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=142), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **1st** [Alarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=7), [Déjà Vu](/Spells.aspx?ID=684), [Ill Omen](/Spells.aspx?ID=696) (3 slots) - **2nd** [Blood Vendetta](/Spells.aspx?ID=675), [Soothe](/Spells.aspx?ID=291), [Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=333) (3 slots) - **3rd** [Agonizing Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=665), [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=220), [Sculpt Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=714) (2 slots) - **4th** [Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=40), [Seal Fate](/Spells.aspx?ID=715) (2 slots) **Psychic Daze** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Psychic](/Traits.aspx?ID=430)) The mindreaver targets one creature within 120 feet and violently intrudes into their mind. The target attempts a DC 22 Will save.
**Critical Success** The target creature is unaffected.
**Success** The target creature gains weakness 1 to mental damage for 1 minute. If they already have weakness to mental damage, the value of the weakness increases by one.
**Failure** The target creature takes 2d10 mental damage and gains weakness 1 to mental damage for 1 minute. If they already have weakness to mental damage, the value of the weakness increases by one.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the target's weakness to mental damage increases by 2.
Ulat-Kini Culture The creatures who surface humanoids consider scum still hold grand empires beneath the waves. While some ulat-kinis still revere the alghollthu who created them and others have learned to hate their creators, there are ulat-kinis who have found a new way forward. They have developed their own culture outside of alghollthu influence, founding new underwater traditions and creating new cultural standards.
name: Ulat-Kini Mindreaver
