<title level="1" pfs="">[Giant Bloodseeker](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2694)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The Court of Ether breeds bloodseekers to enormous sizes to serve as cavalry mounts for their non-winged warriors. These bloodseekers serve as capable mounts—at least until they detect the scent of blood.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 2">[Giant Bloodseeker](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2694)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=9" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 84

**Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +9, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +7

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +5

**Con** +2

**Int** -5

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Blood-Crazed** If the bloodseeker starts its turn within 60 feet of a creature with [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) damage, it must attempt a DC 11 flat check. On a failure, the bloodseeker attempts to move adjacent to the bleeding creature and Strike it with its proboscis. The bloodseeker gains a +4 circumstance bonus to attack rolls against this target.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 19 

**Fort** +6 

**Ref** +11 

**Will** +6 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 25

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 10 feet, fly 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
barbed leg +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d4+3 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
proboscis +7,
**Damage** 1d6+3 piercing plus Blood Drain

**Blood Drain** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The bloodseeker’s last action was a successful Strike with its proboscis against a target with blood; **Effect** The bloodseeker sucks blood from that target, dealing 2d4 damage, and the bloodseeker gains temporary Hit Points equal to the damage dealt. The target becomes [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10).

**[Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)** <actions string="Single Action" />  

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-347" />
name: Giant Bloodseeker

<title level="1" pfs="">[Bloodseeker Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2695)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The Court of Ether release the less useful, smaller breed of bloodseekers into the Endless Gulf, where they form into dangerous swarms of the insatiable devourers. This has lead to a dangerous new game, Seek-a-Bang, where fey mounted on [giant bloodseekers](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2694) throw [thunderstones](/Equipment.aspx?ID=79) at swarms of their mounts' smaller cousins and see how many they can kill with a single throw. So far, the fatalities are few enough to be acceptable.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Bloodseeker_Swarm.png" />
</column>

</row>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet

</row>

**Resistances**

**Weaknesses**
area damage 6, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 3

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 10 feet, fly 30 feet

**Blood Draining Bites** <actions string="Single Action" />  Each creature in the swarm's space takes 1d6 piercing damage plus 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and is [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). A successful DC 20 Reflex save reduces the piercing damage by half and negates the bleed damage and drained condition.

</column>

Calignis often get stereotyped as murderers, but the title of assassin is one of the rarest in their society. Caligni settlements go decades without having offspring born with enough potential. However, once a child shows they have “the gift,” the settlements leaders might give the babe to the owb, who mold and shape the child into an instrument of shadows and death. Lauded and shunned, caligni assassins often become unwitting pawns in clashes between caligni settlements or between rival factions of caligni society. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Caligni Assassin](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2696) **Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 85 **Perception** +14; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Caligni](/Languages.aspx?ID=28), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=21), [Shae](/Languages.aspx?ID=71), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +20, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +15, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +15, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +20 **Str** +3 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 **Shadow Shift** Caligni assassins are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) in [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) or [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2404) even to creatures that can see clearly in those light levels.
**Swift Sneak** The assassin can move their full speed while [Sneaking](/Actions.aspx?ID=2765). **Items** [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [spiked gauntlet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=11)_, [throwing knives](/Weapons.aspx?ID=243) (4) --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +18 **Will** +12 **HP** 135 **Countershadow** ([darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet casts a [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100) spell; **Effect** The caligni assassin attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the triggering spell. Caligni assassins automatically recognize light spells.
**Death Umbral Shroud** ([darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) When a caligni assassin dies, their body disintegrates into a veil of supernatural darkness for 1 round. A 4th-level [darkness](/Domains.aspx?ID=6) spell appears, centered on the assassin's location, with a counteract DC of 23.
**Deny Advantage** The assassin isn't [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to creatures of 8th level or lower that are [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39), flanking, or using surprise attack.
**[Light Blindness](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=22)** --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** _spiked gauntlet_ +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d4+9 piercing plus Umbral Veil **Ranged** throwing knife +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 1d4+9 piercing **[Quick Draw](/Feats.aspx?ID=498)** **Shadow Leap** ([Darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) **Requirements** The assassin is in [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) or [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=409); **Effect** The assassin vanishes into shadow, appearing in a square of dim light or darkness within 60 feet. If the assassin appears adjacent to a creature, they can Strike that creature, otherwise the assassin can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62). **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The assassin deals an additional 2d6 precision damage to flat-footed creatures. **Umbral Veil** ([Darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39)) **Requirements** The assassin's last action was a successful melee Strike; **Effect** The assassin extends a tendril of shadow to obscure the target's vision, [blinding](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) them for 1 round (DC 23 Will negates). On a critical failure, they're blind for 1 minute. Creatures with greater darkvision are immune to this ability.
Shadowfade Caligni assassins gain their power from an infusion of planar essence directly from the [Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=11), which the [owb](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1257) help them develop into powerful abilities. This comes at a cost: all caligni assassins eventually succumb to an affliction known as shadowfade. This magical malady causes the caligni's body to painfully warp when concealed in shadows, as the plane's ability to create twisted reflections of reality changes their physical form directly. Sometimes a caligni assassin will flee home and strike out on their own to avoid becoming a twisted tool for their greedy leaders. Such freelancers are in high demand for those seeking a fatal solution to their own political problems.
name: Caligni Assassin

**Tight Formation** While adjacent to another royal guard, each royal guard gains a +2 circumstance bonus to their Fortitude and Reflex DCs against attempts to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38), [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40), or [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=29) them. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** guisarme +15 ([Reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 1d10+6 slashing plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=20) **Ranged** light hammer +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 1d6+6 bludgeoning **Occult Innate Spells** DC 21 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=286) - **2nd** [Blood Vendetta](/Spells.aspx?ID=675), [Paranoia](/Spells.aspx?ID=214) **Brutal Beating** A royal guard deals an additional 1d10 precision damage to prone creatures. **[Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=20)**

markdown:
Involved in both domestic security and international espionage, the agents of King Orgukagan's secret police are skilled at capturing enemies for interrogation, infiltrating rival organizations, and fighting without weapons. When the king takes special interest in a project, event, or individual, he sends one of his specially trained agents to handle the situation. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 25 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 [Hryngar King's Agent](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2698) **Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 86 **Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +16, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +5 **Lie to Me** A king's agent uses their [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) DC when someone tries to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47) to them. **Items** [Bola](/Weapons.aspx?ID=92) (2), [Hand Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=69), [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3), [average manacles with keys](/Equipment.aspx?ID=32), [spellstrike ammunition I](/Equipment.aspx?ID=167) (2; [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=288), [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=45) [“drop prone”]) --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +13 **Will** +14 **HP** 78 **Counter Feint** **Trigger** A creature fails to [feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=48) the king's agent; **Effect** The creature is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against the next melee attack that the king's agent attempts before the end of their next turn.
**[Light Blindness](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=22)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188)), **Damage** 1d6+4 bludgeoning **Melee** dagger +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d4+4 piercing **Ranged** hand crossbow +13 ([range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6+2 piercing **Ranged** dagger +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 1d4+4 piercing **Ranged** bola +13 ([Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [Ranged Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=251), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 1d6+4 bludgeoning **Occult Innate Spells** DC 21 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=286) - **2nd** [Blood Vendetta](/Spells.aspx?ID=675), [Paranoia](/Spells.aspx?ID=214) **Nerve Strike** The king's agent Strikes with their fist. If they hit, the target is [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) until the end of the agent's next turn (DC 19 Fortitude negates). **Quick Arrest** **Requirements** The hryngar king's agent is holding [manacles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=32) and is adjacent to a creature who is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), or [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38); **Effect** The hryngar king's agent applies the manacles. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The hryngar king's agent deals an additional 2d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.
name: Hryngar King's Agent

Abandoned by their cousins, the surface dwarves, hryngars were saved by [Droskar](/Deities.aspx?ID=28), who taught them to work hard and give no quarter. Now, that hardness is the sword and shield by which the hryngar rule their kingdoms. The forgepriests proselytize Droskar's teachings and punish heretics for any defiance. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Hryngar Forgepriest](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2699) **Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 87 **Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +13, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +15, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +11 **Str** +3 **Dex** +1 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +3 **Items** [Chain Mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=9), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) [light hammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=29)_ --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +11 **Will** +15 **HP** 99 **Resistances** fire 5 **Toil is Strength** ([misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110)) **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet would succeed at a save against a spell or ability that causes the [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) condition; **Effect** The target must reroll the triggering saving throw and use the worse result.
**Light Blindness** --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** _light hammer_ +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d8+5 bludgeoning **Ranged** _light hammer_ +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 2d8+5 bludgeoning **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 21 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=229), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) - **1st** [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45), [Detect Poison](/Spells.aspx?ID=67), [Magic Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=182) - **2nd** [Charitable Urge](/Spells.aspx?ID=677), [Mending](/Spells.aspx?ID=189), [See Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) - **3rd** [Agonizing Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=665), [Crisis of Faith](/Spells.aspx?ID=55), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 21 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=286) - **2nd** [Blood Vendetta](/Spells.aspx?ID=675), [Paranoia](/Spells.aspx?ID=214) **Hammerfall** The forgepriest tosses their hammer up to 30 feet. Where the hammer lands, molten metal erupts from the ground in a 10 foot radius dealing 6d8 fire damage to creatures in the area (DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Creatures who critically fail also take 1d8 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). The forgepriest can't use Hammerfall again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Hryngar Forgepriest

<title level="1" pfs="">[Redcap Cavalry](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2700)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Whomever first imagined putting a fey with a bloodsoaked hat on a giant blood-crazed insect certainly had a sense of humor, but that didn't stop the redcaps from trying to domesticate and train giant bloodseekers. Although a flying cavalry was Queen Frilogarma's goal, her court ended up accidentally inventing redcap racing. In this sport, one redcap with a freshly soaked cap is swung around a racetrack by a giant arm while several other redcaps on giant bloodseekers fly after them. The “bait” redcap doesn't usually survive, but they are the center of attention for about two minutes.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Redcap_Cavalry.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 6">[Redcap Cavalry](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2700)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Fey" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=69" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 88

**Perception** +14; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +17, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +13, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +13, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +11

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +4

**Con** +2

**Int** +1

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Items**
iron boots, red cap, [Scythe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=39)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 23 

**Fort** +12 

**Ref** +14 

**Will** +11 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 75
([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) 10)

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5, divine revulsion

**Divine Revulsion** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) If a redcap sees a creature brandish a [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=41) of a good deity (which requires an Interact action by that creature) or use one to cast a [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48) spell, the redcap must attempt a DC 21 Will save. It then becomes temporarily immune to all brandished religious symbols for 10 minutes.

**Critical Success** The redcap is unaffected.

**Success** The redcap is [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).

**Failure** The redcap gains the [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17) condition for 1 round and is frightened 4.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 50 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
scythe +17 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)),
**Damage** 1d10+11 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
boot +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [versatile B](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 1d6+9 piercing

**Blood Soak** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) The redcap dips its cap in the blood of a slain foe. The foe must have died in the last minute, and the redcap must have helped kill it. The redcap gains a +4 status bonus to damage rolls for 1 minute.

**Flyby Swing** <actions string="Reaction" />  **Trigger** The recap's bloodseeker mount moves away from a creature within the redcap's reach; **Requirements** The redcap is mounted on a giant bloodseeker; **Effect** The redcap Strikes the creature with their scythe.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-348" />
name: Redcap Cavalry

<title level="1" pfs="">[Ether Sprite](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2701)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The excitable sprites of the Ether Court prefer to frolic, drink, insult each other, and perform improv comedy.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 13

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Sprite_Ether.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature -1">[Ether Sprite](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2701)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Tiny" />
<trait label="Fey" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=69" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 89

**Perception** +5

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +4, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +4, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +4, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +4

<row gap="medium">
**Str** -2

**Dex** +3

**Con** +0

**Int** +0

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Natural Glow** ([light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) An ether sprite sheds light as a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=60). The sprite can suppress this glow by spending a single action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.

**Items**
[Rapier](/Weapons.aspx?ID=36)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 14 

**Fort** +1 

**Ref** +6 

**Will** +3 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 8

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 1

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 10 feet, fly 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
rapier +6 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 1d6–2 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
luminous spark +6 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 1d4 fire

**Hilarious Insult** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The ether sprite taunts a creature within 30 feet and rolls [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) against the target's Will DC. On a success, the target is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) until the end of their next turn.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Sprite Collections</title>

Ether sprites have a reputation for being thieves. In truth, they're generally curious about the world and simply have a tendency to walk away with objects that don't belong to them, not understanding the concept of ownership. As a result, ether sprites tend to have small interesting objects in their possession, such as talismans or gemstones.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-95" />
name: Ether Sprite

<title level="1" pfs="">[Ether Sprite Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2702)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

One ether sprite is quite enough for most, and can be tiresome for even the fey. When a group of them get together, it's outright bedlam.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 3">[Ether Sprite Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2702)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Fey" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=69" />
<trait label="Swarm" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=239" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 89

**Perception** +9; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23)

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +9, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +9, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +9, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +9

<row gap="medium">
**Str** -2

**Dex** +4

**Con** +0

**Int** +0

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +4
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 19 

**Fort** +5 

**Ref** +11 

**Will** +7 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 32

</row>

**Immunities**
precision, swarm mind

**Resistances**
bludgeoning 3, piercing 5, slashing 5

**Weaknesses**
area damage 6, [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 3, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 3

**Rowdy Clamor** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206)) 20 feet. The sprite swarm creates a din by laughing, taunting, and insulting nearby creatures. Creatures in the area are [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 10 feet, fly 40 feet

**Luminous Pattern** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The sprites glow brightly, flying in a shifting pattern of light. All creatures within 20 feet become [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 minute (DC 16 Will negates).

**Spark Bombardment** <actions string="Single Action" />  The sprites hurl luminous sparks at a creature within 20 feet dealing 5d4 fire damage (DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).

**Swarm Steal** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The sprites rummage through the possessions of a single creature within its space, attempting a [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check against the creature's Reflex DC. On a success, the sprites take one item of negligible or light bulk that the creature is carrying but not holding or readied. On a critical success, the item can be one that is held or readied, such as a weapon or potion.

**Swarming Needles** <actions string="Single Action" />  The sprites numerous rapiers deal 2d6 piercing damage to each enemy in the swarm's space (DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Sprite Collections</title>

Ether sprites have a reputation for being thieves. In truth, they're generally curious about the world and simply have a tendency to walk away with objects that don't belong to them, not understanding the concept of ownership. As a result, ether sprites tend to have small interesting objects in their possession, such as talismans or gemstones.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-95" />
name: Ether Sprite Swarm

summary_markdown: One ether sprite is quite enough for most, and can be tiresome for even the fey. When a group of them get together, it's outright bedlam.

markdown:
Cave worm eggs that lie dormant for a long period may experience significant climactic shift. If the temperature falls below freezing, the worms may hatch as glacial worms, also called white worms. These creatures immediately migrate as far north as possible, seeking the glaciers or icebergs that will nourish and house them. Glacial worms lack stingers, as liquid venom is less effective in cold temperatures. Occasionally, such creatures are carried far south on icebergs that have carved off the main ice sheets of the polar regions. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Glacial Worm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2703) **Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 67 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 100 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +34 **Str** +10 **Dex** +0 **Con** +8 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -1 --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +30 **Ref** +22 **Will** +22 **HP** 370 (cold healing) **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 15 **Cold Healing** When a glacial worm is in extreme cold or surrounded by ice, it gains [fast healing 10](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15). When struck by a magical cold effect, a glacial worm regains Hit Points equal to half the cold damage the effect would otherwise deal.
**Inexorable** The glacial worm recovers from the [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35), and [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) conditions at the end of its turn. It's also immune to penalties to its Speeds and the [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) condition, and it ignores [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) and greater difficult terrain.
**Leech Heat** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27)) 30 feet. A glacial worm's intense cold absorbs heat. Creatures that start their turn within the area take 3d6 cold damage (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save).
**Shake It Off** **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** The glacial worm would be affected by a condition or adverse effect (such as [_baleful polymorph_](/Spells.aspx?ID=17)); **Effect** The glacial worm negates the triggering condition or effect. Effects from artifacts, deities, or a similarly powerful source can't be avoided in this way. --- **Speed** 40 feet, burrow 40 feet, swim 20 feet **Melee** jaws +32 ([deadly 2d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+13 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** body +30 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+12 bludgeoning plus 1d6 cold **Fast Swallow** **Trigger** The worm Grabs a creature; **Effect** The worm uses Swallow Whole. **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** Huge, 3d8+12 bludgeoning plus 2d6 cold, Rupture 28 **Thrash** The worm makes a Strike once against each creature in its reach. It can Strike up to once with its jaws and any number of times with its body. Each attack counts toward the worm's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after it makes all the attacks.
name: Glacial Worm

One subspecies that some call unnatural is the gray worm. It is theorized that these cave worms hatched near the corpses of thousands of graves, perhaps deep in the layers of the Darklands that once held large populations at the moment of Starfall. The negative energy associated with the collective extinction of so much life has warped these worms so that they embody the misery and death caused by the cataclysm. They are unique among cave worms in that they prefer not to swallow prey alive, but to eat only dead flesh. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 33 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 [Gray Worm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2704) **Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 67 **Perception** +19; blood scent, [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 100 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +24, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +19 **Str** +7 **Dex** -1 **Con** +7 **Int** -3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -1 **Blood Scent** The gray worm can smell blood in the air from up to 1 mile away, or in the earth from up to 1,000 feet away. --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +16 **Will** +19 **HP** 248 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Weaknesses** positive 10 **Inexorable** The gray worm recovers from the [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35), and [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) conditions at the end of its turn. It's also immune to penalties to its Speeds and the [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) condition, and it ignores [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) and greater difficult terrain.
**Shake It Off** **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** The gray worm would be affected by a condition or adverse effect (such as [_baleful polymorph_](/Spells.aspx?ID=17)); **Effect** The gray worm negates the triggering condition or effect. Effects from artifacts, deities, or a similarly powerful source can't be avoided in this way. --- **Speed** 20 feet, burrow 40 feet **Melee** jaws +24 ([deadly 1d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d10+10 piercing plus 2d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Melee** stinger +24 ([deadly 1d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+10 piercing plus paralysis **Melee** body +22 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+9 bludgeoning **Paralysis** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Any living creature hit by a gray worm's stinger Strike must succeed at a DC 27 Fortitude save or become [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28). It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each such save. **Thrash** The worm makes a Strike once against each creature in its reach. It can Strike up to once with its jaws and any number of times with its body. Each attack counts toward the worm's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after it makes all the attacks.
name: Gray Worm

A juvenile cave worm has left its brood or eaten them, and has started to establish its own territory, hunting in regular intervals. These young worms tend to be more aggressive towards other life forms than their relatively mellow adult kin. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Juvenile Cave Worm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2705) **Source** [Pathfinder #195: Heavy is the Crown](/Sources.aspx?ID=210) pg. 84 **Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 100 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +14 **Str** +6 **Dex** +0 **Con** +6 **Int** -5 **Wis** -2 **Cha** -1 --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +14 **Will** +10 **HP** 170 **Relentless** When a juvenile cave worm gains the [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35), or [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) condition, reduce the duration by half, to a minimum of 1 round. It also recovers from penalties to its Speeds and the [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) condition at the end of its turn. Finally, it ignores non-magical [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) and treats greater difficult terrain like difficult terrain.
**Shake It Off** **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** The juvenile cave worm would be affected by a condition or adverse effect (such as [_baleful polymorph_](/Spells.aspx?ID=17)); **Effect** The juvenile cave worm negates the triggering condition or effect. Effects from [artifacts](/Traits.aspx?ID=253), deities, or a similarly powerful source can't be avoided in this way. --- **Speed** 30 feet, burrow 30 feet, swim 20 feet **Melee** jaws +20 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d12+9 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** body +18, **Damage** 1d8+10 bludgeoning **Clumsy Leap** The cave worm flings its body, [Leaping](/Actions.aspx?ID=81) 30 feet. If it lands next to a creature, it can Strike that creature with its jaws. If the jaws Strike fails, it can Strike with its body using the same multiple attack penalty as the failed jaws Strike, but it becomes [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the start of its next turn. **Killing Shake** **Requirements** The juvenile cave worm has a Medium or smaller creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20); **Effect** The juvenile cave worm shakes the creature back and forth quickly, tearing flesh with its multiple rows of teeth. The target takes 2d12+9 slashing damage and is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). If the creature is adjacent to a solid surface, such as a stone wall, it takes an additional 2d6 bludgeoning damage. The creature attempts a DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against all damage; if the creature succeeds, it isn't knocked prone. **Rock Tunneler** A juvenile cave worm can burrow through solid stone at a Speed of 10 feet. It can leave a tunnel if it desires, and it usually does. **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** Medium, 1d8+10 bludgeoning, Rupture 18
Brood Variance The cave worms presented here are typical of standard rock worms. For crimson worms, use the [elite](/Rules.aspx?ID=790) adjustment and add immunity to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72). For azure worms, increase their swim Speed to 40. Gray worms gain [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42) and a weakness to [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) energy 5. Glacial worms gain immunity to [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27).
name: Juvenile Cave Worm

markdown:
While gestating, cave worms adapt to their surrounding environments, becoming accustomed to extreme temperatures and hazards nearby. For example, cave worms hatched in volcanic environments mature into crimson worms, while those raised near water sources mature into azure worms. Regardless of their environment, larval cave worms all have translucent bodies and pallid carapaces that harden and change color as they mature. When a brood of cave worms hatches from their eggs, the foot-long worms mass together, forming a swarm of ravenous larval worms. This swarm travels ceaselessly, consuming anything in its path. The young are drawn to movement, as living, squirming prey tends to be the most nutritious. As they age, they incorporate more minerals into their diet to support carapace growth. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Larval Cave Worm Brood](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2706) **Source** [Pathfinder #195: Heavy is the Crown](/Sources.aspx?ID=210) pg. 85 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 100 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +6 **Int** -5 **Wis** -2 **Cha** -2 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +19 **Will** +12 **HP** 220 **Immunities** precision, [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=36) **Resistances** bludgeoning 7, piercing 13, slashing 13 **Weaknesses** area damage 13, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 13 **Relentless** When a larval cave worm brood gains the [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35), or [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) condition, reduce the duration by half, to a minimum of 1 round. They also recover from penalties to their Speeds and the [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) condition at the end of their turn. Finally, they ignore non-magical [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) and treat greater difficult terrain like difficult terrain.
**Shake It Off** **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** The larval cave worm brood would be affected by a condition or adverse effect (such as [_baleful polymorph_](/Spells.aspx?ID=17)); **Effect** The larval brood negates the triggering condition or effect. Effects from [artifacts](/Traits.aspx?ID=253), deities, or a similarly powerful source can't be avoided in this way. --- **Speed** 30 feet, burrow 30 feet, swim 20 feet **Ferocious Gnaw** Each foe in the larval swarm's space takes 6d6 piercing damage (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). **Pummeling Thrash** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) **Requirements** The larval swarm's most recent action was Ferocious Gnaw; **Effect** The larval swarm frantically thrashes in an effort to consume its prey. The larval swarm attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check and compares the result to the Reflex DC of each creature in its space.
**Critical Success** The creature falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) and take 4d6 bludgeoning damage.
**Success** The creature falls prone and takes 2d6 bludgeoning damage.
Early Hatching A single cave worm egg can produce several thousand larval cave worms when it slowly matures over a millennia. However, if a cave worm egg is disturbed or damaged before its natural cycle completes, the egg enters a rapid stage of maturation. Only a few hundred worms survive this process, creating significantly fewer offspring than when allowed to mature slowly.
name: Larval Cave Worm Brood

In secret conclaves around Nar-Voth, hidden in plain sight within hryngar settlements, are the workshops and training grounds for the Darklands' most technologically sophisticated group of killers. Most hryngar assassins train from childhood, having been recruited from orphanages, and are under a life-debt obligation to their guild leader. While on contract, hryngar assassins are on the lookout for new technologies, gadgets, and alchemical creations. They bring what they find back to their guild to be reverse engineered and replicated for other guild assassins. When the opportunity presents itself, they destroy the schematics and formulae that other organizations, inventors, and alchemists have created if they can't steal them. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 25 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 [Hryngar Assassin](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2707) **Source** [Pathfinder #195: Heavy is the Crown](/Sources.aspx?ID=210) pg. 86 **Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +17, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +19, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +14, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +17 **Str** +2 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** +4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 **Items** [clockwork grappling gun](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1099), [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3), [extreme climbing kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=14), [Hunting Spider Venom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=118) (5 doses applied to first magazine), +1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [repeating heavy crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=178) (4 magazines with 5 bolts each) --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +17 **Will** +12 +2 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) **HP** 115 **[Light Blindness](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=22)**
**Disengaging Shot** **Requirements** The hryngar assassin is equipped with a loaded repeating heavy crossbow; **Trigger** A creature critically misses the hryngar assassin with a melee Strike; **Effect** The hryngar assassin Strides, then Strikes the triggering creature with their repeating heavy crossbow; the target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against this Strike. --- **Speed** 25 feet; climb 10 feet **Melee** dagger +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d4+8 piercing **Ranged** _repeating heavy crossbow_ +18 ([range 180 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [Repeating](/Traits.aspx?ID=374)), **Damage** 2d10+2 piercing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 25 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=286) - **2nd** [Blood Vendetta](/Spells.aspx?ID=675), [Paranoia](/Spells.aspx?ID=214) **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The hryngar assassin deals an extra 2d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures. **Surprise Attack** On the first round of combat, creatures that haven't acted yet are [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to the hryngar assassin.
Bleeding Edge Killing Tech Hryngar assassins use the most advanced gadgets and technology available to them. Their inventors are hard at work reverse engineering any specialized equipment they find so they can provide moderately equivalent designs to the best assassins. Elite assassins gain one [consumable gadget](/Equipment.aspx?Category=15&Subcategory=71) of their level or lower, often selected for a specific job.
name: Hryngar Assassin

A hryngar breccia squad is a group of hryngar martial combatants armed with melee weapons and steel shields. Though more likely to be encountered near large hryngar settlements, these strike forces see wide use throughout the Darklands. Hryngar breccia squads often serve as military units, mercenary companies, city patrols, and caravan guards. Like all warriors, the favored combat methods of a hryngar breccia squad change by location, often due to surrounding environment and popular equipment. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Hryngar Breccia Squad](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2708) **Source** [Pathfinder #195: Heavy is the Crown](/Sources.aspx?ID=210) pg. 87 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +16, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +18, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +18 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +15 **Will** +18 +2 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 150 **Weaknesses** area damage12, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 6 **[Light Blindness](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=22)**

**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32)** --- **Speed** 20 feet; troop movement **Bolt Barrage** The hryngar breccia squad draws or reloads its crossbows, then launches a ranged attack in the form of a volley. This volley is a 10-foot burst within 120 feet that deals 4d8 piercing damage (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). When the hryngar breccia squad is reduced to 8 or fewer squares, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **[Form Up](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=43)** **General Melee** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The hryngar breccia squad engages in a coordinated attack with its melee weapons against each enemy within 5 feet (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.

3d8+14 bludgeoning damage **Push Back** **Requirements** The hryngar breccia squad has its shields raised; **Effect** The hryngar breccia squad pushes against adjacent foes, knocking them back. The hryngar breccia squad attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38), comparing the result to the Fortitude DC of each enemy within 5 feet of the hryngar breccia squad. The hryngar breccia squad is not knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) on a critical failure. **Raise Shields** The hryngar breccia squad raises [steel shields](/Shields.aspx?ID=3), with the effects of [Raise a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=98). **Troop Movement** Whenever the hryngar breccia squad Strides, it first [Forms Up](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=43) as a free action to condense into a 20-foot-by-20-foot area (minus any missing squares), then moves up to its Speed. This works just like a Gargantuan creature moving; for instance, if any square of the hryngar breccia squad enters [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453), the extra movement cost applies to the entire hryngar breccia squad.
Rift Jumpers Hryngar breccia squads that specialize in spelunking are known as Rift Jumpers, named for their reckless leaps into perilous chasms. They swap their shields and warhammers for [light picks](/Weapons.aspx?ID=30) and train with their climbing equipment until it serves as an extension of their body. Rift Jumpers lose the Raise Shields, Shield Block, and Push Back actions, and gain a climb Speed of 20 feet.
name: Hryngar Breccia Squad

markdown:
A servant of [Torag](/Deities.aspx?ID=18), Stoneriver resembles a pangolin with glowing blue eyes and brassy, rune-covered scales. Stoneriver burrows through Golarion's mantle and crust as he hunts down subterranean fiends, aids virtuous communities, and defends sacred sites, often collaborating with dwarves. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Stoneriver](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2709) **Source** [Pathfinder #195: Heavy is the Crown](/Sources.aspx?ID=210) pg. 88 **Perception** +23; (33 to detect lies and illusions) [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet **Languages** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +20, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +26, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +21, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +20, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +23, [Torag Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +24 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +7 **Int** +4 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +20 **Will** +21 +2 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 220 , regeneration 30 (deactivated by [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) or [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64)) **Immunities** [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) **Weaknesses** cold 10 **Bolster Torag's Chosen** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) 20 feet. [Dwarves](/Traits.aspx?ID=54) and creatures that worship [Torag](/Deities.aspx?ID=18) in the area gain a +1 status bonus to AC and Will saves.
**[Retributive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=31)** --- **Speed** 30 feet, burrow 30 feet, swim 30 feet **Melee** tongue +26 ([Good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d6+11 bludgeoning plus 1d6 good and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** claw +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+11 slashing plus 1d6 good and 1d6 fire **Melee** tail +26 ([Backswing](/Traits.aspx?ID=173), [fatal d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=178), [Good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+11 slashing plus 1d6 good and 1d6 fire **Divine Innate Spells** DC 29, attack +24 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171) - **1st** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will; evil only) - **2nd** [Remove Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=252) (at will), [Restoration](/Spells.aspx?ID=258) - **3rd** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) (×2) - **4th** [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86), [Searing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=269) (×2) - **5th** [Blazing Fissure](/Spells.aspx?ID=870), [Flame Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=120) - **Constant (5th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Devour Magic** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) Whenever Stoneriver deals damage with [Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35), he consumes one of the swallowed creature's magical effects, as [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) with a +21 [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) modifier. **[Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27)** **Measure Soul** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) Stoneriver mentally pries through the mind of a creature within 60 feet, dealing 10d8 mental damage (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save). Stoneriver can choose to make this damage nonlethal or choose not to damage the creature at all. For 1 minute, Stoneriver gains a +1 circumstance bonus to attack rolls, damage rolls, and Perception checks against that creature. If the creature is evil and it failed its save, the creature is additionally [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (frightened 4 on a critical failure). Stoneriver can't use Measure Soul again for 1d4 rounds. **Rock Tunneler** Stoneriver can burrow through solid stone, leaving behind a tunnel if he desires. **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) Large, 6d6 bludgeoning plus 2d6 fire and devour magic, Rupture 40
Forgeheart Five Stoneriver has fathered numerous children through the years, each clever, brave, and bold. Among these, five have joined together to combat evil as a team. Calling themselves the Forgeheart Five, these overly optimistic and bombastic creatures strive to be as virtuous and heroic as their father. Thus far, their methods have been more flashy than effective.
name: Stoneriver

markdown:
Toilforged sentinels are intelligent war machines painstakingly constructed from iron, steel, and other metals over the course of a full year. With twin treads, a central cage filled with eternal flames, and numerous weapons (including a hammer, cannon, and grasping claw capable of snatching up foes), a toilforged sentinel is an intimidating sight. Creatures consumed by a sentinel's flames become captives, their souls eternally bound to the flames, powering the device. Such souls are freed upon the sentinel's destruction. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Toilforged Sentinel](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2710) **Source** [Pathfinder #195: Heavy is the Crown](/Sources.aspx?ID=210) pg. 89 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), find the faithless ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet **Languages** [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18, [Droskar Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +13 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** -1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 **Find the Faithless** ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) The toilforged sentinel detects the presence of creatures that don't worship [Droskar](/Deities.aspx?ID=28) within 60 feet as an imprecise sense. --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +13 **Will** +12 **HP** 130 **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poisoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374) or [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=378)) **Weaknesses** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 6, [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) 11, vulnerability to exorcism **Exertion Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 20 feet. A toilforged sentinel saps the energy of nearby creatures. Each round a living creature begins its turn in the aura, it must attempt a DC 22 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature takes 1d6 mental damage (2d6 on a critical failure) and becomes [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15). This fatigue ends as soon as the creature remains outside of the aura for 1 minute.
**Vulnerability to Exorcism** When a toilforged sentinel fails a saving throw against a spell that would banish tormented spirits or manipulate souls, such as [_banishment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=19) or [_bind soul_](/Spells.aspx?ID=21), instead of experiencing the listed effect, it takes 10 damage per spell level. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** grasping claw +16 ([Grapple](/Traits.aspx?ID=184), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Tethered](/Traits.aspx?ID=252)), **Damage** 2d8+11 slashing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** hammer +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)), **Damage** 2d12+11 bludgeoning **Ranged** flaming cannon +17 ([range increment 200 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d10 bludgeoning and 1d6 fire **Droskar's Grasp** The toilforged sentinel can Grab a creature using only one hand. It can move normally with a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) in its claw, carrying the creature along. If it has a creature grabbed in this way, it can't use its claw Strike. It activates Swallow Whole with its claw by depositing a grabbed creature into its soul furnace. **Soul Furnace** The toilforged sentinel has a cage incorporated into its central body, filled with profane fire and called a soul furnace. As long as a toilforged sentinels' soul furnace contains a living creature, the toilforged sentinel is [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) (Stride or Strike). **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) Large, 3d6 fire and 3d6 negative, Rupture 21
Salvation Toilforged sentinels are religious constructs created to destroy heretics: those who refuse to worship Droskar. They take orders only from the clergy of Droskar and find particular satisfaction from consuming worshippers of [Torag](/Deities.aspx?ID=18) and other dwarven deities.
name: Toilforged Sentinel

**Jaws** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) **Requirements** Four-Tooth has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33); **Effect** Four-Tooth makes a jaws unarmed Strike against the grabbed or restrained target with an attack modifier of +19 that deals 1d8+5 piercing damage, deals 3d6 poison damage, and exposes the target to the [sewer haze](/Diseases.aspx?ID=11) disease.

**Slashing Snare** <actions string="Two Actions" />  Four-Tooth can Step and then make a spiked chain Strike. If it hits and deals damage, he can attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) the target. Both attacks count toward his multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after he's made both attacks.

**Snagging Chain**   Four-Tooth can use a spiked chain to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) with the [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) skill even if he doesn't have a free hand, and he adds the weapon's item bonus to attack rolls to the Athletics check.

</column>
name: Four-Tooth

Each attack counts toward the worm's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after it makes all its attacks.

**Vomit Worms** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The statue disgorges a [bore worm swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1083) into an adjacent 10-foot-by-10-foot space. Creatures in the swarm's area are immediately affected by the bore worm swarm's Swarming Bites, after which the bore worm swarm rolls initiative and acts independently before disappearing after 1d6 minutes.

**Sheltered Command** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Trigger** The battlepriest is targeted with an attack while adjacent to or sharing a space with a [hryngar](/Traits.aspx?ID=53); **Effect** The battlepriest commands the hryngar to intercept the attack. The hryngar becomes the target of the attack. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** _light hammer_ +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 2d6+8 bludgeoning **Ranged** _light hammer_ +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 2d6+8 bludgeoning **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 28 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=84), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) - **2nd** [See Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) - **3rd** [Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=26), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Heroism](/Spells.aspx?ID=149) - **4th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86), [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) - **5th** [Flame Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=120), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) (×3), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Spiritual Guardian](/Spells.aspx?ID=305) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 28 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=286) - **2nd** [Paranoia](/Spells.aspx?ID=214) - **4th** [Blood Vendetta](/Spells.aspx?ID=675) **Part the Troops** The hryngar battlepriest and their mount can share the same space as creatures with both the [hryngar](/Traits.aspx?ID=53) and [troop](/Traits.aspx?ID=367) traits. Such creatures ignore the [Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39) ability of any creature the battlepriest is riding. **Toil and Trample** **Requirements** The battlepriest is mounted on a creature with a [Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39) ability; **Effect** The battlepriest commands their mount. Until the beginning of the battlepriest's next turn, the saving throw DC of the mount's Trample increases to 26, and the Trample deals an additional 3d6 evil damage.

In addition, any time he gains the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition, reduce its value by 1.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32)** --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** _longsword_ +20 ([versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d8+10 slashing **Melee** _shield boss_ +20, **Damage** 2d6+10 bludgeoning **Ranged** light hammer +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 1d6+10 bludgeoning **Double Slice** Hrungul makes a longsword Strike, followed by a shield boss Strike against the same target at a –2 penalty, each using his current multiple attack penalty. If both attacks hit, he combines their damage and applies resistances and weaknesses only once. This counts as two attacks when calculating his multiple attack penalty. **Grim Efficiency** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) **Frequency** once per round; **Trigger** A foe within 30 feet critically fails a Strike against Hrungul or targets Hrungul with an effect against which he critically succeeds at the saving throw; **Effect** Hrungul's defense displays his effortless military skill, unsettling the triggering foe. He attempts to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) the foe, using his [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill modifier in place of his [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) skill. **Quick Block** At the start of his turn, Hrungul gains an additional reaction that he can use only to [Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32).
name: Hrungul Ironeye

Driven by political and magical ambition, this sorcerer has rediscovered a unique spellcasting art used to command [cave worms](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=21). For all its emphasis on personal toil, [hryngar](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=38) culture rewards ensnaring others in a web of obligation and subservience. The more one's subordinates produce, the greater their master's take, so a hryngar who commands a mighty cave worm would be powerful indeed! At least this is what Narseigus believes. His early training to be a miner was interrupted by the emergence of his magical talents. When his arm momentarily and unexpectedly transformed into a tentacle that strangled his mentor, this one murderous act both spared him any education debt to his mentor and set him on a new career path... only for him to become indebted to magical masters who claimed most of his subsequent profits. In his limited spare time, he read avidly, always seeking new inspiration that could advance his career and livelihood. This research eventually unearthed worm calling, an obscure hryngar magical tradition that had fallen out of favor—both due the psychological damage involved and the catastrophic consequences that might ensue should a cave worm break free. Knowing of no living worm callers (or at least unable to indebt himself to even more masters), Narseigus began piecing together the techniques from fragmentary sources. Yet the written word had limits. He increasingly realized that mastering this art required studying cave worms, learning their every instinct to attune his mind to theirs. Absconding without closing out his debts, he embarked on a decades-long expedition throughout Nar-Voth to follow cave worms, hone his spells, and swindle supplies as needed. And then he felt it: the telltale tremors of a cave worm, but massive beyond any he'd ever witnessed. He eagerly tracked the beast, witnessing its trail of destruction and discovering that this was the legendary [crimson worm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=75), Zogototaru. The creature's power captivated him. Why should he settle for controlling lesser worms when he could enthrall such a behemoth? Yet even though Zogototaru had left countless tunnels over the millennia, tracing her whereabouts proved challenging. Narseigus at last identified her lair, though getting to that lair would require traversing dwarven territory and excavating countless tons of stone. To his chagrin, he needed help. Fortunately, King Orgukagan of Hagegraf needed a miracle. With no great deeds to his name, the king knew his reign was unremarkable and his ongoing rule was equally precarious. Thus, when Narseigus proposed a military venture that would both crush Rolgrimmdur and help Narseigus seize a mighty asset to fuel future victories, the king eagerly bankrolled his operation. The sorcerer received everything he needed for his research, including privileged access to the king's ear. The operation's scope steadily expanded, earning Narseigus a coalition army, growing clout, and an impressive list of enemies who resented his rise to power. But for Narseigus, it would all be a means to an end. All that mattered was his magic. Campaign Role For much of the campaign, Narseigus is a sinister mastermind always several steps ahead of the PCs. They gradually learned of him in “Cult of the Cave Worm,” the previous adventure, uncovering his plans to invade Rolgrimmdur, command Zogototaru, and break into the Sky King's Tomb. However, his self-serving objectives have made him many enemies, and the more the PCs uncover, the more of his embittered rivals they meet and might turn against him. Ultimately, he can only rely on his thralls. Yet Narseigus is so driven that he rarely glances back to notice the damage the PCs cause. By the time he does notice, it's too late. He's one of the final fights of the campaign, letting the PCs clash with Zogototaru and orchestrate Narseigus's comeuppance in one exciting scene. Narratively, Narseigus provides time pressure to what could otherwise be a casual expedition. After all, Taargick's tomb has survived over 9,000 years. What's another few months? But with Narseigus and his schemes, finding the tomb and thwarting the sorcerer become intertwined goals, keeping the PCs on the move. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 38 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 [Narseigus Wormcaller](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2719) **Source** [Pathfinder #195: Heavy is the Crown](/Sources.aspx?ID=210) pg. 90 **Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 10 feet **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3), [Orcish](/Languages.aspx?ID=9), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +18, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +19, [Cave Worm Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +24, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +24, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +24, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +24, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +22, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +22, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +20 **Str** +2 **Dex** +1 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +6 **Items** [religious symbol](/Armor.aspx?ID=22), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12)_, [_wand of widening_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=373) (5th-level spell) --- **AC** 31 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +18 **Will** +24 +2 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) **HP** 140 **Inexorable** When Narseigus gains the [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35), or [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) condition, roll a DC 15 flat check. On a success, he ignores the condition.
**[Light Blindness](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=22)**
**Shake It Off** ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** Narseigus fails or critically fails a saving throw against a condition or adverse effect (such as [_baleful polymorph_](/Spells.aspx?ID=17)); Effect Narseigus rerolls the triggering saving throw and uses the better result. --- **Speed** 20 feet, burrow 20 feet, climb 20 feet **Melee** staff +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=198)), **Damage** 2d4+8 bludgeoning **Melee** tail +25 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 1d10+13 bludgeoning **Occult Innate Spells** DC 30 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=286) - **6th** [Blood Vendetta](/Spells.aspx?ID=675), [Paranoia](/Spells.aspx?ID=214) **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 30, attack +24 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) - **1st** [Alarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=7), [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45), [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) (4 slots) - **2nd** [Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=28), [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (4 slots) - **3rd** [Comprehend Language](/Spells.aspx?ID=46), [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=147), [Magic Missile](/Spells.aspx?ID=180) (4 slots) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Enervation](/Spells.aspx?ID=687), [Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=173), [Soothe](/Spells.aspx?ID=291) (4 slots) - **5th** [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=141), [Vampiric Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=354), [Vomit Swarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=726) (4 slots) - **6th** [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=87), [Feeblemind](/Spells.aspx?ID=112), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=289) (3 slots) **Sorcerer Bloodline Spells** DC 30, 3 Focus Points - **6th** [Tentacular Limbs](/Spells.aspx?ID=517), [Aberrant Whispers](/Spells.aspx?ID=491), [Unusual Anatomy](/Spells.aspx?ID=519) **Quickened Casting** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Metamagic](/Traits.aspx?ID=107)) If Narseigus' next action is to cast an occult spontaneous cantrip or an occult spontaneous spell of 4th level or lower, he reduces the number of actions to cast it by 1 (minimum 1 action). **Steady Spellcasting** If a reaction would disrupt Narseigus' spellcasting action, he attempts a DC 15 flat check. On a success, the action isn't disrupted. **Worm Form** ([Morph](/Traits.aspx?ID=113), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) Narseigus partially transforms his lower body into that of a [cave worm](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=21). He can use only his tail unarmed Strike and his burrow and climb Speeds while in this form. This transformation lasts for 1 minute. **Worm's Feast** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Narseigus calls forth a brood of infantile cave worms to feast on his enemy's flesh. One creature within 30 feet must attempt a Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The worms writhe away harmlessly.
**Success** The target takes 2d6 piercing damage and 4d6 [persistent piercing damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
**Failure** The target takes 6d6 piercing damage and 4d6 persistent piercing damage.
**Critical Failure** As failure, plus the target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) as long as it takes persistent piercing damage.
name: Narseigus Wormcaller

<title level="1" pfs="">[Noppera-Bo Grunt](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2720)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Noppera-bo grunts lack the social graces necessary to fully disguise themselves with another person's face.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/NopperaBo_Grunt.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 0">[Noppera-Bo Grunt](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2720)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=1" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 84

**Perception** +4; no face

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 30 feet

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +3, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +5, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +4, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +4

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +2

**Con** +2

**Int** +1

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +2
</row>

**No Face** Noppera-bos have no sense of vision, but their sense of hearing is [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411). They don't breathe and are immune to effects that require breathing. They gain sustenance by absorbing fluids through their skin—traditionally by immersing their hands or faceless head into the fluid to “drink” through osmosis; they can also benefit from [consumables](/Traits.aspx?ID=36), such as [potions](/Traits.aspx?ID=130) or [elixirs](/Traits.aspx?ID=59), in this way.

**Items**
[Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 15 

**Fort** +6 

**Ref** +5 

**Will** +4 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 18

</row>

**Immunities**
[inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=96), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)

**Weaknesses**
sensitive hearing

**Sensitive Hearing** As long as a noppera-bo is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8), they are [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
spear +7,
**Damage** 1d6+1 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
spear +5 ([thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)),
**Damage** 1d6+1 piercing

**Impersonate Shape** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The noppera-bo grunt changes into a faceless version of a Small or Medium [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) they've touched during the last 24 hours. While transformed and their face is covered, the grunt gains a +2 circumstance bonus to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) that creature. The noppera-bo grunt can change back by using this ability again.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-350" />
name: Noppera-Bo Grunt
