<title level="1" pfs="">[Noppera-Bo Trickster](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2721)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These noppera-bos have mastered the art of disguise.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 17

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Noppera-Bo Trickster](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2721)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=1" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 84

**Perception** +5; no face

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 30 feet

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +6, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +4, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +6

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +3

**Con** +2

**Int** +1

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +3
</row>

**No Face** Noppera-bos have no sense of vision, but their sense of hearing is [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411). They don't breathe and are immune to effects that require breathing. They gain sustenance by absorbing fluids through their skin—traditionally by immersing their hands or faceless head into the fluid to “drink” through osmosis; they can also benefit from [consumables](/Traits.aspx?ID=36), such as [potions](/Traits.aspx?ID=130) or [elixirs](/Traits.aspx?ID=59), in this way.

**Items**
[Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=77) (20 arrows), [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 16 

**Fort** +7 

**Ref** +8 

**Will** +5 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 20

</row>

**Immunities**
[inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=96), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)

**Weaknesses**
sensitive hearing

**Sensitive Hearing** As long as a noppera-bo is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8), they are [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shortsword +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 1d6+1 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shortbow +8 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 1d6 piercing

**Impersonate Shape** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The noppera-bo changes into a Small or Medium humanoid they've touched during the last 24 hours, and the noppera-bo gains the target creature's face and voice. While transformed and their face is covered, the noppera-bo gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Impersonate that creature. As long as the noppera-bo has a face, it loses its no face ability and its listed immunities. The noppera-bo can change back by using this ability again.

**Remove Face** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) **Requirements** The noppera-bo trickster is [Impersonating](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) a creature with a face; **Effect** The noppera-bo trickster's facial features melt into nothingness, and they menace a creature within 30 feet. The creature becomes [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) on a failed DC 17 Will save (frightened 2 on a critical failure).

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   The noppera-bo trickster deals an additional 1d4 precision damage to creatures that are [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16).

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-350" />
name: Noppera-Bo Trickster

markdown:
Some noppera-bos harness their natural magic to further frighten victims. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Noppera-Bo Occultist](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2722) **Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 85 **Perception** +8; no face **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 30 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +5, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +7, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +9, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +7, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +7 **Str** +1 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +3 **Wis** +0 **Cha** +3 **No Face** Noppera-bos have no sense of vision, but their sense of hearing is [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411). They don't breathe and are immune to effects that require breathing. They gain sustenance by absorbing fluids through their skin—traditionally by immersing their hands or faceless head into the fluid to “drink” through osmosis; they can also benefit from [consumables](/Traits.aspx?ID=36), such as [potions](/Traits.aspx?ID=130) or [elixirs](/Traits.aspx?ID=59), in this way. **Items** [Staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12) --- **AC** 17 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +9 **Will** +8 **HP** 30 **Immunities** [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=96), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) **Weaknesses** sensitive hearing **Sensitive Hearing** As long as a noppera-bo is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8), they are [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35). --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** staff +7 ([two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=198)), **Damage** 1d4+3 bludgeoning **Occult Innate Spells** DC 18, attack +9 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=229), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) - **1st** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=220), [Sleep](/Spells.aspx?ID=288) **Remove Face** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) **Requirements** The noppera-bo is [Impersonating](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) a creature with a face; **Effect** The noppera-bo occultist's facial features melt into nothingness, and they menace a creature within 30 feet. The creature becomes [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) on a failed DC 18 Will save (frightened 2 on a critical failure). **Impersonate Shape** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The noppera-bo changes into a Small or Medium humanoid they've touched during the last 24 hours, and the noppera-bo gains the target creature's face and voice. While transformed and their face is covered, the noppera-bo gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Impersonate that creature. As long as the noppera-bo has a face, it loses its no face ability and its listed immunities. The noppera-bo can change back by using this ability again. **Steal Face** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** The noppera-bo occultist is faceless; **Effect** The noppera-bo occultist targets one creature within 30 feet. The creature must attempt a DC 18 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until the start of its next turn.
**Failure** The noppera-bo gains the target's face. The target's face vanishes until the end of its next turn. Until then, it is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1), can't speak or ingest, and can't smell. The target must also hold its breath or begin to [suffocate](/Rules.aspx?ID=468).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the effect doesn't end automatically. The target can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each such attempt.
name: Noppera-Bo Occultist

<title level="1" pfs="">[Shinigami](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2723)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Even immortals such as [kami](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=243) aren't immune to death. When a kami or equally powerful entity refuses the call of the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15), a shinigami is summoned to bring these stubborn souls to heel. Exact and swift, shinigami are known to dispense the judgment of death efficiently and dispassionately.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 38

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Shinigami.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 17">[Shinigami](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2723)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="LN" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Monitor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=111" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 86

**Perception** +31; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_detect alignment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (chaotic and lawful only), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344)

**Languages**
[Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34), [Utopian](/Languages.aspx?ID=36); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +31, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +30, [Boneyard Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +32, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +32, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +34, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +30, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +33, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +30, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +31

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +8

**Dex** +6

**Con** +7

**Int** +5

**Wis** +6

**Cha** +9
</row>

**Items**
_+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [axiomatic](/Equipment.aspx?ID=291) [naginata](/Weapons.aspx?ID=297)_, pouch of four gems

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 40 

**Fort** +32 

**Ref** +29 

**Will** +31 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 260
([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) 40)

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)

**Resistances**
all physical 15 (except chaotic and silver), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 15, [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) 15

**Weaknesses**
[silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379) 15

**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 60 feet, DC 35.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, fly 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_naginata_ +33 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 3d8+16 slashing plus 1d6 lawful and spirit touch

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 38
- **7th**
[Enervation](/Spells.aspx?ID=687) (×3), [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222)
- **8th**
[Finger of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=116) (×3), [Scrying](/Spells.aspx?ID=268)
- **9th**
[Bind Soul](/Spells.aspx?ID=21) (×3)
- **Constant (9th)**
[Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (chaotic and lawful only), [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344)

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The shinigami takes on the appearance of a specific Small or Medium [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) creature. This doesn't change their statistics, other than to remove the [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) trait from their Strikes. The shinigami's human shape is unique to the shinigami and can't be used to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) a creature.

**Pouch of Four Gems**   Each shinigami carries a pouch containing four black sapphires for use with [_bind soul_](/Spells.aspx?ID=21). Each gem is capable of holding a soul of a maximum level equal to that of the shinigami. If the shinigami dies, the gems are destroyed as well, and any souls they contain are released harmlessly to travel to the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15).

**Release Soul** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Requirements** One of the gems in the shinigami's pouch contains a soul from bind soul; **Effect** The shinigami crushes the gem, sending the soul to the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15) while releasing a wave of necromantic energy. All creatures within a 30-foot emanation take 18d6 damage (DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save); the shinigami chooses whether this damage is positive damage or negative damage. A creature who takes damage from this effect and fails the saving throw is also [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) and [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (or sickened 4 and drained 2 on a critical failure).

**Spirit Touch**   A shinigami's Strikes can affect [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creatures with the effect of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Guardians of the Cycle</title>

Shinigami are responsible for investigating and dealing with widespread phenomena that disrupt the natural flow of life. They might be tasked with solving the issue or be told to wait, observe, and provide minimal assistance. While most shinigami can retain their impartiality during such long vigils, some are far from emotionless and end up wavering toward mercy or cruelty
</aside>
name: Shinigami

markdown:
The tradition of venerating spiders as wards against vermin, pests, and evil in general far predates the modern [jorogumo](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1200) rule, with stone spiders filling a role similar to [stone lions](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1183). Stone spider guardian beasts can be found perched on plinths near town and village entrances, standing motionless—for generations in some cases—so that many locals have forgotten their local guardian is anything more than a decoration. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 25 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 [Stone Spider](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2724) **Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 87 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 60 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +12, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +9, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +14, [Settlement Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +12, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +10 **Str** +4 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +0 **Anchored Soul** The stone spider is bonded to a specific settlement and must remain within 10 miles of the settlement's border. --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +12 **Will** +14 **HP** 62 **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) **Bonded Vessel** The condition of a stone spider's vessel dictates its maximum HP. Undamaged, the vessel is an object with 62 HP (BT 31). When the stone spider is in spirit form, damaging it doesn't hurt the vessel, but damaging the vessel deals an equal amount of damage to the stone spider. When the stone spider Inhabits its Vessel, they're a single target, and damage reduces the HP of both the stone spider and the vessel. If the vessel is [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2), the stone spider can still fight while inhabiting it and suffers no ill effect, but if the vessel is ever destroyed, the stone spider is instantly slain and can't reconstitute.
**Reconstitution** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When the stone spider reaches 0 Hit Points, its spirit dissipates. If its bonded vessel is intact, the stone spider re-forms in this vessel after 2d4 days, fully healed. If the vessel is [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2), it must first be [Repaired](/Actions.aspx?ID=42), after which the stone spider re-forms in 3d4 days. --- **Speed** fly 40 feet **Ranged** spirit web +14 ([range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** spirit web trap **Divine Innate Spells** DC 21 - **1st** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will; evil only) - **3rd** [Dream Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=90) **Inhabit Vessel** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) The spider touches and melds with its bonded vessel, bringing it to life. It can cease Inhabiting its Vessel by spending a single action, which has the concentrate trait. While Inhabiting the Vessel, it loses its fly Speed and gains Immunities healing, nonlethal; Resistances physical 6 (except bludgeoning); Speed 25 feet, climb 25 feet; and the following Strike.
  • **Melee** stone fangs +14 ([finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d8+7 piercing
**Spirit Body** When not Inhabiting its Vessel, the stone spider is [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) and gains resistance 6 to all damage (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75) damage and [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297); double resistance against non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)). **Spirit Web Trap** ([Force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75)) A creature hit by the stone spider's web attack is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) and stuck to the nearest surface until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 22).
Mindscape Perks When Ugly Cute, the stone spider who protects Willowshore, is slain in the modern era and their bonded vessel is destroyed, they don't dissipate. Instead, they awaken in Willowshore's mindscape, fully merged with their vessel. While in the mindscape, Ugly Cute can move their stone body as if it were a living body. They function at all times as if Inhabiting their Vessel and they are unable to cease Inhabiting their Vessel. If Ugly Cute is destroyed in the mindscape, they remain destroyed and can't reconstitute, although if the mindscape were to reset, Ugly Cute would return to life as well, once more fully Inhabiting their Vessel.
name: Stone Spider

The typical thatchling appears as a humanoid bundle of dry grass and twigs, held together by coils of what looks like glowing blue hair. These minor undead can't form bodies larger than those of a small human child. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 16 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 [Thatchling](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2725) **Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 88 **Perception** +6; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +5, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +5 **Str** +0 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 15 **Fort** +4 **Ref** +7 **Will** +6 **HP** 16 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** bludgeoning 2, piercing 2 **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 5 **Distracting Giggle** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) 15 feet. The thatchling's giggle echoes in the back of the mind. Creatures within the aura take a –1 status penalty to Will saves.
**Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When a thatchling is destroyed by damage other than fire, it attempts a DC 11 flat check. On a success, it reforms after 2d4 days within 120 feet of where it was destroyed, fully healed. A thatchling is always permanently destroyed if reduced to 0 Hit Points by fire damage or if the obstacle preventing the soul from reincarnating is removed. --- **Speed** 30 feet; thicket walker **Melee** frond +5 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 1d6 slashing **Thatchling's Curse** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The thatchling focuses its attention on a single target within 30 feet that it can see, and then begins muttering and whispering nonsense. The target must attempt a DC 16 Will save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Thatchling's Curse for 24 hours.
**Success** The target is momentarily disoriented and has difficulty moving in the right direction. The target takes a –5-foot status penalty to its Speeds for 1 round.
**Failure** As success, but the penalty to its Speeds lasts for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the penalty to its Speeds lasts until the curse is removed or until the thatchling that created the effect is permanently destroyed, whichever comes first. In addition, when the cursed creature attempts a [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) check to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=64) or [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66), they use the outcome for one degree of success worse than the result of their check, and they can't take the [Scout](/Actions.aspx?ID=518) exploration activity. **Thicket Walker** Thatchlings ignore difficult terrain caused by brambles, tangled grass, and undergrowth, even those manipulated by magic.
name: Thatchling

markdown:
When a soul doomed to become a thatchling dies in the throes of great anguish, they can return after death as a red-hooded thatchling. These vengeful thatchlings appear to wear red hoods when viewed at a distance, but up close, the grisly truth—that their heads are blood-soaked—is revealed. Unlike other thatchlings, red-hooded thatchlings are skilled at trickery, and their ability to appear as a living [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) through illusion magic allows them to lure the unexpected into ambushes much more easily. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 21 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 [Red-Hooded Thatchling](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2726) **Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 89 **Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +7 **Str** +0 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 17 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +9 **Will** +8 **HP** 32 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** bludgeoning 3, piercing 3 **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 5 **Distracting Giggle** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) 15 feet. The thatchling's giggle echoes in the back of the mind. Creatures within the aura take a –1 status penalty to Will saves.
**Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When a thatchling is destroyed by damage other than fire, it attempts a DC 11 flat check. On a success, it reforms after 2d4 days within 120 feet of where it was destroyed, fully healed. A thatchling is always permanently destroyed if reduced to 0 Hit Points by fire damage or if the obstacle preventing the soul from reincarnating is removed. --- **Speed** 30 feet; thicket walker **Melee** frond +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 1d6+2 slashing plus 1d4 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 18, attack +10 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **1st** [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=159) **Create Thatchling Spawn** ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) A living [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) who's slain by a red-hooded thatchling while under the effects of Thatchling's Curse rises as a thatchling after 1d4 rounds. This spawn is under the command of the red-hooded thatchling that killed it. If a red-hooded thatchling controls too many at once (as determined by the GM), some thatchlings can free themselves with a DC 18 Will saving throw at the start of their turn. If the red-hooded thatchling who created the spawn is destroyed, the spawn becomes an autonomous thatchling. **Howl of Vengeful Fury** ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** The red-hooded thatchling sobs pitifully before unleashing a terrifying scream. All non-thatchling creatures within a 30-foot emanation take 2d10 sonic damage (DC 18 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save) and become immune to all thatchlings' distracting giggle for 1 minute. Thatchlings in the area gain 5 temporary Hit Points and a +1 status bonus to their Strikes and saving throws for 1 minute. **Thatchling's Curse** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The thatchling focuses its attention on a single target within 30 feet that it can see, and then begins muttering and whispering nonsense. The target must attempt a DC 18 Will save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Thatchling's Curse for 24 hours.
**Success** The target is momentarily disoriented and has difficulty moving in the right direction. The target takes a –5-foot status penalty to its Speeds for 1 round.
**Failure** As success, but the penalty to its Speeds lasts for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the penalty to its Speeds lasts until the curse is removed or until the thatchling that created the effect is permanently destroyed, whichever comes first. In addition, when the cursed creature attempts a [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) check to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=64) or [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66), they use the outcome for one degree of success worse than the result of their check, and they can't take the [Scout](/Actions.aspx?ID=518) exploration activity. **Thicket Walker** Thatchlings ignore difficult terrain caused by brambles, tangled grass, and undergrowth, even those manipulated by magic.
name: Red-Hooded Thatchling

markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="">[Vloriak (Despoiler Demon)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2727)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Vloriaks rise from the sinful souls of those whose acts of sabotage resulted in despair and terror, and they delight in the destruction of mortal artifice. Often called despoiler demons, vloriaks were the followers of Xar-Azmak. Their numbers have dwindled over the eons since the Lord of Rust's death; those that exist now are scattered throughout the [Abyss](/Planes.aspx?ID=13). Their chitinous bodies and faceted eyes give them an insectile appearance.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Demon_Vloriak.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 5">[Vloriak](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2727)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Demon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=42" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=70" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rusthenge](/Sources.aspx?ID=215) pg. 62

**Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +12

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +2

**Con** +4

**Int** -1

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +3
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 21 

**Fort** +15 

**Ref** +11 

**Will** +13 

+1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 90

</row>

**Resistances**
[acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3) 5

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=272) 5, good 5

**Restoration Vulnerability** A vloriak suffers agonizing pain when a creature or object recovers from a debilitating effect in its proximity. The first time in a round in which a creature who is affected by a negative condition and is within sight of the demon reduces that condition's value, the demon takes 3d6 mental damage and cannot Lick Rust on its next turn.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +15,
**Damage** 2d6+6 slashing plus 1d6 evil

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tongue +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d6 acid plus 1d6 evil and rust

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 22, attack +14
- **Cantrips (3rd)**
[Acid Splash](/Spells.aspx?ID=3)
- **2nd**
[Acid Arrow](/Spells.aspx?ID=2) (×3), [Shatter](/Spells.aspx?ID=279)
- **3rd**
[Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=213)

**Lick Rust** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) **Requirements** The vloriak rusted a metal item with its tongue this turn; **Effect** The vloriak attempts a tongue Strike on the same target it just attacked. If it hits, it deals no damage as it licks away the rust and heals 2d6 Hit Points (or 4d6 Hit Points if the Strike was a critical hit). It can't Lick Rust on its next turn.

**Rust**   A vloriak's saliva causes metal to rust rapidly. If it succeeds at a tongue Strike or [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) attempt, the vloriak deals 2d6 damage (doubled on a critical hit) to a metal item the target is wearing or holding, ignoring its Hardness. If the vloriak hits an unattended metal item, the item takes this damage automatically. If a creature uses the [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) reaction with a metal shield against a tongue attack, the shield is automatically [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2), but no other item is rusted on that attack.

**Spew Rusted Shards** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The vloriak spews a 15-foot cone of acid and rusted metal. Creatures in the area take 3d6 acid and 3d6 piercing damage (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). A creature that takes any piercing damage is exposed to [tetanus](/Diseases.aspx?ID=3). The vloriak can't Spew Rusted Shards for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Vlorian Influence</title>

Vloriaks hail from the otherworldly realm of Vlorus, and as such carry within themselves the potential for decay and rusting ruin. Other creatures that dwell in Vlorus— particularly fiends who rise to power there—can gain similar powers over rust as well. Fiends of any type can become infested with unique rust-themed abilities if they hail from this otherworldly realm.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-28" />
name: Vloriak

The primordial envy is most often found in underground ruins within Edasseril. It hungers for magic, leaching it from any source it can come into contact with. This hunger can be exploited by clever adventurers who bait it with powerful destructive magics. A primordial envy is one of the most defensive sin oozes, and it tends to layer [abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2) spells over itself to both increase its resilience and empower its attacks. **[Recall Knowledge - Ooze](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Primordial Envy](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2728) **Source** [Rusthenge](/Sources.aspx?ID=215) pg. 63 **Perception** +6; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), magic sense ([precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411)) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +9 **Str** +2 **Dex** -4 **Con** +5 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 **Magic Sense** A primordial envy can detect [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) auras, items, and spellcasters within 30 feet. --- **AC** 12 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +4 **Will** +8 –2 status vs. [evocation](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1671) and [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117) **HP** 60 **Immunities** critical hits, [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) **Resistances** piercing 5, slashing 5 **Confiscate Spell** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11)) **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet that the primordial envy can sense Casts a Spell; **Effect** The primordial envy's surface shimmers with a kaleidoscope of color that shines out to illuminate the triggering creature. The primordial envy attempts to counter the spell, with a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) modifier of +12. On a success, the spell is countered and the primordial envy gains 2d6 temporary Hit Points. --- **Speed** 15 feet **Melee** pseudopod +9 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 1d10+4 bludgeoning plus 1d6 mental and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Spell Drain** ([Abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11)) The primordial envy attempts to drain magic from a spellcasting creature that it has [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33). The spellcasting creature must attempt a DC 20 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Spell Drain for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature takes 1d6 mental damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 2d6 mental damage and becomes [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 hour.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but one of the creature's uncast spells or unused spell slots is lost, as if they had cast it. The spell or slot lost is one of the highest-level spells the creature can cast, determined randomly if multiple prepared spells are available. If a cantrip is drained, the spellcaster loses access to that cantrip for 10 minutes. The primordial envy becomes [quickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) for 1 round, and can use the extra action to Stride or Strike.
name: Primordial Envy

**[Recall Knowledge - Ethereal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 21 **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 21 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 [Phantom Boar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2729) **Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 15 **Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +6 **Str** -5 **Dex** +1 **Con** +4 **Int** -3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Walk the Ethereal Line** The phantom walks the thin line between the [Ethereal](/Equipment.aspx?ID=284) and [Material Planes](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) in order to exist on both planes simultaneously. They can shift back to solely the Ethereal Plane by using this ability again. --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +5 **Will** +8 **HP** 22 **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision **Resistances** all damage 2 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75) or [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) **Susceptible to Death** Though phantoms aren't alive, neither are they [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160), and they are uniquely vulnerable to the effects of death. A phantom whose Hit Points are reduced to 0 as a result of a [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effect (such as from a spell like [_finger of death_](/Spells.aspx?ID=116)) is immediately whisked away to the River of Souls, where their soul resumes the usual path to the afterlife.
**Vulnerable to Kindness** Any creature within 30 feet that the phantom can see and hear can issue a calm and friendly greeting to it as a two-action activity with the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) traits. A creature greeting the phantom in this manner must attempt a DC 18 [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) check.
**Critical Success** The phantom takes 4d8 mental damage and is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
**Success** The phantom takes 2d8 mental damage.
**Failure** The phantom takes 1d8 mental damage.
**Critical Failure** The phantom is unaffected, and is not affected by this weakness for 1 round. --- **Speed** fly 40 feet **Melee** tusk +7 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 1d8+3 force **Occult Innate Spells** DC 15, attack +7 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132) - **1st** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110) **Boar Charge** The phantom boar Strides twice and then makes a tusk Strike. As long as it moved at least 20 feet, it gains a +2 circumstance bonus to its attack roll.
name: Phantom Boar
resistance_markdown: all damage 2 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75) or [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103))

**[Recall Knowledge - Ethereal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 20 **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Phantom Wolf](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2731) **Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 15 **Perception** +7; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +5 **Str** -5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** -3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Walk the Ethereal Line** The phantom walks the thin line between the [Ethereal](/Equipment.aspx?ID=284) and [Material Planes](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) in order to exist on both planes simultaneously. They can shift back to solely the Ethereal Plane by using this ability again. --- **AC** 15 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +9 **Will** +5 **HP** 14 **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision **Resistances** all damage 2 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75) or [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) **Susceptible to Death** Though phantoms aren't alive, neither are they [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160), and they are uniquely vulnerable to the effects of death. A phantom whose Hit Points are reduced to 0 as a result of a [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effect (such as from a spell like [_finger of death_](/Spells.aspx?ID=116)) is immediately whisked away to the River of Souls, where their soul resumes the usual path to the afterlife.
**Vulnerable to Kindness** Any creature within 30 feet that the phantom can see and hear can issue a calm and friendly greeting to it as a two-action activity with the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) traits. A creature greeting the phantom in this manner must attempt a DC 17 [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) check.
**Critical Success** The phantom takes 4d8 mental damage and is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
**Success** The phantom takes 2d8 mental damage.
**Failure** The phantom takes 1d8 mental damage.
**Critical Failure** The phantom is unaffected, and is not affected by this weakness for 1 round. --- **Speed** fly 35 feet **Melee** jaws +7 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 1d6+2 force **Occult Innate Spells** DC 14, attack +6 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132) - **1st** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110) **Pack Attack** The phantom wolf's Strikes deal an additional 1d4 damage to creatures within reach of at least one of its allies.
name: Phantom Wolf
resistance_markdown: all damage 2 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75) or [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103))

**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Zoudou the Zealous](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2734) **Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 66 **Perception** +8; no face **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 30 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +9, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +11, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +9, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +10, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +9, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +8 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 **No Face** Noppera-bos have no sense of vision, but their sense of hearing is [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411). They don't breathe and are immune to effects that require breathing. They gain sustenance by absorbing fluids through their skin—traditionally by immersing their hands or faceless head into the fluid to “drink” through osmosis; they can also benefit from [consumables](/Traits.aspx?ID=36), such as [potions](/Traits.aspx?ID=130) or [elixirs](/Traits.aspx?ID=59), in this way. **Items** [_Fiend's Hunger_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2683) (page 81), tattered ledger, twisted knot of wood ([religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=41) of Kugaptee) --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +11 **Will** +7 **HP** 45 **Immunities** [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=96), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) **Weaknesses** sensitive hearing **Identity Crisis** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** Zoudou successfully Steals the Face of an adjacent creature; **Effect** As Zoudou Steals the creature's Face, she telepathically draws at the creature's sense of identity and self. The creature must attempt a DC 20 Will save. On a failure, the creature questions their own identity and becomes [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round. On a critical failure, they're slowed 1 for 1 minute.
**Oath of Anonymity** When Zoudou has a face, she can't cast divine prepared spells.
**Sensitive Hearing** As long as a noppera-bo is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8), they are [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35). --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _Fiend's Hunger_ +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d4+4 piercing **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 20, attack +12 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=84), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) - **1st** [Bane](/Spells.aspx?ID=18), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) - **2nd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) **Rituals** - **2nd** [Consecrate](/Rituals.aspx?ID=8) **Impersonate Shape** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The noppera-bo changes into a Small or Medium humanoid they've touched during the last 24 hours, and the noppera-bo gains the target creature's face and voice. While transformed and their face is covered, the noppera-bo gains a +2 circumstance bonus to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) that creature. As long as the noppera-bo has a face, it loses its no face ability and its listed immunities. The noppera-bo can change back by using this ability again. **Regain Anonymity** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) **Requirement** Zoudou has a face; **Effect** Zoudou melts the face she's wearing and gains 3d6 temporary Hit Points. She can't Regain Anonymity again for 1d4 rounds. **Silent Casting** When Zoudou is faceless, she can substitute an additional somatic component for a verbal component. **Steal Face** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** The noppera-bo occultist is faceless; **Effect** The noppera-bo occultist targets one creature within 30 feet. The creature must attempt a DC 20 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until the start of its next turn.
**Failure** The noppera-bo gains the target's face. The target's face vanishes until the end of its next turn. Until then, it is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1), can't speak or ingest, and can't smell. The target must also hold its breath or begin to [suffocate](/Rules.aspx?ID=468).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the effect doesn't end automatically. The target can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each such attempt.
name: Zoudou the Zealous

markdown:
Growing up a street urchin in Sze, Ban-niang came to view other urchins as her siblings. She pulled them and herself through to adulthood by working as a racketeer before securing a position as an imperial soldier guard. Her siblings remained in the criminal world but retained loyalty to their sister and acted as informants and financiers to help her climb the ranks of the city watch. Planning ahead for the day when she grew old and frail and simply wanted to enjoy the wealth and power she'd amassed, Ban-niang volunteered to be an inaugural village guard captain in the “backwaters” that was Willowshore when Lung Wa started its lumber operations. Occupying the position for over two decades, Ban-niang retired in 7088 ic but, by that point, had grown fond of the town she'd once dismissed as a backwater and stayed on in her retirement. She has cultivated her image of “Granny Hu,” pretending to let age mellow her ambitions, but in fact, she knows many folks' dark secrets and isn't afraid to use this knowledge to manipulate others. Campaign Use If Granny Hu becomes Willowshore's interim governor, she favors Northridge's businesses and goals. This development makes it easier for the PCs to bolster the town's defenses in the coming months, but at the cost of being able to efficiently prepare food stores for the coming winter. Once the town of Willowshore is safe at the start of Chapter 3, the PCs can attempt to interact with Granny Hu using the [Influence](/Rules.aspx?ID=1201) subsystem. An influence encounter with Granny Hu is a 3rd-level challenge, and an influence round with Granny Hu takes 1 hour. If any attempt to Influence Granny Hu results in a critical failure and the PCs have fewer than 5 Influence Points with her, she uses her So Have You Heard? ability against that PC soon thereafter. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Ban-Niang "Granny" Hu](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2735) **Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 90 **Perception** +11 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Halfling](/Languages.aspx?ID=7) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +11, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +13, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +13, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +11, [Willowshore Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +11 **Str** +2 **Dex** +1 **Con** +1 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 **So Have You Heard?** ([exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=66)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Granny Hu can shift a PC's reputation in Willowshore over the course of 2 hours of work. She attempts a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check against the target's Deception, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6), or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) DC, whichever is highest. If the target is admired or revered by at least one faction in Willowshore, Granny Hu takes a –1 status penalty to her check, or –2 if the target is admired or revered by both factions. The target is then temporarily immune to So Have You Heard? for 1 week.
**Critical Success** The target takes a –2 circumstance penalty to Charisma-based skill checks when interacting with Willowshore residents for a week.
**Success** As critical success, but only a –1 circumstance penalty and for only 1 day.
**Failure** The target is unaffected.
**Critical Failure** The plan backfires; not only is the target unaffected, but Granny Hu can't use So Have You Heard? for 1 month. **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31)_, smoking pipe worth 20 gp, [_spider lily tattoo_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2687) --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +10 **Will** +13 +1 status vs. [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), nerves of steel **HP** 75 **Nerves of Steel** If Granny Hu succeeds at a save against a [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effect, she gets a critical success instead.
**Are You Sure About That?** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet who's visible to Granny Hu and who has been exposed to Want to Try Me? in the past week says or does something that would be against Granny Hu's interests; **Effect** Granny Hu makes a subtle gesture, reminding the triggering creature of their secret. The creature must succeed at a DC 22 Will Save or the triggering action is disrupted. Regardless of the outcome, the creature then becomes temporarily immune to this ability for 24 hours.
**Want to Try Me?** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet attempts to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=52) or [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) a creature; **Effect** Granny Hu threatens to expose a secret of the triggering creature. She attempts an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) check against the triggering creature's Will DC. Her reputation means she can threaten a PC even if she doesn't actually know a secret, but if she does know an especially dangerous or shameful one, she gains a +2 circumstance bonus to her Intimidation check.
**Critical Success** The creature is [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), and the result of their Coerce or Demoralize attempt is reduced by one degree of success.
**Success** As success, but frightened 1.
**Failure** The creature is unaffected.
**Critical Failure** The creature is unaffected, gains a +1 circumstance bonus to their Coerce or Demoralize attempt, and is immune to Want to Try Me? for 24 hours. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _longsword_ +13 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d8+3 slashing
name: Ban-Niang "Granny" Hu

markdown:
At the tender age of 10, Shou Matsuki was already part of the family carpentry business and participating in the construction of the monastery. A few weeks short of being 99 years old this year, Shou is the only human left in Willowshore who has experienced the village's entire history in person. He fondly remembers the vibrant days when pilgrims filled the streets, speaks highly of his fellow migrant workers in the decade when they sought work outside the village, and grumbles about Lung Wa's takeover. Outwardly, Old Matsuki is a belligerent elderly man who prefers alcohol over tea, thinks the Northridge faction is always up to no good, and laments at the brothel's transformation into an inn. In private, everyone knows he has a soft spot for the future of Willowshore's culture, traditions, and people. During the monster invasion, his mind remains sharp and focused as he organizes Southbank to fortify their position, buying precious time for the surviving villagers to figure out how they might take Willowshore back. Campaign Use If Shou Matsuki becomes Willowshore's interim governor, he favors Southbank's farmers and traditions. This development makes it easier for the PCs to prepare the town's food stores for the coming winter, but at the cost of not having a head start at shoring up the town's defenses in the coming months. Once the town of Willowshore is safe at the start of Chapter 3, the PCs can attempt to interact with Old Matsuki using the [Influence](/Rules.aspx?ID=1201) subsystem. An influence encounter with Old Matsuki is a 3rd-level challenge, and an influence round with him takes 1 hour. If any attempt to Influence him results in a critical failure and the PCs have fewer than 5 Influence Points with him, he uses his Respect your Elders! ability against that PC soon thereafter. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Shou "Old" Matsuki](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2736) **Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 92 **Perception** +12; cataracts, wood-grain speaker **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Hwan](/Languages.aspx?ID=98), [Minkaian](/Languages.aspx?ID=121), [Tengu](/Languages.aspx?ID=47) **Skills** [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +13, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +12, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +12, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +11, [Willowshore Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +13 **Str** +2 **Dex** -1 **Con** +1 **Int** +4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 **Cataracts** Old Matsuki's deteriorating eyesight imposes a –2 circumstance penalty to vision-based Perception checks.
**Wood-Grain Speaker** Old Matsuki gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception when examining wood items, structures, and trees. He can use [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24) for items primarily made of wood. This ability isn't affected by Old Matsuki's cataracts.
**You Know, They Say...** Old Matsuki can use [Willowshore Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) in place of [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) to attempt [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) checks with a –2 penalty. He has the effects of the [Dubious Knowledge](/Feats.aspx?ID=776) feat for [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) checks attempted in this way. **Items** calabash bottle worth 20 gp, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12)_, [sterling carpenter's artisan tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3), --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +8 **Will** +12 **HP** 70 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _staff_ +11 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=198)), **Damage** 2d4+4 bludgeoning **A Drink for Good Luck** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Frequency** once per season; **Requirements** Old Matsuki has a drink in hand; **Effect** Old Matsuki toasts to good fortune, then selects up to six other creatures within 30 feet before he drinks his drink. He, and all creatures that drink before the end of their next turn, become infused with good luck. Once before the end of the current season, each target can reroll a failed saving throw as a free action, taking the second result. **Respect Your Elders!** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Old Matsuki publicly chastises a character he can see and who's within 30 feet for being disrespectful, not just against him or Willowshore's other elders, but against Willowshore's traditions. Whether or not his reprimands are justified, the target of this tirade must attempt a DC 22 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and immune to Respect Your Elders! for 1 week.
**Success** The creature feels a momentary bout of shame, becoming [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round.
**Failure** Feelings of shame and doubt nag at the creature, distracting them enough that they become stupefied 1 for 1 hour. **Wait, Give Me a Moment...** **Trigger** Old Matsuki fails a check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26); **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Old Matsuki realizes his error, then rerolls the triggering check with a +2 circumstance bonus.
name: Shou "Old" Matsuki

Once a creature attempts this save, it's temporarily immune to further Sickening Displays for 1 minute. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.

**Failure** The creature is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), and is flat-footed for as long as it's sickened.
**Critical Failure** The creature is sickened 2 and is flat-footed for as long as it's sickened. **Rust Creep** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) A creature bitten by the Vlorian cythnigot becomes afflicted by [rust creep](/Diseases.aspx?ID=25), but with a DC 20 Fortitude save.

**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Clockwork Serpent Spy](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2744) **Source** [Rusthenge](/Sources.aspx?ID=215) pg. 44 **Perception** +10; [low-light vision](/Traits.aspx?ID=100) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7 **Str** +0 **Dex** +4 **Con** +0 **Int** -5 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Record Audio** The clockwork spy records all sounds within 25 feet onto a small gemstone worth 1 gp embedded in its body. The clockwork spy can record up to 1 hour of sound on a single gemstone.
Once it begins recording, it can't cease recording early, nor can it record onto a gemstone that already contains a recording. Some clockwork spies contain multiple gemstones to allow for a series of recordings. Since clockwork spies are not intelligent, they must be given simple commands regarding when to start recording sounds. A clockwork spy can differentiate between different kinds of creatures but not between specific individuals.
The spy can start or stop playback of recorded sound by spending a single action. Removing a gemstone from or installing a gemstone into a clockwork spy requires a successful DC 14 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=69); on a failure, the gemstone is undamaged, but any recorded sounds are erased and the gemstone still can't be used to make another recording.
**Wind Up** 24 hours, DC 17, standby --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +3 **Ref** +9 **Will** +6 **HP** 15 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Weaknesses** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 3, [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=378) 3 **Self-Destruct** A clockwork spy must use this reaction unless specifically programmed otherwise by its creator; **Trigger** The clockwork spy is reduced to 0 Hit Points; **Effect** The spy thrashes around and emits a tinny scream followed by a steady ticking sound. At the beginning of what would have been its next turn, the clockwork spy explodes, dealing 1d12 piercing damage in a 5-foot radius (DC 17 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Its gemstone is destroyed, along with any information contained inside it. An adjacent creature can cancel the self-destruct sequence by succeeding at a DC 17 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=69). --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** sharpened gear +7 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 1d6–1 slashing plus bleeding gash **Bleeding Gash** On a critical hit with a sharpened gear Strike, the clockwork serpent spy also inflicts 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) (DC 17 Fortitude save negates the additional bleed damage).
name: Clockwork Serpent Spy

**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Clockwork Belimarius](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2745) **Source** [Rusthenge](/Sources.aspx?ID=215) pg. 47 **Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +12 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** -5 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -5 **[Wind-Up](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=575)** 24 hours, DC 21, standby **Items** clockwork wand, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [halberd](/Weapons.aspx?ID=26)_ --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +12 **Will** +8 **HP** 45 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 5 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374), bludgeoning, or orichalcum) **Weaknesses** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 5, [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=378) 5 **Clockwork Wand** The clockwork mage uses a mechanical wand as a focus to channel magical energy. This wand is built into the clockwork mage's chest, with only the crystal at the end exposed. The mage can Interact to the remove the wand, or someone else can remove it with a DC 25 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=69). The clockwork mage becomes unable to cast any spells except cantrips while the wand is removed.
When removed, the clockwork wand is a magic wand containing the last 1st-level innate spell the clockwork mage cast ([_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), if the clockwork Belimarius hasn't yet cast a 1st-level spell in this adventure). The spells are placed within the wand while the mage is created, and the creator can substitute other arcane spells of the appropriate level. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _halberd_ +16 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d10+4 piercing plus dispelling critical **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 21, attack +13 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280), [Tanglefoot](/Spells.aspx?ID=330) - **1st** [Color Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=44), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Mage Armor](/Spells.aspx?ID=176) - **2nd** [Magnetic Repulsion](/Spells.aspx?ID=945), [Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=256) **Dispelling Critical** On a critical hit with a halberd Strike, shards of [noqual](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1419) built into the clockwork Belimarius glow green for a moment. Instead of inflicting additional damage with the critical hit, the clockwork Belimarius instead attempts a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check with a +18 bonus against one magical effect active on the target.
name: Clockwork Belimarius

**Tongue Grab**   If the boggard scout hits a creature with its tongue, that creature becomes [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the boggard. A typical limit is 120 feet. Some ghosts are instead bound to a room, building, item, or creature that was special to it rather than a location.
 --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +15 **Will** +17 **HP** 82 (negative healing, rejuvenation) **Immunities** death effects, disease, paralyzed, poison, unconscious **Resistances** all damage 5 ([except force](/Traits.aspx?ID=666), [ghost touch](/Traits.aspx?ID=217), [or positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128)) double resistance vs. non-magical) **Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Returning her sister's holy prayer beads and revealing her fate allows Yuni to move on (although she'll return as a ghost next year if the PCs don't break the Willowshore mindscape before then). --- **Speed** fly 25 feet **Melee** ghostly hand +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d8+8 negative **Frightful Moan** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The ghost laments its fate, forcing each living creature within 30 feet to attempt a DC 25 Will save. On a failure, a creature becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2 (or frightened 3 on a critical failure). On a success, a creature is temporarily immune to this ghost's frightful moan for 1 minute. **Tiger Attack** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33)) Yuni cries out in pain as a ghostly tiger appears to rip and tear at her body, only for additional ghostly tigers to appear and do the same to all creatures in a 30-foot emanation. Creatures in this area take 4d6 slashing damage (DC 25 basic Reflex save).

markdown:
A bakeneko comes into existence when the life of a cat is extended past its normal lifespan, whether through incredible fortune, magic, or other means. With each passing year, the feline becomes more and more monstrous—growing in size, becoming more intelligent, and gaining an affinity for magic. Eventually, they become able to walk upright on their rear legs, pursue new hobbies, and learn languages. Despite this expanded intellect, many bakenekos prefer to enjoy the first decades of their ascension continuing their lives as usual as a (particularly oversized) house cat. For some, they do so because they want to acquire knowledge of the world that they couldn't comprehend as cats, but for others, it's just a desire to live out their lives with their current families and loved ones before moving on to what comes next. Bakenekos enjoy dancing above all other pleasures and can't resist nodding their heads, tapping their paws, or swishing their tails in time to music. They often animate corpses simply to watch them dance—a pastime that often spells trouble for nearby settlements should the undead get free from the bakeneko's control. Bakenekos typically have a playful and mischievous demeanor, and while they aren't inherently evil creatures, they're still feared for their ability to animate corpses and command fire. Villagers are often frightened half to death by the sight of a flaming cat strolling alongside a walking corpse, and bakenekos' association with evil and the occult has caused some villages in Tian Xia to vilify all cats. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Bakeneko](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2753) **Source** [Pathfinder #197: Let the Leaves Fall](/Sources.aspx?ID=222) pg. 82 **Perception** +9; darkvision, scent (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +10, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +8, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +10, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +10 **Str** +3 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +7 **Will** +4 **HP** 35 **Resistances** fire 5 **Weaknesses** cold iron 5 **Cat at Heart** When a bakeneko is killed, they immediately reincarnate into a normal house cat who can never again become a bakeneko.
**Feel the Beat** Bakenekos love to dance. If a bakeneko begins their turn within 30 feet of a source of music, they become slowed 1 until the start of their next turn as they follow their compulsion to move to the rhythm of the tune by swishing their tail in time, stomping from one hind leg to the other, or clapping their front paws.
**Flammable** Thanks to their high-fat diets, bakenekos secrete flammable oils from their skin that coats their fur. If a bakeneko takes any fire damage, their entire body lights up in flame, turning them into a furry fireball until the end of their next turn. While burning in this way, a bakeneko's Strikes inflict an additional 1d6 fire damage. --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** claw +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 1d8+5 slashing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 20 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177) - **2nd** [Animate Dead](/Spells.aspx?ID=666) (zombie only) **Rituals** - **2nd** [Create Undead](/Rituals.aspx?ID=10) (zombie only) **Skilled Ritualist** Bakenekos can only create zombies with _create undead_ but can perform the ritual without secondary casters and lower the casting time to 4 hours.
Nekomatas Bakenekos mostly see their animated but mindless zombies as amusements, though they also use them as guardians or even as agents for revenge. Still, they do their best to ensure that undead they animate aren't left to wander free. Most tales of “evil bakenekos” in fact refer to a similar and much more dangerous creature, the nekomata—a level 6 Medium-sized variant of the bakeneko whose bite causes a debilitating disease of the mind.
name: Bakeneko

markdown:
A dalgyal gwishin is a faceless, undead spirit that wears a stone mask. This forlorn undead forms from the soul of a mortal who died a lonely death with no one to remember them, and is similarly unable to remember what it looked like in life. A dalgyal gwishin wanders the land of the living in search of any trace of who they were when they were alive. Full of anger, they remain envious of well-loved or famous individuals who are surrounded by close friends and admirers—the type of people who would never share the dalgyal gwishin's fate. While a dalgyal gwishin has little more than a ghostly body made of mist, the featureless stone mask it wears on its head is solid. Even the stolen faces it claims are transitory, cracking off after a day to form what looks akin to a fragile porcelain death mask. Some of these lonely spirits keep these masks and add them to a collection in a remote and forlorn lair, hoping to find companionship in an ever-growing display of silent faces. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Dalgyal Gwishin](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2754) **Source** [Pathfinder #197: Let the Leaves Fall](/Sources.aspx?ID=222) pg. 83 **Perception** +12; lifesense (precise) 120 feet, no vision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); can't speak any language, telepathy 120 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13 **Str** -5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 **Lifesense** The dalgyal gwishin senses the vital essence of living and undead creatures within the listed range. --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +15 **Will** +12 **HP** 58 (negative healing) **Immunities** death effects, disease, paralyzed, poison, precision, unconscious **Resistances** all 5 ([except force](/Traits.aspx?ID=666), [ghost touch](/Traits.aspx?ID=217), [or positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-magical) [Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17) ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 30 feet, DC 19
**Stone-Faced** While the dalgyal gwishin has no physical body, its stone face is solid. It can't pass through solid objects. --- **Speed** fly 40 feet **Melee** head +12 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d6+2 bludgeoning plus 2d6 mental **Jealous Frenzy** ([[divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [[enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61)](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [[mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Trigger** The dalgyal gwishin Strikes a creature that has a higher Charisma modifier than itself; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The dalgyal gwishin enters an envious frenzy as it senses strong ties of friendship and personality within the creature it struck. The dalgyal gwishin becomes [quickened ](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89)1 but can only use its quickened action to Strike the triggering creature. If the dalgyal gwishin fails to Strike the triggering creature at least once during its turn, Jealous Frenzy ends. It also ends after 1 minute. After Jealous Frenzy ends, the dalgyal gwishin is [slowed ](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92)1 for 1 minute. **Peel Face** ([[divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [[necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Trigger** The dalgyal gwishin critically hits a creature or reduces a creature to 0 Hit Points while in a Jealous Frenzy; **Effect** The dalgyal gwishin presses its stony face to the creature's face, causing a ghostly imprint of that face to peel away and duplicate onto its head. The triggering creature must succeed at a DC 22 Will save or become [stupefied ](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94)1 (stupefied 2 on a critical failure). The dalgyal gwishin's Jealous Frenzy ends, and it doesn't become slowed.
Faceless Horror The dalgyal gwishin's most significant physical trait—the lack of a face—is shared with the living aberrations known as noppera-bos. Despite this shared feature and the ability to steal faces, the two creatures don't cooperate and are more often bitter enemies. Anyone slain by a dalgyal gwishin leaves behind an unnerving faceless corpse
name: Dalgyal Gwishin
